```javascript
      totalWords: structuredChapters.reduce((sum, ch) => 
        sum + ch.scenes.reduce((sSum, sc) => sSum + sc.wordCount, 0), 0),
    }
  };
}

function chunkByWordCount(text, maxWords) {
  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\n+/);
  const chunks = [];
  let currentChunk = '';
  let currentCount = 0;

  for (const para of paragraphs) {
    const paraWords = estimateWordCount(para);
    if (currentCount + paraWords > maxWords && currentChunk.length > 0) {
      chunks.push(currentChunk);
      currentChunk = para;
      currentCount = paraWords;
    } else {
      currentChunk += (currentChunk ? '\n\n' : '') + para;
      currentCount += paraWords;
    }
  }
  if (currentChunk.length > 0) chunks.push(currentChunk);
  return chunks;
}

export async function importManuscriptToProject(parsedData, documentManager) {
  // Create project
  const projectId = await documentManager.createProject({
    name: parsedData.title,
    metadata: {
      importedAt: Date.now(),
      source: 'manuscript-import',
      originalStats: parsedData.stats
    }
  });

  // Create chapters and scenes
  for (const chapter of parsedData.chapters) {
    const chapterId = await documentManager.createChapter({
      projectId,
      title: chapter.title,
      order: chapter.order
    });

    for (const scene of chapter.scenes) {
      await documentManager.createScene({
        chapterId,
        title: scene.title,
        content: scene.content,
        order: scene.order,
        status: scene.status,
        notes: '',
        tags: ['imported'],
        wordCount: scene.wordCount
      });
    }
  }

  return projectId;
}
```

### Asset Import Service — Full Implementation

```javascript
// src/services/importAssets.js
import { splitByH1, splitByH2, extractMetadataLines } from './markdownParser.js';

// --- Detection ---

export function detectAssetType(markdownText) {
  const lower = markdownText.toLowerCase();
  const scores = {
    character: 0,
    worldRule: 0,
    beatMap: 0,
    sceneInventory: 0,
    manuscript: 0
  };

  // Character signals
  if (lower.includes('motivation')) scores.character += 2;
  if (lower.includes('backstory')) scores.character += 2;
  if (lower.includes('role')) scores.character += 1;
  if (lower.includes('relationship')) scores.character += 2;
  if (lower.includes('personality')) scores.character += 2;
  if (lower.includes('appearance') || lower.includes('physical')) scores.character += 1;
  if (lower.includes('arc')) scores.character += 1;
  if (lower.includes('dossier')) scores.character += 3;

  // World rule signals
  if (lower.includes('magic system')) scores.worldRule += 3;
  if (lower.includes('geography')) scores.worldRule += 2;
  if (lower.includes('constraint')) scores.worldRule += 2;
  if (lower.includes('lore')) scores.worldRule += 2;
  if (lower.includes('world') && lower.includes('rule')) scores.worldRule += 3;
  if (lower.includes('politics') || lower.includes('economy')) scores.worldRule += 1;
  if (lower.includes('technology') || lower.includes('religion')) scores.worldRule += 1;
  if (lower.includes('history of')) scores.worldRule += 1;

  // Beat map signals
  if (lower.includes('act 1') || lower.includes('act i')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('act 2') || lower.includes('act ii')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('act 3') || lower.includes('act iii')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('beat')) scores.beatMap += 2;
  if (lower.includes('inciting incident')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('climax')) scores.beatMap += 2;
  if (lower.includes('midpoint')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('pinch point')) scores.beatMap += 3;
  if (lower.includes('turning point')) scores.beatMap += 2;

  // Scene inventory signals
  if (lower.includes('pov:') || lower.includes('pov :')) scores.sceneInventory += 3;
  if (lower.includes('location:')) scores.sceneInventory += 2;
  if (lower.includes('timeline:')) scores.sceneInventory += 2;
  if (lower.includes('purpose:')) scores.sceneInventory += 2;
  if (lower.includes('scene inventory') || lower.includes('scene list')) scores.sceneInventory += 3;

  // Manuscript signals
  const chapterMatches = (lower.match(/^#+\s*chapter/gm) || []).length;
  if (chapterMatches >= 2) scores.manuscript += 5;
  const sceneBreaks = (lower.match(/\n---\n|\n\*\*\*\n/g) || []).length;
  if (sceneBreaks >= 3) scores.manuscript += 3;
  const wordCount = lower.split(/\s+/).length;
  if (wordCount > 5000) scores.manuscript += 2;
  if (wordCount > 20000) scores.manuscript += 3;

  // Return highest scoring type
  const sorted = Object.entries(scores).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1

---

```javascript
  // Return highest scoring type
  const sorted = Object.entries(scores).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
  
  // If top score is 0 or tied with too many, return unknown
  if (sorted[0][1] === 0) return { type: 'unknown', confidence: 0, scores };
  
  const confidence = sorted[0][1] / (sorted[0][1] + sorted[1][1] + 1);
  
  return {
    type: sorted[0][0],
    confidence, // 0-1, higher = more certain
    scores
  };
}

// --- Character Parsing ---

export function parseCharacterDossier(markdownText) {
  const h1Sections = splitByH1(markdownText);
  
  // Could be one character per file or multiple characters in one file
  return h1Sections.map(section => {
    const h2s = splitByH2(section.content);
    const fields = {};
    
    for (const sub of h2s) {
      const key = sub.heading.toLowerCase().trim();
      fields[key] = sub.content.trim();
    }
    
    // Also try extracting metadata-style lines from the whole content
    const meta = extractMetadataLines(section.content);
    
    return {
      name: section.heading,
      role: fields['role'] || meta['role'] || '',
      description: fields['description'] || fields['appearance'] || fields['physical description'] || '',
      personality: fields['personality'] || fields['traits'] || '',
      motivation: fields['motivation'] || fields['goal'] || fields['want'] || '',
      backstory: fields['backstory'] || fields['background'] || fields['history'] || '',
      arc: fields['arc'] || fields['character arc'] || '',
      relationships: parseRelationshipsList(fields['relationships'] || fields['connections'] || ''),
      notes: fields['notes'] || fields['additional notes'] || '',
      raw: section.content // Keep raw for reference
    };
  });
}

function parseRelationshipsList(text) {
  if (!text) return [];
  const lines = text.split('\n').filter(l => l.trim().startsWith('-') || l.trim().startsWith('*'));
  return lines.map(line => {
    const match = line.match(/[-*]\s*\*\*(.+?)\*\*[:\s]*(.+)/);
    if (match) return { character: match[1].trim(), relationship: match[2].trim() };
    // Fallback: just clean the line
    return { character: line.replace(/^[-*]\s*/, '').trim(), relationship: '' };
  });
}

// --- World Rule Parsing ---

export function parseWorldRules(markdownText) {
  const h1Sections = splitByH1(markdownText);
  
  return h1Sections.map(section => {
    const h2s = splitByH2(section.content);
    const fields = {};
    
    for (const sub of h2s) {
      fields[sub.heading.toLowerCase().trim()] = sub.content.trim();
    }
    
    return {
      title: section.heading,
      category: fields['category'] || fields['type'] || 'General',
      description: fields['description'] || fields['overview'] || fields['details'] || section.content.trim(),
      constraints: fields['constraints'] || fields['limitations'] || fields['rules'] || '',
      related: fields['related'] || fields['connections'] || fields['see also'] || '',
      raw: section.content
    };
  });
}

// --- Beat Map Parsing ---

export function parseBeatMap(markdownText) {
  const h1Sections = splitByH1(markdownText);
  
  // Each H1 is an Act, each H2 is a Beat
  return h1Sections.map(act => {
    const beats = splitByH2(act.content);
    return {
      title: act.heading,
      beats: beats.map((beat, idx) => ({
        title: beat.heading,
        order: idx,
        description: beat.content.trim(),
        // Try to extract metadata from beat content
        ...extractBeatMetadata(beat.content)
      }))
    };
  });
}

function extractBeatMetadata(text) {
  const meta = extractMetadataLines(text);
  return {
    characters: meta['characters'] || meta['who'] || '',
    location: meta['location'] || meta['where'] || '',
    tension: meta['tension'] || meta['stakes'] || '',
    purpose: meta['purpose'] || meta['function'] || ''
  };
}

// --- Scene Inventory Parsing ---

export function parseSceneInventory(markdownText) {
  const h1Sections = splitByH1(markdownText);
  
  return h1Sections.map(section => {
    const meta = extractMetadataLines(section.content);
    const h2s = splitByH2(section.content);
    const fields = {};
    for (const sub of h2s) {
      fields[sub.heading.toLowerCase().trim()] = sub.content.trim();
    }
    
    return {
      title: section.heading,
      chapter: meta['chapter'] || '',
      pov: meta['pov'] || meta['point of view'] || '',
      location: meta['location'] || meta['setting'] || '',
      timeline: meta['timeline'] || meta['time'] || meta['when'] || '',
      purpose: meta['purpose'] || meta['goal'] || '',
      status: meta['status'] || 'draft',
      summary: fields['summary'] || fields['description'] || '',
      notes: fields['notes'] || '',
      characters: meta['characters'] || '',
      conflict: meta['conflict'] || fields['conflict'] || '',
      raw: section.content
    };
  });
}
```

### Mapping Imported Assets to App Domains

Now the critical part — how do these parsed structures map to the existing Preproduction system in the app. Looking at the existing codebase, the app has a document management system with IndexedDB. The Preproduction phase likely has domains like Characters, World Rules, Beat Maps, and Scene Inventory that feed into the generation pipeline.

```javascript
// src/services/importAssets.js (continued)

export async function importAssetsToProject(parsedFiles, projectId, documentManager) {
  const report = {
    imported: [],
    skipped: [],
    conflicts: [],
    errors: []
  };

  for (const pf of parsedFiles) {
    try {
      switch (pf.confirmedType) {
        case 'character': {
          const characters = parseCharacterDossier(pf.text);
          for (const char of characters) {
            // Check for existing character with same name
            const existing = await documentManager.findCharacterByName(projectId, char.name);
            if (existing) {
              report.conflicts.push({
                type: 'character',
                name: char.name,
                existing,
                incoming: char,
                resolution: null // user decides later
              });
            } else {
              const id = await documentManager.saveDocument({
                projectId,
                type: 'character',
                title: char.name,
                content: JSON.stringify(char),
                domain: 'preproduction',
                subdomain: 'characters',
                tags: ['imported', char.role].filter(Boolean),
                createdAt: Date.now(),
                updatedAt: Date.now()
              });
              report.imported.push({ type: 'character', name: char.name, id });
            }
          }
          break;
        }

        case 'worldRule': {
          const rules = parseWorldRules(pf.text);
          for (const rule of rules) {
            const id = await documentManager.saveDocument({
              projectId,
              type: 'worldRule',
              title: rule.title,
              content: JSON.stringify(rule),
              domain: 'preproduction',
              subdomain: 'worldRules',
              category: rule.category,
              tags: ['imported', rule.category].filter(Boolean),
              createdAt: Date.now(),
              updatedAt: Date.now()
            });
            report.imported.push({ type: 'worldRule', name: rule.title, id });
          }
          break;
        }

        case 'beatMap': {
          const acts = parseBeatMap(pf.text);
          // Beat map is typically one per project — store as project-level metadata
          const id = await documentManager.saveDocument({
            projectId,
            type: 'beatMap',
            title: 'Imported Beat Map',
            content: JSON.stringify(acts),
            domain: 'preproduction',
            subdomain: 'beatMap',
            tags: ['imported'],
            createdAt: Date.now(),
            updatedAt: Date.now()
          });
          report.imported.push({ type: 'beatMap', name: 'Beat Map', id });
          break;
        }

        case 'sceneInventory': {
          const scenes = parseSceneInventory(pf.text);
          for (const scene of scenes) {
            const id = await documentManager.saveDocument({
              projectId,
              type: 'sceneInventory',
              title: scene.title,
              content: JSON.stringify(scene),
              domain: 'preproduction',
              subdomain: 'sceneInventory',
              tags: ['imported', scene.status].filter(Boolean),
              createdAt: Date.now(),
              updatedAt: Date.now()
            });
            report.imported.push({ type: 'sceneInventory', name: scene.title, id });
          }
          break;
        }

        default:
          report.skipped.push({ file: pf.file.name, reason: 'Unknown type' });
      }
    } catch (err) {
      report.errors.push({ file: pf.file.name, error: err.message });
    }
  }

  return report;
}
```

### Conflict Resolution UI

When importing characters or other assets that might already exist:

```jsx
function ConflictResolver({ conflicts, onResolve }) {
  return (
    <div className="conflict-resolver">
      {conflicts.map((conflict, idx) => (
        <div key={idx} className="conflict-item">
          <h4>⚠️ {conflict.type}: "{conflict.name}" already exists</h4>
          <div className="conflict-comparison">
            <div className="existing">
              <h5>Existing</h5>
              <pre>{JSON.stringify(conflict.existing, null, 2)}</pre>
            </div>
            <div className="incoming">
              <h5>Importing</h5>
              <pre>{JSON.stringify(conflict.incoming, null, 2)}</pre>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div className="conflict-actions">
            <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'skip')}>Skip (keep existing)</button>
            <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'replace')}>Replace with imported</button>
            <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'merge')}>Merge (combine fields)</button>
            <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'duplicate')}>Import as duplicate</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}
```

```javascript
// src/services/manuscriptAnalysis.js

export async function analyzeImportedManuscript(projectId, documentManager, llmService) {
  const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
  const allScenes = [];
  
  for (const chapter of chapters) {
    const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(chapter.id);
    allScenes.push(...scenes.map(s => ({ ...s, chapterTitle: chapter.title })));
  }

  const fullText = allScenes.map(s => s.content).join('\n\n---\n\n');
  
  const analysis = {
    projectId,
    timestamp: Date.now(),
    stages: {}
  };

  // Stage 1: Extract Characters automatically
  analysis.stages.characters = await extractCharactersFromText(fullText, llmService);
  
  // Stage 2: Extract World Rules / Setting details
  analysis.stages.worldRules = await extractWorldRulesFromText(fullText, llmService);
  
  // Stage 3: Derive Beat Map from existing structure
  analysis.stages.beatMap = await deriveBeatMapFromChapters(chapters, allScenes, llmService);
  
  // Stage 4: Build Scene Inventory with metadata
  analysis.stages.sceneInventory = await buildSceneInventory(chapters, allScenes, llmService);
  
  // Stage 5: Continuity check
  analysis.stages.continuity = await checkContinuity(allScenes, analysis.stages.characters, llmService);

  return analysis;
}

async function extractCharactersFromText(fullText, llmService) {
  // For very long texts, we need to chunk and aggregate
  const chunks = chunkText(fullText, 8000); // ~8k tokens per chunk
  
  const allCharacters = new Map();
  
  for (const chunk of chunks) {
    const prompt = `Analyze this fiction excerpt and extract all named characters. For each character, provide:
- name
- role (protagonist/antagonist/supporting/minor)
- physical description (if mentioned)
- personality traits (if evident)
- relationships to other characters (if shown)
- motivations (if revealed)

Return as JSON array.

Text:
---
${chunk}
---

Return ONLY valid JSON: [{"name": "...", "role": "...", "description": "...", "personality": "...", "relationships": [...], "motivation": "..."}]`;

    const response = await llmService.generate(prompt);
    try {
      const chars = JSON.parse(response);
      for (const char of chars) {
        if (allCharacters.has(char.name)) {
          // Merge with existing entry
          const existing = allCharacters.get(char.name);
          mergeCharacterData(existing, char);
        } else {
          allCharacters.set(char.name, char);
        }
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to parse character extraction response:', e);
    }
  }
  
  return Array.from(allCharacters.values());
}

function mergeCharacterData(existing, incoming) {
  // Append new description details
  if (incoming.description && !existing.description.includes(incoming.description)) {
    existing.description = (existing.description + ' ' + incoming.description).trim();
  }
  if (incoming.personality && !existing.personality.includes(incoming.personality)) {
    existing.personality = (existing.personality + ' ' + incoming.personality).trim();
  }
  if (incoming.motivation && !existing.motivation.includes(incoming.motivation)) {
    existing.motivation = (existing.motivation + ' ' + incoming.motivation).trim();
  }
  // Merge relationships
  if (incoming.relationships) {
    const existingNames = new Set(existing.relationships.map(r => r.character || r.name));
    for (const rel of incoming.relationships) {
      if (!existingNames.has(rel.character || rel.name)) {
        existing.relationships.push(rel);
      }
    }
  }
  // Upgrade role if more specific
  if (existing.role === 'minor' && incoming.role !== 'minor') {
    existing.role = incoming.role;
  }
}

async function extractWorldRulesFromText(fullText, llmService) {
  const chunks = chunkText(fullText, 8000);
  const rules = [];
  
  for (const chunk of chunks) {
    const prompt = `Analyze this fiction excerpt and extract world-building elements:
- Magic systems or supernatural rules
- Geography and locations
- Political structures
- Technology level
- Cultural customs
- Historical events referenced
- Any established "rules" of the fictional world

Return as JSON array.

Text:
---
${chunk}
---

Return ONLY valid JSON: [{"title": "...", "category": "...", "description": "...", "constraints": "..."}]`;

    const response = await llmService.generate(prompt);
    try {
      const extracted = JSON.parse(response);
      rules.push(...extracted);
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to parse world rules extraction:', e);
    }
  }
  
  // Deduplicate by title similarity
  return deduplicateByTitle(rules);
}

function deduplicateByTitle(items) {
  const seen = new Map();
  for (const item of items) {
    const normalized = item.title.toLowerCase().trim();
    if (seen.has(normalized)) {
      // Merge descriptions
      const existing = seen.get(normalized);
      if (item.description && !existing.description.includes(item.description)) {
        existing.description += ' ' + item.description;
      }
    } else {
      seen.set(normalized, { ...item });
    }
  }
  return Array.from(seen.values());
}

async function deriveBeatMapFromChapters(chapters, scenes, llmService) {
  // Build a summary of each chapter for the

```javascript
async function deriveBeatMapFromChapters(chapters, scenes, llmService) {
  // Build a summary of each chapter for the LLM to analyze structure
  const chapterSummaries = chapters.map(ch => {
    const chScenes = scenes.filter(s => s.chapterTitle === ch.title);
    const wordCount = chScenes.reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.wordCount || 0), 0);
    const firstWords = chScenes.map(s => s.content.substring(0, 200)).join(' ... ');
    return {
      title: ch.title,
      order: ch.order,
      sceneCount: chScenes.length,
      wordCount,
      preview: firstWords
    };
  });

  const prompt = `You are a story structure analyst. Given these chapter summaries from a novel, identify the major story beats and map them to a three-act structure.

Chapters:
${chapterSummaries.map(ch => `${ch.order + 1}. "${ch.title}" (${ch.sceneCount} scenes, ${ch.wordCount} words)\nPreview: ${ch.preview}`).join('\n\n')}

Identify:
- Act boundaries (where Act 1/2/3 begin)
- Key beats: Hook, Inciting Incident, First Plot Point, First Pinch Point, Midpoint, Second Pinch Point, Second Plot Point, Climax, Resolution
- For each beat, which chapter(s) it spans

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "acts": [
    {
      "title": "Act 1 - Setup",
      "startChapter": 1,
      "endChapter": 4,
      "beats": [
        {"title": "Hook", "chapter": 1, "description": "..."},
        {"title": "Inciting Incident", "chapter": 2, "description": "..."}
      ]
    }
  ]
}`;

  const response = await llmService.generate(prompt);
  try {
    return JSON.parse(response);
  } catch (e) {
    console.warn('Failed to parse beat map derivation:', e);
    return { acts: [], error: 'Failed to derive beat map' };
  }
}

async function buildSceneInventory(chapters, scenes, llmService) {
  const inventory = [];
  
  // Process in batches to avoid overwhelming the LLM
  for (const scene of scenes) {
    const truncated = scene.content.substring(0, 3000);
    
    const prompt = `Analyze this scene from a novel and extract metadata:

Chapter: "${scene.chapterTitle}"
Scene text:
---
${truncated}
---

Extract:
- POV character (whose perspective)
- Location/setting
- Time of day / timeline position
- Characters present
- Core conflict or tension
- Scene purpose (setup/confrontation/resolution/transition/revelation)
- Emotional tone
- One-sentence summary

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "pov": "...",
  "location": "...",
  "timeline": "...",
  "characters": ["..."],
  "conflict": "...",
  "purpose": "...",
  "tone": "...",
  "summary": "..."
}`;

    try {
      const response = await llmService.generate(prompt);
      const metadata = JSON.parse(response);
      inventory.push({
        sceneId: scene.id,
        chapterTitle: scene.chapterTitle,
        sceneTitle: scene.title,
        wordCount: scene.wordCount,
        ...metadata
      });
    } catch (e) {
      inventory.push({
        sceneId: scene.id,
        chapterTitle: scene.chapterTitle,
        sceneTitle: scene.title,
        wordCount: scene.wordCount,
        error: 'Analysis failed'
      });
    }
  }
  
  return inventory;
}

async function checkContinuity(scenes, characters, llmService) {
  const issues = [];
  
  // Check character consistency across scenes
  const characterAppearances = new Map();
  
  for (const scene of scenes) {
    // Simple name detection for tracking
    for (const char of characters) {
      if (scene.content.includes(char.name)) {
        if (!characterAppearances.has(char.name)) {
          characterAppearances.set(char.name, []);
        }
        characterAppearances.get(char.name).push({
          sceneId: scene.id,
          chapterTitle: scene.chapterTitle,
          sceneTitle: scene.title,
          order: scene.order
        });
      }
    }
  }

  // For each character with multiple appearances, check for continuity
  for (const [charName, appearances] of characterAppearances) {
    if (appearances.length < 2) continue;
    
    // Sample first and last appearance for contradiction check
    const firstScene = scenes.find(s => s.id === appearances[0].sceneId);
    const lastScene = scenes.find(s => s.id === appearances[appearances.length - 1].sceneId);
    
    if (!firstScene || !lastScene) continue;
    
    const prompt = `Check for continuity issues with the character "${charName}" between these two scenes from the same novel.

Early scene (${appearances[0].chapterTitle}):
---
${firstScene.content.substring(0, 2000)}
---

Later scene (${appearances[appearances.length - 1].chapterTitle}):
---
${lastScene.content.substring(0, 2000)}
---

Look for:
- Physical description contradictions
- Personality inconsistencies
- Timeline impossibilities
- Knowledge contradictions (character knows something they shouldn't yet)
- Location impossibilities

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{"issues": [{"type": "...", "description": "...", "severity": "low|medium|high"}]}
If no issues found, return: {"issues": []}`;

    try {

function chunkText(text, maxTokenEstimate) {
  // Rough estimate: 1 token ≈ 4 chars
  const maxChars = maxTokenEstimate * 4;
  const chunks = [];
  
  if (text.length <= maxChars) return [text];
  
  // Split on paragraph boundaries
  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\n+/);
  let current = '';
  
  for (const para of paragraphs) {
    if (current.length + para.length > maxChars && current.length > 0) {
      chunks.push(current);
      current = para;
    } else {
      current += (current ? '\n\n' : '') + para;
    }
  }
  if (current) chunks.push(current);
  
  return chunks;
}

### The Import Wizard UI — Tying It All Together

```jsx
// src/components/ImportWizard.jsx
import React, { useState, useCallback } from 'react';
import { detectAssetType } from '../services/importAssets.js';
import { parseManuscript } from '../services/importManuscript.js';

const STEPS = ['upload', 'classify', 'preview', 'options', 'importing', 'complete'];

export default function ImportWizard({ projectId, documentManager, llmService, onComplete, onCancel }) {
  const [step, setStep] = useState('upload');
  const [files, setFiles] = useState([]);
  const [classifiedFiles, setClassifiedFiles] = useState([]);
  const [importOptions, setImportOptions] = useState({
    runAnalysis: true,
    extractCharacters: true,
    extractWorldRules: true,
    deriveBeatMap: true,
    buildSceneInventory: true,
    checkContinuity: true
  });
  const [progress, setProgress] = useState({ current: 0, total: 0, message: '' });
  const [report, setReport] = useState(null);
  const [conflicts, setConflicts] = useState([]);

  // Step 1: Upload
  const handleFileDrop = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const droppedFiles = Array.from(e.dataTransfer?.files || e.target.files || []);
    const mdFiles = droppedFiles.filter(f => 
      f.name.endsWith('.md') || f.name.endsWith('.markdown') || f.name.endsWith('.txt')
    );
    setFiles(prev => [...prev, ...mdFiles]);
  }, []);

  const handleRemoveFile = useCallback((idx) => {
    setFiles(prev => prev.filter((_, i) => i !== idx));
  }, []);

  // Step 2: Classify
  const classifyFiles = useCallback(async () => {
    setStep('classify');
    const classified = [];
    
    for (const file of files) {
      const text = await file.text();
      const detection = detectAssetType(text);
      classified.push({
        file,
        text,
        detectedType: detection.type,
        confidence: detection.confidence,
        scores: detection.scores,
        confirmedType: detection.type // user can override
      });
    }
    
    setClassifiedFiles(classified);
  }, [files]);

  const handleTypeOverride = useCallback((idx, newType) => {
    setClassifiedFiles(prev => prev.map((cf, i) => 
      i === idx ? { ...cf, confirmedType: newType } : cf
    ));
  }, []);

  // Step 3: Preview
  const handlePreview = useCallback(() => {
    setStep('preview');
  }, []);

  // Step 4: Options
  const handleOptions = useCallback(() => {
    setStep('options');
  }, []);

  // Step 5: Import
  const handleImport = useCallback(async () => {
    setStep('importing');
    
    const manuscriptFiles = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => cf.confirmedType === 'manuscript');
    const assetFiles = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => cf.confirmedType !== 'manuscript');
    
    let totalSteps = manuscriptFiles.length + assetFiles.length;
    if (importOptions.runAnalysis) totalSteps += 5; // 5 analysis stages
    setProgress({ current: 0, total: totalSteps, message: 'Starting import...' });
    
    let currentStep = 0;
    
    try {
      // Import manuscripts first
      for (const mf of manuscriptFiles) {
        setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: `Importing manuscript: ${mf.file.name}` });
        const parsed = parseManuscript(mf.text);
        await importManuscriptToProject(parsed, documentManager);
      }
      
      // Import assets
      if (assetFiles.length > 0) {
        setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Importing assets...' });
        const assetReport = await importAssetsToProject(assetFiles, projectId, documentManager);
        
        if (assetReport.conflicts.length > 0) {
          setConflicts(assetReport.conflicts);
          // Pause for conflict resolution — handled in

      setReport(prev => ({ ...prev, error: err.message }));
      setStep('complete');
    }
  }, [classifiedFiles, importOptions, projectId, documentManager, llmService]);

  const runAnalysisStages = async (startStep, totalSteps) => {
    let currentStep = startStep;
    
    if (importOptions.extractCharacters) {
      setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Extracting characters...' });
      const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
      const allScenes = [];
      for (const ch of chapters) {
        const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(ch.id);
        allScenes.push(...scenes);
      }
      const fullText = allScenes.map(s => s.content).join('\n\n---\n\n');
      const characters = await extractCharactersFromText(fullText, llmService);
      
      // Save extracted characters as documents
      for (const char of characters) {
        await documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'character',
          title: char.name,
          content: JSON.stringify(char),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: 'characters',
          tags: ['auto-extracted', char.role].filter(Boolean),
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
      }
      setReport(prev => ({ ...prev, characters }));
    }
    
    if (importOptions.extractWorldRules) {
      setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Extracting world rules...' });
      const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
      const allScenes = [];
      for (const ch of chapters) {
        const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(ch.id);
        allScenes.push(...scenes);
      }
      const fullText = allScenes.map(s => s.content).join('\n\n---\n\n');
      const worldRules = await extractWorldRulesFromText(fullText, llmService);
      
      for (const rule of worldRules) {
        await documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'worldRule',
          title: rule.title,
          content: JSON.stringify(rule),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: 'worldRules',
          tags: ['auto-extracted', rule.category].filter(Boolean),
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
      }
      setReport(prev => ({ ...prev, worldRules }));
    }
    
    if (importOptions.deriveBeatMap) {
      setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Deriving beat map...' });
      const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
      const allScenes = [];
      for (const ch of chapters) {
        const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(ch.id);
        allScenes.push(...scenes.map(s => ({ ...s, chapterTitle: ch.title })));
      }
      const beatMap = await deriveBeatMapFromChapters(chapters, allScenes, llmService);
      
      await documentManager.saveDocument({
        projectId,
        type: 'beatMap',
        title: 'Derived Beat Map',
        content: JSON.stringify(beatMap),
        domain: 'preproduction',
        subdomain: 'beatMap',
        tags: ['auto-derived'],
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
      });
      setReport(prev => ({ ...prev, beatMap }));
    }
    
    if (importOptions.buildSceneInventory) {
      setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Building scene inventory...' });
      const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
      const allScenes = [];
      for (const ch of chapters) {
        const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(ch.id);
        allScenes.push(...scenes.map(s => ({ ...s, chapterTitle: ch.title })));
      }
      const inventory = await buildSceneInventory(chapters, allScenes, llmService);
      
      await documentManager.saveDocument({
        projectId,
        type: 'sceneInventory',
        title: 'Scene Inventory',
        content: JSON.stringify(inventory),
        domain: 'preproduction',
        subdomain: 'sceneInventory',
        tags: ['auto-built'],
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
      });
      setReport(prev => ({ ...prev, sceneInventory: inventory }));
    }
    
    if (importOptions.checkContinuity) {
      setProgress({ current: ++currentStep, total: totalSteps, message: 'Checking continuity...' });
      const chapters = await documentManager.getChapters(projectId);
      const allScenes = [];
      for (const ch of chapters) {
        const scenes = await documentManager.getScenes(ch.id);
        allScenes.push(...scenes.map(s => ({ ...s, chapterTitle: ch.title })));
      }
      const characters = report?.characters || [];
      const continuity = await checkContinuity(allScenes, characters, llmService);

      const continuity = await checkContinuity(allScenes, characters, llmService);

        projectId,
        type: 'continuityReport',

        content: JSON.stringify(continuity),

  const handleConflictResolution = useCallback(async (idx, resolution) => {
    const conflict = conflicts[idx];
    const updatedConflicts = [...conflicts];
    updatedConflicts[idx] = { ...conflict, resolution };
    setConflicts(updatedConflicts);
    
    // Apply resolution
    switch (resolution) {
      case 'skip':
        // Do nothing, keep existing
        break;
      case 'replace':
        await documentManager.updateDocument(conflict.existing.id, {
          content: JSON.stringify(conflict.incoming),
          updatedAt: Date.now(),
          tags: [...(conflict.existing.tags || []), 'replaced-by-import']
        });
        break;
      case 'merge':
        const merged = mergeDocuments(conflict.existing, conflict.incoming);
        await documentManager.updateDocument(conflict.existing.id, {
          content: JSON.stringify(merged),
          updatedAt: Date.now(),
          tags: [...(conflict.existing.tags || []), 'merged-import']
        });
        break;
      case 'duplicate':
        await documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: conflict.type,
          title: conflict.incoming.name + ' (imported)',
          content: JSON.stringify(conflict.incoming),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: conflict.type === 'character' ? 'characters' : conflict.type,
          tags: ['imported', 'duplicate'],
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
        break;
    }
    
    // If all conflicts resolved, continue import
    if (updatedConflicts.every(c => c.resolution !== null)) {
      setStep('importing');
      if (importOptions.runAnalysis) {
        await runAnalysisStages(progress.current, progress.total);
      }
      setStep('complete');
    }
  }, [conflicts, projectId, documentManager, importOptions, progress]);

  function mergeDocuments(existing, incoming) {
    const existingData = typeof existing.content === 'string' ? JSON.parse(existing.content) : existing;
    const incomingData = incoming;
    
    const merged = { ...existingData };
    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(incomingData)) {
      if (!value) continue;
      if (!merged[key]) {
        merged[key] = value;
      } else if (typeof value === 'string' && typeof merged[key] === 'string') {
        if (!merged[key].includes(value)) {
          merged[key] = merged[key] + '\n\n' + value;
        }
      } else if (Array.isArray(value) && Array.isArray(merged[key])) {
        const existingSet = new Set(merged[key].map(v => JSON.stringify(v)));
        for (const item of value) {
          if (!existingSet.has(JSON.stringify(item))) {
            merged[key].push(item);
          }
        }
      }
    }
    return merged;
  }

  // --- Render ---
  
  return (
    <div className="import-wizard-overlay">
      <div className="import-wizard">
        <div className="wizard-header">
          <h2>Import Files</h2>
          <div className="wizard-steps">
            {STEPS.map(s => (
              <span key={s} className={`wizard-step ${s === step ? 'active' : ''}`}>
                {s.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + s.slice(1)}
              </span>
            ))}
          </div>
          <button className="wizard-close" onClick={onCancel}>×</button>
        </div>

        <div className="wizard-body">
          {step === 'upload' && (
            <UploadStep
              files={files}
              onDrop={handleFileDrop}
              onRemove={handleRemoveFile}
              onNext={classifyFiles}
            />
          )}

          {step === 'classify' && (
            <ClassifyStep
              classifiedFiles={classifiedFiles}
              onTypeOverride={handleTypeOverride}
              onNext={handlePreview}
              onBack={() => setStep('upload')}
            />
          )}

          {step === 'preview' && (
            <PreviewStep
              classifiedFiles={classifiedFiles}
              onNext={handleOptions}
              onBack={() => setStep('classify')}
            />
          )}

          {step === 'options' && (
            <OptionsStep
              options={importOptions}
              onChange={setImportOptions}
              hasManuscript={classifiedFiles.some(cf => cf.confirmedType === 'manuscript')}
              onNext={handleImport}
              onBack={handlePreview}
            />
          )}

          {step === 'conflicts' && (
            <ConflictResolver
              conflicts={conflicts}
              onResolve={handleConflictResolution}
            />
          )}

          {step === 'importing' && (
            <ImportingStep progress={progress} />
          )}

          {step === 'complete' && (
            <CompleteStep report={report} onDone={onComplete} />
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```jsx
// Sub-components for each wizard step

function UploadStep({ files, onDrop, onRemove, onNext }) {
  const fileInputRef = React.useRef(null);
  const [dragOver, setDragOver] = useState(false);

  return (
    <div className="wizard-step-content">
      <div
        className={`drop-zone ${dragOver ? 'drag-over' : ''}`}
        onDragOver={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); setDragOver(true); }}
        onDragLeave={() => setDragOver(false)}
        onDrop={(e) => { setDragOver(false); onDrop(e); }}
        onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()}
      >
        <div className="drop-zone-icon">📄</div>
        <p>Drag & drop Markdown files here, or click to browse</p>
        <p className="drop-zone-hint">Supports .md, .markdown, .txt files</p>
        <input
          ref={fileInputRef}
          type="file"
          multiple
          accept=".md,.markdown,.txt"
          onChange={onDrop}
          style={{ display: 'none' }}
        />
      </div>

      {files.length > 0 && (
        <div className="file-list">
          <h4>{files.length} file{files.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} selected</h4>
          {files.map((f, idx) => (
            <div key={idx} className="file-list-item">
              <span className="file-name">{f.name}</span>
              <span className="file-size">{(f.size / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB</span>
              <button className="file-remove" onClick={() => onRemove(idx)}>×</button>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="wizard-actions">
        <button disabled={files.length === 0} onClick={onNext}>
          Next: Classify Files →
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

function ClassifyStep({ classifiedFiles, onTypeOverride, onNext, onBack }) {
  const typeOptions = [
    { value: 'manuscript', label: '📖 Manuscript' },
    { value: 'character', label: '👤 Character Dossier' },
    { value: 'worldRule', label: '🌍 World Rules' },
    { value: 'beatMap', label: '🗺️ Beat Map' },
    { value: 'sceneInventory', label: '📋 Scene Inventory' },
    { value: 'unknown', label: '❓ Unknown / Skip' }
  ];

  const confidenceColor = (conf) => {
    if (conf >= 0.8) return '#4caf50';
    if (conf >= 0.5) return '#ff9800';
    return '#f44336';
  };

  return (
    <div className="wizard-step-content">
      <p>We've analyzed your files and detected their types. Please verify or correct:</p>
      
      <div className="classification-list">
        {classifiedFiles.map((cf, idx) => (
          <div key={idx} className="classification-item">
            <div className="classification-header">
              <span className="file-name">{cf.file.name}</span>
              <span 
                className="confidence-badge"
                style={{ backgroundColor: confidenceColor(cf.confidence) }}
              >
                {Math.round(cf.confidence * 100)}% confident
              </span>
            </div>
            
            <div className="classification-scores">
              {Object.entries(cf.scores).map(([type, score]) => (
                <div key={type} className="score-bar">
                  <span className="score-label">{type}</span>
                  <div className="score-track">
                    <div 
                      className="score-fill" 
                      style={{ width: `${score * 100}%` }}
                    />
                  </div>
                  <span className="score-value">{Math.round(score * 100)}</span>
                </div>
              ))}
            </div>

            <div className="classification-select">
              <label>Import as:</label>
              <select
                value={cf.confirmedType}
                onChange={(e) => onTypeOverride(idx, e.target.value)}
              >
                {typeOptions.map(opt => (
                  <option key={opt.value} value={opt.value}>{opt.label}</option>
                ))}
              </select>
            </div>

            <details className="file-preview-details">
              <summary>Preview content</summary>
              <pre className="file-preview-text">
                {cf.text.substring(0, 1000)}
                {cf.text.length > 1000 ? '\n...(truncated)' : ''}
              </pre>
            </details>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>

      <div className="wizard-actions">
        <button onClick={onBack}>← Back</button>
        <button onClick={onNext}>Next: Preview Import →</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

function PreviewStep({ classifiedFiles, onNext, onBack }) {

  const beatMaps = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => cf.confirmedType === 'beatMap');
  const sceneInventories = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => cf.confirmedType === 'sceneInventory');
  const skipped =

          {manuscriptPreviews.map((mp, idx) => (
            <div key={idx} className="preview-card">
              <strong>{mp.fileName}</strong>
              <ul>
                <li>{mp.chapters} chapters, {mp.totalScenes} scenes</li>
                <li>~{mp.estimatedWords.toLocaleString()} words</li>
                <li>Chapters: {mp.chapterTitles.join(', ')}</li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {characters.length > 0 && (
        <div className="preview-section">
          <h4>👤 Character Dossiers ({characters.length})</h4>
          {characters.map((cf, idx) => (
            <div key={idx} className="preview-card">
              <strong>{cf.file.name}</strong>
              <p>{cf.text.substring(0, 200)}...</p>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {worldRules.length > 0 && (
        <div className="preview-section">
          <h4>🌍 World Rules ({worldRules.length})</h4>

              <p>{cf.text.substring(0, 200)}...</p>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {beatMaps.length > 0 && (
        <div className="preview-section">
          <h4>🗺️ Beat Maps ({beatMaps.length})</h4>

              <strong>{cf.file.name}</strong>
            </div>

        <div className="preview-section">
          <h4>📋 Scene Inventories ({sceneInventories.length})</h4>

              <strong>{cf.file.name}</strong>
            </div>

              <strong>{cf.file.name}</strong> — will not be imported
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="wizard-actions">
        <button onClick={onBack}>← Back</button>
        <button onClick={onNext}>Next: Options →</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

                <input

                  checked={options.deriveBeatMap}
                  onChange={() => toggle

function ConflictResolver({ conflicts, onResolve }) {
  return (
    <div className="wizard-step-content">
      <h3>Conflicts Detected</h3>
      <p>Some imported items match existing project data. Choose how to handle each:</p>

      {conflicts.map((conflict, idx) => (
        <div key={idx} className={`conflict-card ${conflict.resolution ? 'resolved' : ''}`}>
          <div className="conflict-header">
            <span className="conflict-type">{conflict.type}</span>
            <strong>{conflict.incoming.name || conflict.incoming.title}</strong>
            {conflict.resolution && (
              <span className="conflict-resolved-badge">✓ {conflict.resolution}</span>
            )}
          </div>

          <div className="conflict-comparison">
            <div className="conflict-existing">
              <h5>Existing</h5>
              <pre>{typeof conflict.existing.content === 'string' 
                ? conflict.existing.content.substring(0, 300) 
                : JSON.stringify(conflict.existing, null, 2).substring(0, 300)
              }...</pre>
            </div>
            <div className="conflict-incoming">
              <h5>Incoming</h5>
              <pre>{JSON.stringify(conflict.incoming, null, 2).substring(0, 300)}...</pre>
            </div>
          </div>

          {!conflict.resolution && (
            <div className="conflict-actions">
              <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'skip')} title="Keep existing, discard incoming">
                Skip
              </button>
              <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'replace')} title="Replace existing with incoming">
                Replace
              </button>
              <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'merge')} title="Merge both versions together">
                Merge
              </button>
              <button onClick={() => onResolve(idx, 'duplicate')} title="Keep both as separate entries">
                Keep Both
              </button>
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

function ImportingStep({ progress }) {
  const percentage = progress.total > 0 
    ? Math.round((progress.current / progress.total) * 100) 
    : 0;

  return (
    <div className="wizard-step-content importing-step">
      <div className="import-spinner" />
      <h3>Importing...</h3>
      <p className="import-message">{progress.message}</p>
      <div className="progress-bar-container">
        <div className="progress-bar" style={{ width: `${percentage}%` }} />
      </div>
      <p className="progress-text">{progress.current} / {progress.total} ({percentage}%)</p>
    </div>
  );
}

function CompleteStep({ report, onDone }) {
  return (
    <div className="wizard-step-content complete-step">
      <div className="complete-icon">✅</div>
      <h3>Import Complete</h3>

      {report?.error && (
        <div className="import-error">
          <strong>Error:</strong> {report.error}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="import-summary">
        {report?.characters && (
          <div className="summary-item">
            <span className="summary-icon">👤</span>
            <span>{report.characters.length} characters extracted</span>
          </div>
        )}
        {report?.worldRules && (
          <div className="summary-item">
            <span className="summary-icon">🌍</span>
            <span>{report.worldRules.length} world rules extracted</span>
          </div>
        )}
        {report?.beatMap && !report.beatMap.error && (
          <div className="summary-item">
            <span className="summary-icon">🗺️</span>
            <span>{report.beatMap.acts?.length || 0} acts mapped with story beats</span>
          </div>
        )}
        {report?.sceneInventory && (
          <div className="summary-item">
            <span className="summary-icon">📋</span>
            <span>{report.sceneInventory.length} scenes inventoried</span>
          </div>
        )}
        {report?.continuity && (
          <div className

        )}
      </div>

      {report?.continuity?.issues?.length > 0 && (
        <div className="continuity-issues-preview">
          <h4>Continuity Issues</h4>
          {report.continuity.issues.slice(0, 5).map((issue, idx) => (
            <div key={idx} className="continuity-issue">
              <strong>{issue.character}</strong>
              <ul>
                {issue.issues.map((iss, j) => (
                  <li key={j}>{iss}</li>
                ))}
              </ul>
            </div>
          ))}
          {report.continuity.issues.length > 5 && (
            <p className="more-issues">
              ...and {report.continuity.issues.length - 5} more issues. 
              See the full Continuity Report in your project.
            </p>
          )}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="wizard-actions">
        <button className="primary" onClick={onDone}>
          Done — Go to Project
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Now let me write the helper functions that bridge the classified files into the document manager.

```javascript
// src/services/importBridge.js
// Bridges between parsed/classified files and the project's document store

export async function importManuscriptToProject(parsed, documentManager, projectId) {
  const results = { chapters: 0, scenes: 0 };
  
  for (const chapter of parsed.chapters) {
    const chapterDoc = await documentManager.saveDocument({
      projectId,
      type: 'chapter',
      title: chapter.title,
      content: '', // chapter is a container
      domain: 'production',
      subdomain: 'chapters',
      order: chapter.order,
      metadata: {
        originalHeading: chapter.title,
        importedAt: Date.now()
      },
      createdAt: Date.now(),
      updatedAt: Date.now()
    });
    results.chapters++;

    for (let i = 0; i < chapter.scenes.length; i++) {
      const scene = chapter.scenes[i];
      await documentManager.saveDocument({
        projectId,
        type: 'scene',
        parentId: chapterDoc.id,
        title: scene.title || `Scene ${i + 1}`,
        content: scene.content,
        domain: 'production',
        subdomain: 'scenes',
        order: i,
        metadata: {
          chapterTitle: chapter.title,
          sceneIndex: i,
          wordCount: scene.content.split(/\s+/).length,
          importedAt: Date.now()
        },
        createdAt: Date.now(),
        updatedAt: Date.now()
      });
      results.scenes++;
    }
  }

  // Store frontmatter if present
  if (parsed.frontmatter && Object.keys(parsed.frontmatter).length > 0) {
    await documentManager.saveDocument({
      projectId,
      type: 'metadata',
      title: 'Manuscript Metadata',
      content: JSON.stringify(parsed.frontmatter, null, 2),
      domain: 'preproduction',
      subdomain: 'metadata',
      metadata: { source: 'frontmatter', importedAt: Date.now() },
      createdAt: Date.now(),
      updatedAt: Date.now()
    });
  }

  return results;
}

export async function importAssetsToProject(classifiedFiles, projectId, documentManager) {
  const results = { imported: 0, conflicts: [] };

  for (const cf of classifiedFiles) {
    if (cf.confirmedType === 'unknown') continue;

    const subdomain = {
      character: 'characters',
      worldRule: 'worldRules',
      beatMap: 'beatMap',
      sceneInventory: 'sceneInventory'
    }[cf.confirmedType] || cf.confirmedType;

    // Check for conflicts with existing documents
    const existing = await documentManager.findDocuments({
      projectId,
      type: cf.confirmedType,
      domain: 'preproduction'
    });

    // Simple name-based conflict detection
    const incomingTitle = extractTitleFromContent(cf.text, cf.file.name);
    const conflicting = existing.find(doc => {
      const docTitle = doc.title?.toLowerCase().trim();
      const incTitle = incomingTitle.toLowerCase().trim();
      return docTitle === incTitle || 
             levenshteinSimilarity(docTitle, incTitle) > 0.85;
    });

    if (conflicting) {
      results.conflicts.push({
        type: cf.confirmedType,
        existing: conflicting,
        incoming: {
          name: incomingTitle,
          content: cf.text,
          fileName: cf.file.name
        },
        resolution: null
      });
      continue;
    }

    // No conflict — import directly
    await documentManager.saveDocument({
      projectId,
      type: cf.confirmedType,
      title: incomingTitle,
      content: cf.text,
      domain: 'preproduction',
      subdomain,
      tags: ['imported'],
      metadata: {
        sourceFile: cf.file.name,
        detectedType: cf.detectedType,
        confirmedType: cf.confirmedType,
        confidence: cf.confidence,
        importedAt: Date.now()
      },
      createdAt: Date.now(),
      updatedAt: Date.now()
    });
    results.imported++;

```javascript
  }

  return results;

function extractTitleFromContent(text, fallbackFileName) {
  // Try to extract title from first heading
  const headingMatch = text.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m);
  if (headingMatch) return headingMatch[1].trim();
  
  // Try YAML frontmatter title
  const yamlMatch = text.match(/^---\s*\n[\s\S]*?title:\s*(.+)\n[\s\S]*?---/);
  if (yamlMatch) return yamlMatch[1].trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
  
  // Fall back to filename without extension
  return fallbackFileName.replace(/\.(md|markdown|txt)$/i, '').replace(/[-_]/g, ' ');
}

function levenshteinSimilarity(a, b) {
  if (!a || !b) return 0;
  const longer = a.length > b.length ? a : b;
  const shorter = a.length > b.length ? b : a;
  if (longer.length === 0) return 1.0;
  
  const costs = [];
  for (let i = 0; i <= shorter.length; i++) {
    let lastValue = i;
    for (let j = 0; j <= longer.length; j++) {
      if (i === 0) {
        costs[j] = j;
      } else if (j > 0) {
        let newValue = costs[j - 1];
        if (shorter[i - 1] !== longer[j - 1]) {
          newValue = Math.min(Math.min(newValue, lastValue), costs[j]) + 1;
        }
        costs[j - 1] = lastValue;
        lastValue = newValue;
      }
    }
    if (i > 0) costs[longer.length] = lastValue;
  }
  return (longer.length - costs[longer.length]) / longer.length;
}

Now let me write the LLM-powered analysis functions. These are the core AI extraction functions.

```javascript
// src/services/importAnalysis.js
// LLM-powered analysis functions for post-import processing

export async function extractCharactersFromText(fullText, llmService) {
  // Chunk the text if it's too long for a single prompt
  const chunks = chunkText(fullText, 12000);
  let allCharacters = [];

  for (const chunk of chunks) {
    const prompt = `Analyze the following fiction text and extract all named characters. For each character provide:
- name: Full name as used in the text
- aliases: Any other names, nicknames, or titles
- role: protagonist, antagonist, supporting, minor, mentioned
- description: Physical appearance if described
- personality: Key personality traits observed
- relationships: Array of { character, relationship } pairs
- firstAppearance: Brief note of where they first appear
- arc: Brief character arc summary if discernible

Return ONLY valid JSON as an array of character objects. No markdown formatting.

TEXT:
${chunk}`;

    try {
      const response = await llmService.complete(prompt, {
        temperature: 0.3,
        maxTokens: 4000
      });
      
      const parsed = parseJSONResponse(response);
      if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
        allCharacters.push(...parsed);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.warn('Character extraction failed for chunk:', err);
    }
  }

  // Deduplicate and merge characters found across chunks
  return deduplicateCharacters(allCharacters);
}

export async function extractWorldRulesFromText(fullText, llmService) {
  const chunks = chunkText(fullText, 12000);
  let allRules = [];

  for (const chunk of chunks) {
    const prompt = `Analyze the following fiction text and extract world-building rules and setting details. Categorize each into:
- magic_system: How magic/supernatural elements work, costs, limitations
- geography: Locations, distances, terrain, climate
- politics: Power structures, factions, governments, laws
- technology: Tech level, specific inventions, capabilities
- culture: Customs, religions, languages, social norms
- history: Historical events referenced, timeline
- economics: Currency, trade, resources
- biology: Species, creatures, flora/fauna

For each rule provide:
- title: Short descriptive title
- category: One of the categories above
- description: Detailed description of the rule
- evidence: Quote or reference from the text
- confidence: high, medium, low — how explicitly stated vs inferred

Return ONLY valid JSON as an array. No markdown formatting.

TEXT:
${chunk}`;

    try {
      const response = await llmService.complete(prompt, {
        temperature: 0.3,
        maxTokens: 4000
      });
      
      const parsed = parseJSONResponse(response);
      if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
        allRules.push(...parsed);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.warn('World rule extraction failed for chunk:', err);
    }
  }

  return deduplicateRules(allRules);
}

export async function deriveBeatMapFromChapters(chapters, scenes, llmService) {
  // Build a condensed summary of each chapter for the LLM
  const chapterSummaries = chapters.map(ch => {
    const chScenes = scenes.filter(s => s.chapterTitle === ch.title);
    const sceneTexts = chScenes.map((s, i) => {
      const preview = s.content.substring(0, 500);
      return `  Scene ${i + 1}: ${preview}...`;
    }).join('\n');
    return `Chapter: ${ch.title}\n${sceneTexts}`;
  }).join('\n\n---\n\n');

  const prompt = `Analyze this novel's chapter structure and map it to a three-act story structure with beats.

  const prompt = `Analyze this novel's chapter structure and map it to a three-act story structure with beats.

For each act, identify:
- act: 1, 2, or 3
- title: Name for this act
- startChapter: Which chapter it begins
- endChapter: Which chapter it ends
- beats: Array of story beats, each with:

Also provide:

CHAPTER SUMMARIES:

export async function buildSceneInventory(chapters, llmService) {
  const allScenes = [];

  for (const chapter of chapters) {
    for (let i = 0; i < chapter.scenes.length; i++) {
      const scene = chapter.scenes[i];
      const sceneText = scene.content.substring(0, 3000);

      const prompt = `Analyze this scene from a novel and provide a structured inventory entry.

Scene from "${chapter.title}", Scene ${i + 1}:
${sceneText}

Provide:
- title: A short descriptive title for this scene
- pov: Point-of-view character name
- povType: first, third-limited, third-omniscient, second
- location: Where the scene takes place
- timeOfDay: morning, afternoon, evening, night, unspecified
- timeRelative: How this scene relates temporally to the previous (immediately after, hours later, days later, weeks later, flashback, etc.)
- charactersPresent: Array of character names present in the scene
- charactersMentioned: Array of character names mentioned but not present
- conflict: The primary conflict or tension in this scene
- conflictType: internal, interpersonal, societal, environmental, supernatural
- purpose: What this scene accomplishes for the story (advances plot, reveals character, builds world, creates tension, provides relief, etc.)
- mood: The emotional tone (tense, melancholic, hopeful, humorous, etc.)
- keyEvents: Array of important things that happen
- hooks: Any unresolved questions or tension that carries forward
- wordCount: Approximate word count

Return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown formatting.`;

      try {
        const response = await llmService.complete(prompt, {
          temperature: 0.3,
          maxTokens: 1500
        });
        
        const parsed = parseJSONResponse(response);
        if (parsed) {
          allScenes.push({
            ...parsed,
            chapterTitle: chapter.title,
            chapterOrder: chapter.order,
            sceneIndex: i,
            wordCount: scene.content.split(/\s+/).length
          });
        }
      } catch (err) {
        console.warn(`Scene inventory failed for ${chapter.title} scene ${i + 1}:`, err);
        allScenes.push({
          title: `Scene ${i + 1}`,
          chapterTitle: chapter.title,
          chapterOrder: chapter.order,
          sceneIndex: i,
          wordCount: scene.content.split(/\s+/).length,
          error: err.message
        });
      }
    }
  }

  return allScenes;
}

export async function checkContinuity(scenes, characters, llmService) {
  // Build a condensed timeline and character tracker
  const sceneTimeline = scenes.map((s, idx) => ({

    chapter: s.chapterTitle,

    name: c.name,
    aliases: c.aliases,
    description: c.description,
    relationships: c.relationships
  }));

  const timelineStr = JSON.stringify(sceneTimeline, null, 1);
  const charStr = JSON.stringify(characterSummaries, null, 1);

  const prompt = `You are a continuity editor. Analyze the following scene timeline and character data for a novel. 
Identify any continuity errors, contradictions, or inconsistencies.

Check for:
1. Character presence: Is a character in two places at once? Does a character appear after supposedly leaving/dying?
2. Timeline: Do temporal references make sense? Are there impossible time jumps?
3. Location: Do characters travel impossible distances? Are locations described inconsistently?
4. Character details: Do physical descriptions, abilities, or knowledge contradict across scenes?
5. Object tracking: Do important objects appear/disappear without explanation?
6. Relationship consistency: Do character relationships change without justification?

SCENE TIMELINE:
${timelineStr.substring(0, 8000)}

CHARACTERS:
${charStr.substring(0, 4000)}

Return ONLY valid JSON with structure:
{
  "issues": [
    {
      "type": "character_presence" | "timeline" | "location" | "character_detail" | "object" | "relationship",
      "severity": "error" | "warning" | "

// Helper functions

function chunkText(text, maxChunkSize) {
  if (text.length <= maxChunkSize) return [text];
  
  const chunks = [];
  let remaining = text;
  
  while (remaining.length > 0) {
    if (remaining.length <= maxChunkSize) {
      chunks.push(remaining);
      break;
    }
    
    // Try to break at a paragraph boundary
    let breakPoint = remaining.lastIndexOf('\n\n', maxChunkSize);
    if (breakPoint < maxChunkSize * 0.5) {
      // No good paragraph break, try sentence
      breakPoint = remaining.lastIndexOf('. ', maxChunkSize);
    }
    if (breakPoint < maxChunkSize * 0.5) {
      // No good sentence break, just cut
      breakPoint = maxChunkSize;
    }
    
    chunks.push(remaining.substring(0, breakPoint));
    remaining = remaining.substring(breakPoint).trim();
  }
  
  return chunks;
}

function parseJSONResponse(response) {
  // LLMs sometimes wrap JSON in markdown code blocks
  let cleaned = response.trim();
  
  // Remove markdown code fences
  if (cleaned.startsWith('```json')) {
    cleaned = cleaned.slice(7);
  } else if (cleaned.startsWith('```')) {
    cleaned = cleaned.slice(3);
  }
  if (cleaned.endsWith('```')) {
    cleaned = cleaned.slice(0, -3);
  }
  cleaned = cleaned.trim();
  
  try {
    return JSON.parse(cleaned);
  } catch (e) {
    // Try to find JSON within the response
    const jsonMatch = cleaned.match(/[\[{][\s\S]*[\]}]/);
    if (jsonMatch) {
      try {
        return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
      } catch (e2) {
        console.error('Failed to parse JSON from LLM response:', e2);
        console.error('Response was:', response.substring(0, 500));
        return null;
      }
    }
    return null;
  }
}

function deduplicateCharacters(characters) {
  const merged = new Map();
  
  for (const char of characters) {
    const key = normalizeCharacterName(char.name);
    
    if (merged.has(key)) {
      const existing = merged.get(key);
      // Merge data
      existing.aliases = [...new Set([...(existing.aliases || []), ...(char.aliases || [])])];
      existing.relationships = mergeRelationships(existing.relationships, char.relationships);
      if (char.description && (!existing.description || char.description.length > existing.description.length)) {
        existing.description = char.description;
      }
      if (char.personality && (!existing.personality || char.personality.length > existing.personality.length)) {
        existing.personality = char.personality;
      }
      if (char.arc && (!existing.arc || char.arc.length > existing.arc.length)) {
        existing.arc = char.arc;
      }
      // Upgrade role if more prominent
      const rolePriority = { protagonist: 5, antagonist: 4, supporting: 3, minor: 2, mentioned: 1 };
      if ((rolePriority[char.role] || 0) > (rolePriority[existing.role] || 0)) {
        existing.role = char.role;
      }
    } else {
      merged.set(key, { ...char });
    }
  }
  
  return Array.from(merged.values());
}

function normalizeCharacterName(name) {
  return (name || '').toLowerCase().trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ');
}

function mergeRelationships(existing = [], incoming = []) {
  const map = new Map();
  for (const rel of [...existing, ...incoming]) {
    const key = normalizeCharacterName(rel.character);
    if (!map.has(key) || (rel.relationship && rel.relationship.length > (map.get(key).relationship || '').length)) {
      map.set(key, rel);
    }
  }
  return Array.from(map.values());
}

function deduplicateRules(rules) {
  const merged = new Map();
  
  for (const rule of rules) {
    const key = (rule.title || '').toLowerCase().trim();
    
    if (merged.has(key)) {
      const existing = merged.get(key);
      // Keep the one with more detail
      if (rule.description && rule.description.length > (existing.description || '').length) {
        merged.set(key, { ...existing, ...rule });
      }
    } else {
      merged.set(key, { ...rule });
    }
  }
  
  return Array.from(merged.values());
}
```

```javascript
// src/services/importOrchestrator.js
// Orchestrates the full import + analysis pipeline

import { parseManuscript } from './markdownParser';
import { importManuscriptToProject, importAssetsToProject } from './importBridge';
import {
  extractCharactersFromText,
  extractWorldRulesFromText,
  deriveBeatMapFromChapters,
  buildSceneInventory,
  checkContinuity
} from './importAnalysis';

export class ImportOrchestrator {
  constructor(documentManager, llmService) {
    this.documentManager = documentManager;
    this.llmService = llmService;
    this.onProgress = null; // callback: ({ current, total, message }) => void
  }

  setProgressCallback(cb) {
    this.onProgress = cb;
  }

  _progress(current, total, message) {
    if (this.onProgress) {
      this.onProgress({ current, total, message });
    }
  }

  async run(projectId, classifiedFiles, options, conflictResolutions = []) {
    const report = {};
    const manuscripts = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => cf.confirmedType === 'manuscript');
    const assets = classifiedFiles.filter(cf => 
      cf.confirmedType !== 'manuscript' && cf.confirmedType !== 'unknown'
    );

    // Calculate total steps
    let totalSteps = 0;
    if (manuscripts.length > 0) totalSteps += 1; // parse + import manuscripts
    if (assets.length > 0) totalSteps += 1; // import assets
    if (options.runAnalysis) {
      if (options.extractCharacters) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.extractWorldRules) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.deriveBeatMap) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.buildSceneInventory) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.checkContinuity) totalSteps += 1;
    }

    let currentStep = 0;

    // Step 1: Import manuscripts
    let parsedManuscripts = [];
    if (manuscripts.length > 0) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Parsing and importing manuscripts...');
      
      for (const mf of manuscripts) {
        const parsed = parseManuscript(mf.text);
        parsedManuscripts.push(parsed);
        const result = await importManuscriptToProject(parsed, this.documentManager, projectId);
        report.manuscriptImport = result;
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    // Step 2: Import assets (characters, world rules, etc.)
    if (assets.length > 0) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Importing supplementary files...');
      
      const assetResult = await importAssetsToProject(assets, projectId, this.documentManager);
      report.assetImport = assetResult;

      // Handle conflict resolutions
      if (assetResult.conflicts.length > 0 && conflictResolutions.length > 0) {
        for (let i = 0; i < assetResult.conflicts.length; i++) {
          const conflict = assetResult.conflicts[i];
          const resolution = conflictResolutions[i];
          if (resolution) {
            await this._resolveConflict(conflict, resolution, projectId);
          }
        }
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    // Combine all manuscript text for analysis
    const fullText = manuscripts.map(mf => mf.text).join('\n\n---\n\n');
    const allChapters = parsedManuscripts.flatMap(pm => pm.chapters);

    // Step 3: Extract characters
    if (options.runAnalysis && options.extractCharacters && fullText) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Extracting characters from manuscript...');
      
      try {
        const characters = await extractCharactersFromText(fullText, this.llmService);
        report.characters = characters;

        // Save extracted characters as documents
        for (const char of characters) {
          await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
            projectId,
            type: 'character',
            title: char.name,
            content: JSON.stringify(char, null, 2),
            domain: 'preproduction',
            subdomain: 'characters',
            tags: ['extracted', char.role],
            metadata: {
              ...char,
              source: 'ai-extraction',
              extractedAt: Date.now()
            },
            createdAt: Date.now(),
            updatedAt: Date.now()
          });
        }
      } catch (err) {
        report.characters = [];
        report.characterError = err.message;
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    // Step 4: Extract world rules
    if (options.runAnalysis && options.extractWorldRules && fullText) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Extracting world rules and setting...');
      
      try {
        const worldRules = await extractWorldRulesFromText(fullText, this.llmService);
        report.worldRules = worldRules;

        // Save as a single world-building document
        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'worldRule',
          title: 'Extracted World Rules',
          content: JSON.stringify(worldRules, null, 2),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: 'worldRules',
          tags: ['extracted'],
          metadata: {
            ruleCount: worldRules.length,
            categories: [...new Set(worldRules.map(r => r.category))],
            source: 'ai-extraction',
            extractedAt: Date.now()
          },
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()

      try {
        const allScenes = allChapters.flatMap(ch => 
          ch.scenes.map((s, i) => ({
            ...s,
            chapterTitle: ch.title,
            sceneIndex: i
          }))
        );
        const beatMap = await deriveBeatMapFromChapters(allChapters, allScenes, this.llmService);
        report.beatMap = beatMap;

        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'beatMap',
          title: 'Story Beat Map',
          content: JSON.stringify(beatMap, null, 2),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: 'beatMap',
          tags: ['extracted'],
          metadata: {
            actCount: beatMap.acts?.length || 0,
            theme: beatMap.theme,
            source: 'ai-extraction',
            extractedAt: Date.now()
          },
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
      } catch (err) {
        report.beatMap = { error: err.message, acts: [] };
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    // Step 6: Build scene inventory
    if (options.runAnalysis && options.buildSceneInventory && allChapters.length > 0) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Building scene inventory...');
      
      try {
        const sceneInventory = await buildSceneInventory(allChapters, this.llmService);
        report.sceneInventory = sceneInventory;

        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'sceneInventory',
          title: 'Scene Inventory',
          content: JSON.stringify(sceneInventory, null, 2),
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: 'sceneInventory',
          tags: ['extracted'],
          metadata: {
            sceneCount: sceneInventory.length,
            source: 'ai-extraction',
            extractedAt: Date.now()
          },
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
      } catch (err) {
        report.sceneInventory = [];
        report.sceneInventoryError = err.message;
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    // Step 7: Check continuity
    if (options.runAnalysis && options.checkContinuity && report.sceneInventory && report.characters) {
      this._progress(currentStep, totalSteps, 'Checking continuity...');
      
      try {
        const continuity = await checkContinuity(
          report.sceneInventory, 
          report.characters, 
          this.llmService
        );
        report.continuity = continuity;

        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: 'continuityReport',
          title: 'Continuity Report',
          content: JSON.stringify(continuity, null, 2),
          domain: 'postproduction',
          subdomain: 'continuity',
          tags: ['extracted'],
          metadata: {
            issueCount: continuity.issues?.length || 0,
            source: 'ai-extraction',
            extractedAt: Date.now()
          },
          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
      } catch (err) {
        report.continuity = { issues: [], summary: 'Check failed: ' + err.message };
      }
      currentStep++;
    }

    this._progress(totalSteps, totalSteps, 'Import complete!');
    return report;
  }

  async _resolveConflict(conflict, resolution, projectId) {
    switch (resolution) {
      case 'skip':
        // Do nothing — keep existing
        break;

      case 'replace':
        // Update existing document with incoming content
        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          ...conflict.existing,
          content: conflict.incoming.content,
          metadata: {
            ...conflict.existing.metadata,
            replacedAt: Date.now(),
            previousContent: conflict.existing.content?.substring(0, 500)
          },
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
        break;

      case 'merge':
        // Append incoming content to existing
        const mergedContent = conflict.existing.content + '\n\n---\n\n' + 
          '## Imported from: ' + conflict.incoming.fileName + '\n\n' +
          conflict.incoming.content;
        
        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          ...conflict.existing,
          content: mergedContent,
          metadata: {
            ...conflict.existing.metadata,
            mergedAt: Date.now(),
            mergedFrom: conflict.incoming.fileName
          },
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
        break;

      case 'duplicate':
        // Create a new document alongside the existing one
        await this.documentManager.saveDocument({
          projectId,
          type: conflict.type,
          title: conflict.incoming.name + ' (imported)',
          content: conflict.incoming.content,
          domain: 'preproduction',
          subdomain: conflict.type + 's',
          tags: ['imported', 'duplicate'],
          metadata: {
            sourceFile: conflict.incoming.fileName,
            duplicateOf: conflict.existing.id,
            importedAt: Date.

---

          createdAt: Date.now(),
          updatedAt: Date.now()
        });
        break;

      default:
        console.warn('Unknown conflict resolution:', resolution);
    }
  }
}

/* src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.css */

.import-wizard-overlay {
  position: fixed;

  left: 0;

  z-index: 1000;

  max-width: 720px;
  max-height: 85vh;
  overflow-y: auto;
  box-shadow: 0 20px 60px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-color, #333);
}

.wizard-header {

  align-items: center;

}

}

.wizard-close {

  border: none;

  color: var(--text-secondary, #999);
  padding: 4px 8px;
  border-radius: 4px;
}

}
