```jsx
// src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.jsx

import React, { useState, useCallback, useRef } from 'react';
import './ImportWizard.css';

const STEPS = ['upload', 'classify', 'options', 'conflicts', 'progress', 'results'];
const STEP_LABELS = ['Upload', 'Classify', 'Options', 'Conflicts', 'Import', 'Results'];

export default function ImportWizard({ projectId, onClose, orchestrator }) {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);
  const [files, setFiles] = useState([]);
  const [classifiedFiles, setClassifiedFiles] = useState([]);
  const [options, setOptions] = useState({
    runAnalysis: true,
    extractCharacters: true,
    extractWorldRules: true,
    deriveBeatMap: true,
    buildSceneInventory: true,
    checkContinuity: true
  });
  const [conflicts, setConflicts] = useState([]);
  const [conflictResolutions, setConflictResolutions] = useState([]);
  const [progress, setProgress] = useState({ current: 0, total: 1, message: '' });
  const [report, setReport] = useState(null);
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);
  const [isProcessing, setIsProcessing] = useState(false);

  const fileInputRef = useRef(null);

  // ---- Upload Step ----

  const handleFileDrop = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    const droppedFiles = Array.from(e.dataTransfer.files);
    addFiles(droppedFiles);
  }, []);

  const handleFileSelect = useCallback((e) => {
    const selectedFiles = Array.from(e.target.files);
    addFiles(selectedFiles);
  }, []);

  const addFiles = (newFiles) => {
    const validExtensions = ['.md', '.txt', '.markdown', '.json', '.yaml', '.yml'];
    const filtered = newFiles.filter(f => {
      const ext = '.' + f.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
      return validExtensions.includes(ext);
    });

    if (filtered.length < newFiles.length) {
      setError(`${newFiles.length - filtered.length} file(s) skipped (unsupported format)`);
      setTimeout(() => setError(null), 3000);
    }

    setFiles(prev => {
      const existingNames = new Set(prev.map(f => f.name));
      const unique = filtered.filter(f => !existingNames.has(f.name));
      return [...prev, ...unique];
    });
  };

  const removeFile = (index) => {
    setFiles(prev => prev.filter((_, i) => i !== index));
  };

  const formatFileSize = (bytes) => {
    if (bytes < 1024) return bytes + ' B';
    if (bytes < 1024 * 1024) return (bytes / 1024).toFixed(1) + ' KB';
    return (bytes / (1024 * 1024)).toFixed(1) + ' MB';
  };

  // ---- Classification Step ----

  const runClassification = async () => {
    setIsProcessing(true);
    setError(null);

    try {
      const results = [];
      for (const file of files) {
        const text = await readFileAsText(file);
        // Use the orchestrator's LLM service to classify
        // For now, use heuristic classification
        const classification = heuristicClassify(file.name, text);
        results.push({
          fileName: file.name,
          text,
          ...classification
        });
      }
      setClassifiedFiles(results);
      setCurrentStep(1);
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Classification failed: ' + err.message);
    } finally {
      setIsProcessing(false);
    }
  };

  const readFileAsText = (file) => {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const reader = new FileReader();
      reader.onload = () => resolve(reader.result);
      reader.onerror = () => reject(reader.error);
      reader.readAsText(file);
    });
  };

  const heuristicClassify = (fileName, text) => {
    const lower = fileName.toLowerCase();
    const textLower = text.toLowerCase().substring(0, 2000);

    // Check for manuscript indicators
    if (lower.includes('manuscript') || lower.includes('draft') || lower.includes('novel')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests manuscript' };
    }

    // Check for chapter headings in content
    const chapterPattern = /^#{1,2}\s+(chapter|part|prologue|epilogue)/im;
    if (chapterPattern.test(text)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Contains chapter headings' };
    }

    // Check for character sheet indicators
    if (lower.includes('character') || lower.includes('cast')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'character', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests character document' };
    }
    if (textLower.includes('age:') && textLower.includes('appearance:')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'character', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Content resembles character sheet' };
    }

    // Check for world-building
    if (lower.includes('world') || lower.includes('setting') || lower.includes('lore')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'worldRule', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests world-building' };
    }
    if (textLower.includes('magic system') || textLower.includes('geography') || textLower.includes('history of')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'worldRule', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Content resembles world-building' };
    }

    // Check for outline / beat sheet
    if (lower.includes('outline') || lower.includes('beat') || lower.includes('synopsis')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'beatMap', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests story outline' };
    }

    // Check for notes
    if (lower.includes('note') || lower.includes('todo') || lower.includes('idea')) {
      return { suggestedType: 'note', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Filename suggests notes' };
    }

    // Long text is likely manuscript
    if (text.length > 10000) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Long document, likely manuscript' };
    }

    return { suggestedType: 'unknown', confidence: 'low', reason: 'Could not determine type automatically' };
  };

  const updateClassification = (index, newType) => {
    setClassifiedFiles(prev => prev.map((cf, i) => 
      i === index ? { ...cf, confirmedType: newType } : cf
    ));
  };

  // Ensure confirmedType is set when entering classify step
  const confirmClassifications = () => {
    const confirmed = classifiedFiles.map(cf => ({
      ...cf,
      confirmedType: cf.confirmedType || cf.suggestedType
    }));
    setClassifiedFiles(confirmed);
    setCurrentStep(2);
  };

  // ---- Options Step ----

  const toggleOption = (key) => {
    setOptions(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: !prev[key] }));
  };

  // ---- Conflicts Step (skipped if none) ----

  const resolveConflict = (index, resolution) => {
    setConflictResolutions(prev => {
      const updated = [...prev];
      updated[index] = resolution;
      return updated;
    });
  };

  // ---- Run Import ----

  const startImport = async () => {
    setCurrentStep(4); // progress step
    setIsProcessing(true);
    setError(null);

    orchestrator.setProgressCallback((p) => {
      setProgress(p);
    });

    try {
      const result = await orchestrator.run(
        projectId,
        classifiedFiles,
        options,
        conflictResolutions
      );
      setReport(result);
      setCurrentStep(5); // results step
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Import failed: ' + err.message);
      setCurrentStep(5);
    } finally {
      setIsProcessing(false);
    }
  };

  // ---- Navigation ----

  const canProceed = () => {
    switch (currentStep) {
      case 0: return files.length > 0;
      case 1: return classifiedFiles.length > 0;
      case 2: return true;
      case 3: return conflicts.length === 0 || conflictResolutions.length === conflicts.length;
      default: return false;
    }
  };

  const handleNext = async () => {
    switch (currentStep) {
      case 0:
        await runClassification();
        break;
      case 1:
        confirmClassifications();
        break;
      case 2:
        // Check for conflicts before proceeding
        // For now, skip conflicts step if none detected
        setCurrentStep(3);
        if (conflicts.length === 0) {
          // Skip conflicts step, go straight to import
          await startImport();
        }
        break;
      case 3:
        await startImport();
        break;
      default:
        break;
    }
  };

  const handleBack = () => {
    if (currentStep > 0 && currentStep < 4) {
      setCurrentStep(prev => prev - 1);
    }
  };

  // ---- Render ----

  const renderUploadStep = () => (
    <div className="upload-step">
      <p>Drop your manuscript and supporting files to begin import.</p>
      
      <div
        className="drop-zone"
        onDrop={handleFil

              {conflict.type === 'character' && `Character "${conflict.name}" already exists in this project.`}
              {conflict.type === 'worldRule' && `World rule "${conflict.name}" conflicts with existing rule.`}
              {conflict.type === 'chapter' && `Chapter "${conflict.name}" already exists.`}
            </div>
            <div className="conflict-options">
              <label className={conflictResolutions[i] === 'keep' ? 'selected' : ''}>
                <input
                  type="radio"
                  name={`conflict-${i}`}

                  checked={conflictResolutions[i] === 'keep'}
                  onChange={() => resolveConflict(i, 'keep')}

                Keep existing

              <label className={conflictResolutions[i] === 'replace' ? 'selected' : ''}>

                  type="radio"

                  value="replace"
                  checked={conflictResolutions[i] === 'replace'}
                  onChange={() => resolveConflict(i, 'replace')}

                <input

                  value="merge"

  const renderStepContent = () => {
    switch (currentStep) {
      case 0: return renderUploadStep();
      case 1: return renderClassifyStep();
      case 2: return renderOptionsStep();
      case 3: return renderConflictsStep();
      case 4: return renderProgressStep();

      default: return null;
    }

  return (

      <div className="wizard-container" onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>
        {/* Header */}
        <div className="wizard-header">
          <h2 className="wizard-title">Import Wizard</h2>
          <button className="wizard-close" onClick={onClose}>✕</button>
        </div>

        {/* Step Indicator */}
        <div className="wizard-steps">
          {STEP_LABELS.map((label, i) => (
            <div
              key={label}
              className={`wizard-step ${i === currentStep ? 'active' : ''} ${i < currentStep ? 'completed' : ''}`}
            >
              <div className="step-dot">
                {i < currentStep ? '✓' : i + 1}
              </div>
              <span className="step-label">{label}</span>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>

        {/* Content */}
        <div className="wizard-content">
          {renderStepContent()}
        </div>

        {/* Footer */}
        {currentStep !== 4 && (
          <div className="wizard-footer">
            <div className="wizard-footer-left">
              {currentStep > 0 && currentStep < 4 && (
                <button className="wizard-btn" onClick={handleBack}>
                  ← Back
                </button>
              )}
            </div>
            <div className="wizard-footer-right">
              {currentStep === 5 ? (
                <button className="wizard-btn success" onClick={onClose}>
                  Done
                </button>
              ) : (
                <button
                  className="wizard-btn primary"
                  onClick={handleNext}
                  disabled={!canProceed() || isProcessing}
                >
                  {isProcessing ? 'Processing...' : currentStep === 3 ? 'Start Import' : 'Next →'}
                </button>
              )}
            </div>
          </div>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );

// src/services/ImportOrchestrator.js

export default class ImportOrchestrator {
  constructor(storageService, llmService) {
    this.storage = storageService;
    this.llm = llmService;
    this.progressCallback = null;
  }

  setProgressCallback(cb) {
    this.progressCallback = cb;
  }

  reportProgress(current, total, message) {
    if (this.progressCallback) {
      this.progressCallback({ current, total, message });
    }
  }

  async run(projectId, classifiedFiles, options, conflictResolutions) {
    const report = {
      chaptersImported: 0,
      charactersExtracted: 0,
      worldRulesExtracted: 0,
      scenesInventoried: 0,
      beatsIdentified: 0,
      actBreaks: [],
      conflictsResolved: 0,
      continuityIssues: [],
      errors: []
    };

    const manuscripts = classifiedFiles.filter(f => (f.confirmedType || f.suggestedType) === 'manuscript');
    const characterDocs = classifiedFiles.filter(f => (f.confirmedType || f.suggestedType) === 'character');
    const worldDocs = classifiedFiles.filter(f => (f.confirmedType || f.suggestedType) === 'worldRule');
    const beatDocs = classifiedFiles.filter(f => (f.confirmedType || f.suggestedType) === 'beatMap');
    const noteDocs = classifiedFiles.filter(f => (f.confirmedType || f.suggestedType) === 'note');

    // Calculate total steps for progress
    let totalSteps = 0;
    totalSteps += manuscripts.length; // parsing chapters
    totalSteps += characterDocs.length; // importing character docs
    totalSteps += worldDocs.length; // importing world docs
    totalSteps += beatDocs.length; // importing beat docs
    totalSteps += noteDocs.length; // importing notes
    if (options.runAnalysis) {
      if (options.extractCharacters) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.extractWorldRules) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.deriveBeatMap) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.buildSceneInventory) totalSteps += 1;
      if (options.checkContinuity) totalSteps += 1;
    }

    let currentStep = 0;

    // Step 1: Parse and import manuscripts
    for (const ms of manuscripts) {
      this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, `Parsing: ${ms.fileName}`);
      try {
        const chapters = this.parseManusc

    // Step 2: Import character documents
    for (const cd of characterDocs) {
      this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, `Importing characters: ${cd.fileName}`);
      try {
        const characters = this.parseCharacterDoc(cd.text);
        for (const char of characters) {
          await this.storage.saveCharacter(projectId, char);
          report.charactersExtracted++;
        }
      } catch (err) {
        report.errors.push(`Failed to import ${cd.fileName}: ${err.message}`);
      }
    }

    // Step 3: Import world-building documents
    for (const wd of worldDocs) {
      this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, `Importing world rules: ${wd.fileName}`);
      try {
        const rules = this.parseWorldDoc(wd.text);
        for (const rule of rules) {
          await this.storage.saveWorldRule(projectId, rule);
          report.worldRulesExtracted++;
        }
      } catch (err) {
        report.errors.push(`Failed to import ${wd.fileName}: ${err.message}`);
      }
    }

    // Step 4: Import beat/outline documents
    for (const bd of beatDocs) {
      this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, `Importing outline: ${bd.fileName}`);
      try {
        const beats = this.parseBeatDoc(bd.text);
        for (const beat of beats) {
          await this.storage.saveBeat(projectId, beat);
          report.beatsIdentified++;
        }
      } catch (err) {
        report.errors.push(`Failed to import ${bd.fileName}: ${err.message}`);
      }
    }

    // Step 5: Import notes
    for (const nd of noteDocs) {
      this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, `Importing notes: ${nd.fileName}`);
      try {
        await this.storage.saveNote(projectId, {
          title: nd.fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, ''),
          content: nd.text,
          source: 'import'
        });
      } catch (err) {
        report.errors.push(`Failed to import ${nd.fileName}: ${err.message}`);
      }
    }

    // Step 6: AI Analysis (if enabled)
    if (options.runAnalysis && this.llm) {
      const fullManuscriptText = manuscripts.map(m => m.text).join('\n\n---\n\n');

      if (options.extractCharacters && fullManuscriptText.length > 0) {
        this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, 'AI: Extracting characters from manuscript...');
        try {
          const extracted = await this.llmExtractCharacters(fullManuscriptText);
          for (const char of extracted) {
            await this.storage.saveCharacter(projectId, char);
            report.charactersExtracted++;
          }
        } catch (err) {
          report.errors.push(`AI character extraction failed: ${err.message}`);
        }
      }

      if (options.extractWorldRules && fullManuscriptText.length > 0) {
        this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, 'AI: Extracting world rules...');
        try {
          const rules = await this.llmExtractWorldRules(fullManuscriptText);
          for (const rule of rules) {
            await this.storage.saveWorldRule(projectId, rule);
            report.worldRulesExtracted++;
          }
        } catch (err) {
          report.errors.push(`AI world rule extraction failed: ${err.message}`);
        }
      }

      if (options.deriveBeatMap && fullManuscriptText.length > 0) {
        this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, 'AI: Mapping story structure...');
        try {
          const beatResult = await this.llmDeriveBeatMap(fullManuscriptText);
          for (const beat of beatResult.beats) {
            await this.storage.saveBeat(projectId, beat);
            report.beatsIdentified++;
          }
          report.actBreaks = beatResult.actBreaks || [];
        } catch (err) {
          report.errors.push(`AI beat mapping failed: ${err.message}`);
        }
      }

      if (options.buildSceneInventory && fullManuscriptText.length > 0) {
        this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, 'AI: Building scene inventory...');
        try {
          const scenes = await this.llmBuildSceneInventory(fullManuscriptText);
          for (const scene of scenes) {
            await this.storage.saveScene(projectId, scene);
            report.scenesInventoried++;
          }
        } catch (err) {
          report.errors.push(`AI scene inventory failed: ${err.message}`);
        }
      }

      if (options.checkContinuity && fullManuscriptText.length > 0) {
        this.reportProgress(++currentStep, totalSteps, 'AI: Checking continuity...');
        try {
          const issues = await this.llmCheckContinuity(fullManuscriptText);
          report.continuityIssues = issues;
        } catch (err) {
          report.errors.push(`AI continuity check failed: ${err.message}`);
        }
      }
    }

  // ---- Manuscript Parsing ----

  parseManuscriptIntoChapters(text, fileName) {
    const chapters = [];
    
    // Try to split by chapter headings
    // Common patterns: "# Chapter 1", "## Chapter One", "CHAPTER 1", "---" separators
    const chapterPattern = /^(#{1,3}\s+(?:chapter|part|prologue|epilogue|interlude)\s*[:\-]?\s*.*$|^CHAPTER\s+\w+.*$)/gim;
    
    const matches = [...text.matchAll(chapterPattern)];
    
    if (matches.length > 0) {
      for (let i = 0; i < matches.length; i++) {
        const start = matches[i].index;
        const end = i + 1 < matches.length ? matches[i + 1].index : text.length;
        const chapterText = text.substring(start, end).trim();
        const titleLine = matches[i][0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();
        
        chapters.push({
          title: titleLine,
          content: chapterText,
          order: i,
          source: fileName,
          wordCount: chapterText.split(/\s+/).length
        });
      }
    } else {
      // No chapter headings found - try splitting by horizontal rules
      const hrParts = text.split(/\n\s*(?:---+|\*\*\*+|___+)\s*\n/);
      
      if (hrParts.length > 1) {
        hrParts.forEach((part, i) => {
          const trimmed = part.trim();
          if (trimmed.length > 0) {
            // Try to extract title from first line
            const firstLine = trimmed.split('\n')[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();
            chapters.push({
              title: firstLine.length < 100 ? firstLine : `Section ${i + 1}`,
              content: trimmed,
              order: i,
              source: fileName,
              wordCount: trimmed.split(/\s+/).length
            });
          }
        });
      } else {
        // Single block - import as one chapter
        chapters.push({
          title: fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, ''),
          content: text.trim(),
          order: 0,
          source: fileName,
          wordCount: text.trim().split(/\s+/).length
        });
      }
    }

    return chapters;
  }

  // ---- Character Document Parsing ----

  parseCharacterDoc(text) {
    const characters = [];
    
    // Try to split by character entries (## Name or **Name**)
    const charPattern = /^(?:#{1,3}\s+(.+)|^\*\*(.+?)\*\*\s*$)/gm;
    const matches = [...text.matchAll(charPattern)];
    
    if (matches.length > 0) {
      for (let i = 0; i < matches.length; i++) {
        const name = (matches[i][1] || matches[i][2]).trim();
        const start = matches[i].index + matches[i][0].length;
        const end = i + 1 < matches.length ? matches[i + 1].index : text.length;
        const body = text.substring(start, end).trim();
        
        // Extract common fields
        const char = {
          name,
          description: body,
          source: 'import'
        };
        
        // Try to extract structured fields
        const ageMatch = body.match(/age[:\s]+(\d+|[\w\s-]+)/i);
        if (ageMatch) char.age = ageMatch[1].trim();
        
        const roleMatch = body.match(/role[:\s]+(.+)/i);
        if (roleMatch) char.role = roleMatch[1].trim();
        
        const traitMatch = body.match(/traits?[:\s]+(.+)/i);
        if (traitMatch) char.traits = traitMatch[1].split(/[,;]/).map(t => t.trim());
        
        characters.push(char);
      }
    } else {
      // Couldn't parse structured characters, store as single entry
      characters.push({
        name: 'Imported Characters',
        description: text,
        source: 'import'
      });
    }

    return characters;
  }

  // ---- World Document Parsing ----

  parseWorldDoc(text) {
    const rules = [];
    
    const sectionPattern = /^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/gm;
    const matches = [...text.matchAll(sectionPattern)];
    
    if (matches.length > 0) {
      for (let i = 0; i < matches.length; i++) {
        const title = matches[i][1].trim();
        const start = matches[i].index + matches[i][0].length;
        const end = i + 1 < matches.length ? matches[i + 1].index : text.length;
        const body = text.substring(start, end).trim();
        
        rules.push({
          title,
          content: body,
          category: this.guessWorldCategory(title, body),
          source: 'import'
        });
      }
    } else {
      rules.push({
        title: 'Imported World Rules',
        content: text,
        category: 'general',
        source: 'import'
      });
    }

    return rules;
  }

  guessWorldCategory(title, body) {
    const combined = (title + ' ' + body).toLowerCase();
    if (combined.includes('magic') || combined.includes('power') || combined.includes('

  // ---- Beat Document Parsing ----

  parseBeatDoc(text) {
    const beats = [];
    
    const linePattern = /^[-*•]\s+(.+)$|^(\d+[.)]\s+.+)$/gm;
    const matches = [...text.matchAll(linePattern)];
    
    if (matches.length > 0) {
      matches.forEach((match, i) => {
        const content = (match[1] || match[2]).trim();
        beats.push({
          order: i,
          description: content,
          type: this.guessBeatType(content),
          source: 'import'
        });
      });
    } else {
      // Try heading-based sections
      const sectionPattern = /^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/gm;
      const sections = [...text.matchAll(sectionPattern)];
      
      if (sections.length > 0) {
        sections.forEach((match, i) => {
          const start = match.index + match[0].length;
          const end = i + 1 < sections.length ? sections[i + 1].index : text.length;
          const body = text.substring(start, end).trim();
          
          beats.push({
            order: i,
            title: match[1].trim(),
            description: body,
            type: this.guessBeatType(match[1] + ' ' + body),
            source: 'import'
          });
        });
      } else {
        // Single block
        beats.push({
          order: 0,
          title: 'Imported Outline',
          description: text,
          type: 'general',
          source: 'import'
        });
      }
    }

    return beats;
  }

  guessBeatType(text) {
    const lower = text.toLowerCase();
    if (lower.includes('inciting') || lower.includes('catalyst')) return 'inciting_incident';
    if (lower.includes('climax')) return 'climax';
    if (lower.includes('resolution') || lower.includes('denouement')) return 'resolution';
    if (lower.includes('midpoint') || lower.includes('mid-point')) return 'midpoint';
    if (lower.includes('pinch') || lower.includes('reversal')) return 'pinch_point';
    if (lower.includes('opening') || lower.includes('hook')) return 'opening';
    if (lower.includes('dark night') || lower.includes('all is lost')) return 'dark_moment';
    if (lower.includes('turning point') || lower.includes('plot point')) return 'turning_point';
    if (lower.includes('setup') || lower.includes('exposition')) return 'setup';
    return 'beat';
  }

  static classifyFile(fileName, text) {

  // ---- Conflict Detection ----

  async detectConflicts(projectId, classifiedFiles) {
    const conflicts = [];
    
    const existingChapters = await this.storage.getChapters(projectId) || [];
    const existingCharacters = await this.storage.getCharacters(projectId) || [];
    const existingRules = await this.storage.getWorldRules(projectId) || [];

    for (const file of classifiedFiles) {
      const type = file.confirmedType || file.suggestedType;
      
      if (type === 'manuscript') {
        const chapters = this.parseManuscriptIntoChapters(file.text, file.fileName);
        for (const ch of chapters) {
          const existing = existingChapters.find(
            e => e.title.toLowerCase().trim() === ch.title.toLowerCase().trim()
          );
          if (existing) {
            conflicts.push({
              type: 'chapter',
              name: ch.title,
              existingId: existing.id,
              incoming: ch,
              fileName: file.fileName
            });
          }
        }
      }

      if (type === 'character') {
        const characters = this.parseCharacterDoc(file.text);
        for (const char of characters) {
          const existing = existingCharacters.find(
            e => e.name.toLowerCase().trim() === char.name.toLowerCase().trim()
          );
          if (existing) {
            conflicts.push({
              type: 'character',
              name: char.name,
              existingId: existing.id,
              incoming: char,
              fileName: file.fileName
            });
          }
        }
      }

      if (type === 'worldRule') {
        const rules = this.parseWorldDoc(file.text);
        for (const rule of rules) {
          const existing = existingRules.find(
            e => e.title.toLowerCase().trim() === rule.title.toLowerCase().trim()
          );
          if (existing) {
            conflicts.push({
              type: 'worldRule',
              name: rule.title,
              existingId: existing.id,
              incoming: rule,
              fileName: file.fileName
            });
          }
        }
      }
    }

    return conflicts;
  }

  // ---- Utility Methods ----

  getSample(text, maxChars) {
    if (text.length <= maxChars) return text;
    
    // Try to get beginning, middle, and end
    const third = Math.floor(maxChars / 3);
    const midStart = Math.floor(text.length / 2) - Math.floor(third / 2);
    const endStart = text.length - third;
    
    return [
      text.substring(0, third),
      '\n\n[...]\n\n',
      text.substring(midStart, midStart + third),
      '\n\n[...]\n\n',
      text.substring(endStart)
    ].join('');
  }

  parseJsonResponse(response, fallback) {
    try {
      // Try to extract JSON from the response
      let jsonStr = response;
      
      // Look for JSON in code blocks
      const codeBlockMatch = response.match(/```(?:json)?\s*\n?([\s\S]*?)\n?```/);
      if (codeBlockMatch) {
        jsonStr = codeBlockMatch[1];
      } else {
        // Try to find JSON array or object
        const jsonMatch = response.match(/(\[[\s\S]*\]|\{[\s\S]*\})/);
        if (jsonMatch) {
          jsonStr = jsonMatch[1];
        }
      }
      
      return JSON.parse(jsonStr.trim());
    } catch (err) {
      console.warn('Failed to parse LLM JSON response:', err);
      return fallback;
    }
  }
}

// src/utils/fileParsers.js

/**
 * Parse uploaded files into text content.
 * Supports: .txt, .md, .rtf, .docx (basic), .json
 */

export async function parseFile(file) {
  const ext = file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
  
  switch (ext) {
    case 'txt':
    case 'md':
    case 'markdown':
      return await readAsText(file);
    
    case 'rtf':
      return await parseRTF(file);
    
    case 'json':
      return await parseJSON(file);
    
    case 'docx':
      return await parseDocx(file);
    
    default:
      // Try reading as text
      try {
        return await readAsText(file);
      } catch {
        throw new Error(`Unsupported file format

function readAsText(file) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const reader = new FileReader();
    reader.onload = (e) => resolve(e.target.result);
    reader.onerror = () => reject(new Error(`Failed to read ${file.name}`));
    reader.readAsText(file);
  });
}

function readAsArrayBuffer(file) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const reader = new FileReader();
    reader.onload = (e) => resolve(e.target.result);
    reader.onerror = () => reject(new Error(`Failed to read ${file.name}`));
    reader.readAsArrayBuffer(file);
  });
}

async function parseRTF(file) {
  const text = await readAsText(file);
  // Basic RTF stripping - remove RTF control words and groups
  return text
    .replace(/\{\\[^{}]*\}/g, '') // Remove groups like {\fonttbl...}
    .replace(/\\[a-z]+\d*\s?/gi, '') // Remove control words like \par \b0
    .replace(/[{}]/g, '') // Remove remaining braces
    .replace(/\\\\/g, '\\') // Unescape backslashes
    .replace(/\\'/([0-9a-f]{2})/gi, (_, hex) => String.fromCharCode(parseInt(hex, 16))) // Hex chars
    .replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')
    .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
    .trim();
}

async function parseJSON(file) {
  const text = await readAsText(file);
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(text);
    // If it's a structured document, try to extract meaningful text
    if (typeof data === 'string') return data;
    if (data.content) return typeof data.content === 'string' ? data.content : JSON.stringify(data.content, null, 2);
    if (data.text) return data.text;
    if (data.chapters && Array.isArray(data.chapters)) {
      return data.chapters.map((ch, i) => {
        const title = ch.title || `Chapter ${i + 1}`;
        const content = ch.content || ch.text || '';
        return `## ${title}\n\n${content}`;
      }).join('\n\n---\n\n');
    }
    // Fallback: pretty-print the JSON
    return JSON.stringify(data, null, 2);
  } catch {
    return text; // Return raw text if JSON parsing fails
  }
}

async function parseDocx(file) {
  // Basic DOCX parsing without external dependencies
  // DOCX is a ZIP containing XML files
  const buffer = await readAsArrayBuffer(file);
  
  try {
    // Use JSZip if available, otherwise try basic extraction
    if (typeof JSZip !== 'undefined') {
      const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(buffer);
      const documentXml = await zip.file('word/document.xml').async('string');
      return extractTextFromDocumentXml(documentXml);
    }
    
    // Fallback: try to find text content in the raw bytes
    // This is a very rough approach but works for simple documents
    const uint8 = new Uint8Array(buffer);
    const decoder = new TextDecoder('utf-8', { fatal: false });
    const raw = decoder.decode(uint8);
    
    // Look for the document.xml content within the ZIP
    const xmlStart = raw.indexOf('<w:document');
    const xmlEnd = raw.indexOf('</w:document>');
    
    if (xmlStart !== -1 && xmlEnd !== -1) {
      const xml = raw.substring(xmlStart, xmlEnd + '</w:document>'.length);
      return extractTextFromDocumentXml(xml);
    }
    
    throw new Error('Could not parse DOCX file. Consider converting to .txt or .md first.');
  } catch (err) {
    if (err.message.includes('Could not parse')) throw err;
    throw new Error('Failed to parse DOCX file. Please convert to .txt or .md format.');
  }
}

function extractTextFromDocumentXml(xml) {
  const paragraphs = [];
  
  // Extract text from <w:t> elements within <w:p> paragraphs
  const paraPattern = /<w:p[\s>][\s\S]*?<\/w:p>/g;
  let paraMatch;
  
  while ((paraMatch = paraPattern.exec(xml)) !== null) {
    const paraXml = paraMatch[0];
    const texts = [];
    
    const textPattern = /<w:t(?:\s[^>]*)?>([^<]*)<\/w:t>/g;
    let textMatch;
    
    while ((textMatch = textPattern.exec(paraXml)) !== null) {
      texts.push(textMatch[1]);
    }
    
    if (texts.length > 0) {
      // Check if this paragraph has heading style
      const styleMatch = paraXml.match(/<w:pStyle\s+w:val="([^"]+)"/);
      const style = styleMatch ? styleMatch[1] : '';
      
      let line = texts.join('');
      
      if (style.startsWith('Heading') || style.startsWith('heading')) {
        const level = parseInt(style.replace(/\D/g, '')) || 1;
        line = '#'.repeat(Math.min(level, 6)) + ' ' + line;
      }
      
      paragraphs.push(line);
    } else {
      // Empty paragraph = blank line
      paragraphs.push('');
    }
  }
  
  return paragrap

/**
 * Estimate the type of content in a file based on its structure
 */
export function estimateFileStats(text) {
  const words = text.split(/\s+/).filter(w => w.length > 0).length;
  const lines = text.split('\n').length;
  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\s*\n/).filter(p => p.trim().length > 0).length;
  const headings = (text.match(/^#{1,6}\s+.+$/gm) || []).length;
  const dialogueLines = (text.match(/[""].+?[""]|['''].+?[''']/g) || []).length;
  
  return {
    words,
    lines,
    paragraphs,
    headings,
    dialogueLines,
    estimatedPages: Math.ceil(words / 250),
    estimatedReadingMinutes: Math.ceil(words / 200)
  };
}

export function validateFileSize(file, maxSizeMB = 10) {
  const sizeMB = file.size / (1024 * 1024);
  if (sizeMB > maxSizeMB) {
    throw new Error(`File ${file.name} is ${sizeMB.toFixed(1)}MB, exceeding the ${maxSizeMB}MB limit.`);
  }
  return true;
}

export function validateFileType(file) {
  const allowedExtensions = ['txt', 'md', 'markdown', 'rtf', 'docx', 'json'];
  const ext = file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
  if (!allowedExtensions.includes(ext)) {
    throw new Error(`File type .${ext} is not supported. Supported types: ${allowedExtensions.join(', ')}`);
  }
  return true;
}
```

Now let me build the React components for the import wizard UI. I'll create the main ImportWizard component.

```jsx
// src/components/import/ImportWizard.jsx

import React, { useState, useCallback, useRef } from 'react';
import { parseFile, estimateFileStats, validateFileSize, validateFileType } from '../../utils/fileParsers';
import { ImportOrchestrator } from '../../services/ImportOrchestrator';

const STEPS = {
  UPLOAD: 0,
  CLASSIFY: 1,
  OPTIONS: 2,
  CONFLICTS: 3,
  IMPORTING: 4,
  COMPLETE: 5
};

const FILE_TYPE_LABELS = {
  manuscript: 'Manuscript / Draft',
  character: 'Character Profiles',
  worldRule: 'World-Building / Lore',
  beatMap: 'Outline / Beat Sheet',
  note: 'Notes / Reference'
};

const FILE_TYPE_ICONS = {
  manuscript: '📖',
  character: '👤',
  worldRule: '🌍',
  beatMap: '📋',
  note: '📝'
};

export default function ImportWizard({ projectId, storage, llm, onComplete, onCancel }) {
  const [step, setStep] = useState(STEPS.UPLOAD);
  const [files, setFiles] = useState([]);
  const [options, setOptions] = useState({
    runAnalysis: true,
    extractCharacters: true,
    extractWorldRules: true,
    deriveBeatMap: true,
    buildSceneInventory: false,
    checkContinuity: false,
    conflictResolution: 'ask' // 'ask', 'skip', 'overwrite', 'merge'
  });
  const [conflicts, setConflicts] = useState([]);
  const [conflictResolutions, setConflictResolutions] = useState({});
  const [progress, setProgress] = useState({ current: 0, total: 0, message: '' });
  const [report, setReport] = useState(null);
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);
  
  const fileInputRef = useRef(null);
  const orchestratorRef = useRef(null);

  // ---- Step 1: File Upload ----

  const handleFileSelect = useCallback(async (e) => {
    const selectedFiles = Array.from(e.target.files);
    setError(null);
    
    const parsed = [];
    for (const file of selectedFiles) {
      try {
        validateFileSize(file);
        validateFileType(file);
        
        const text = await parseFile(file);
        const stats = estimateFileStats(text);
        const classification = ImportOrchestrator.classifyFile(file.name, text);
        
        parsed.push({
          id: `${file.name}-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`,
          fileName: file.name,
          fileSize: file.size,
          text,
          stats,
          suggestedType: classification.suggestedType,
          confirmedType: null,
          confidence: classification.confidence,
          classificationReason: classification.reason,
          included: true
        });
      } catch (err) {
        setError(prev => (prev ? prev + '\n' : '') + err.message);
      }
    }
    
    setFiles(prev => [...prev, ...parsed]);
  }, []);

  const handleDrop = useCallback(async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    
    const droppedFiles = Array.from(e.dataTransfer.files);
    // Create a synthetic event to reuse handleFileSelect logic
    const syntheticEvent = { target: { files: droppedFiles } };
    await handleFileSelect(syntheticEvent);
  }, [handleFileSelect]);

  const

  const handleDragOver = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
  }, []);

  const removeFile = useCallback((fileId) => {
    setFiles(prev => prev.filter(f => f.id !== fileId));
  }, []);

  const toggleFileIncluded = useCallback((fileId) => {
    setFiles(prev => prev.map(f => 
      f.id === fileId ? { ...f, included: !f.included } : f
    ));
  }, []);

  const updateFileType = useCallback((fileId, newType) => {
    setFiles(prev => prev.map(f =>
      f.id === fileId ? { ...f, confirmedType: newType } : f
    ));
  }, []);

  // ---- Step Navigation ----

  const goToClassify = () => {
    if (files.filter(f => f.included).length === 0) {
      setError('Please add at least one file to import.');
      return;
    }
    setError(null);
    setStep(STEPS.CLASSIFY);
  };

  const goToOptions = () => {
    setError(null);
    setStep(STEPS.OPTIONS);
  };

  const goToConflicts = async () => {
    setError(null);
    
    const orchestrator = new ImportOrchestrator(storage, llm);
    orchestratorRef.current = orchestrator;
    
    const includedFiles = files.filter(f => f.included);
    
    try {
      const detected = await orchestrator.detectConflicts(projectId, includedFiles);
      
      if (detected.length > 0 && options.conflictResolution === 'ask') {
        setConflicts(detected);
        // Initialize resolutions
        const resolutions = {};
        detected.forEach((c, i) => {
          resolutions[i] = 'skip'; // Default to skip
        });
        setConflictResolutions(resolutions);
        setStep(STEPS.CONFLICTS);
      } else {
        // No conflicts or auto-resolution chosen, go straight to import
        startImport(detected);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Conflict detection failed: ${err.message}`);
    }
  };

  const startImport = async (resolvedConflicts = []) => {
    setStep(STEPS.IMPORTING);
    setError(null);
    
    const orchestrator = orchestratorRef.current || new ImportOrchestrator(storage, llm);
    orchestratorRef.current = orchestrator;
    
    // Set up progress callback
    orchestrator.onProgress = (current, total, message) => {
      setProgress({ current, total, message });
    };

    const includedFiles = files.filter(f => f.included);
    
    // Apply conflict resolutions
    const finalConflicts = resolvedConflicts.map((c, i) => ({
      ...c,
      resolution: conflictResolutions[i] || options.conflictResolution || 'skip'
    }));

    try {
      const importReport = await orchestrator.importFiles(projectId, includedFiles, {
        ...options,
        conflicts: finalConflicts
      });
      
      setReport(importReport);
      setStep(STEPS.COMPLETE);
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Import failed: ${err.message}`);
      setStep(STEPS.OPTIONS); // Go back to options on failure
    }
  };

  // ---- Render Helpers ----

  const renderStepIndicator = () => {
    const stepNames = ['Upload', 'Classify', 'Options', 'Conflicts', 'Importing', 'Complete'];
    return (
      <div className="import-wizard-steps">
        {stepNames.map((name, i) => {
          if (i === STEPS.CONFLICTS && conflicts.length === 0 && step !== STEPS.CONFLICTS) return null;
          return (
            <div
              key={name}
              className={`step-indicator ${i === step ? 'active' : ''} ${i < step ? 'completed' : ''}`}
            >
              <span className="step-number">{i < step ? '✓' : i + 1}</span>
              <span className="step-name">{name}</span>
            </div>
          );
        })}
      </div>
    );
  };

  const renderUploadStep = () => (
    <div className="import-step upload-step">
      <h2>Import Files</h2>
      <p className="step-description">
        Upload your manuscript, character sheets, world-building documents, outlines, or notes.
        Supported formats: .txt, .md, .rtf, .docx, .json
      </p>

      <div
        className="drop-zone"
        onDrop={handleDrop}
        onDragOver={handleDragOver}
        onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()}
      >
        <div className="drop-zone-content">
          <span className="drop-icon">📁</span>
          <p>Drag & drop files here, or click to browse</p>
          <p className="drop-hint">Max 10MB per file</p>
        </div>
        <input
          ref={fileInputRef}
          type="file"
          multiple
          accept=".txt,.md,.markdown,.rtf,.docx,.json"
          onChange={handleFileSelect}
          style={{ display: 'none' }}
        />
      </div>

      {files.length > 0 && (
        <div className="file-list">
          <h3>Selected Files ({files.length})</h3>
          {files.map(f => (
            <div key={f.id} className={`file-item ${f.included ? '' : 'excluded'}`}>
              <label className="file-checkbox">

Now I need to build the ImportOrchestrator service that handles the actual import logic, file classification, conflict detection, and AI analysis orchestration.

```js
// src/services/ImportOrchestrator.js

export class ImportOrchestrator {
  constructor(storage, llm) {
    this.storage = storage;
    this.llm = llm;
    this.onProgress = null; // callback(current, total, message)
  }

  /**
   * Classify a file based on its name and content
   */
  static classifyFile(fileName, text) {
    const name = fileName.toLowerCase();
    const sample = text.substring(0, 3000).toLowerCase();
    const wordCount = text.split(/\s+/).length;

    // Check filename patterns first
    if (/character|cast|dramatis/i.test(name)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'character', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests character profiles' };
    }
    if (/world|lore|magic|setting|rules|bible/i.test(name)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'worldRule', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests world-building document' };
    }
    if (/outline|beat|structure|plot|synopsis/i.test(name)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'beatMap', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests outline or beat sheet' };
    }
    if (/note|ref|research|idea|brainstorm/i.test(name)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'note', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests notes or reference material' };
    }
    if (/chapter|manuscript|draft|novel|story|book/i.test(name)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'high', reason: 'Filename suggests manuscript content' };
    }

    // Content-based heuristics
    const dialogueCount = (text.match(/[""\u201C].+?[""\u201D]/g) || []).length;
    const headingCount = (text.match(/^#{1,6}\s+/gm) || []).length;
    const bulletCount = (text.match(/^[\s]*[-*•]\s+/gm) || []).length;
    const numberedCount = (text.match(/^\s*\d+[\.\)]\s+/gm) || []).length;

    // Character profile patterns
    const charPatterns = /\b(name|age|appearance|personality|backstory|motivation|traits|physical description|occupation|role)\s*[:—-]/gi;
    const charMatches = (sample.match(charPatterns) || []).length;
    if (charMatches >= 3) {
      return { suggestedType: 'character', confidence: 'medium', reason: `Content contains ${charMatches} character-profile fields` };
    }

    // World-building patterns
    const worldPatterns = /\b(rule|law|magic system|geography|history|culture|species|race|faction|technology|economy|religion|government)\s*[:—-]/gi;
    const worldMatches = (sample.match(worldPatterns) || []).length;
    if (worldMatches >= 2) {
      return { suggestedType: 'worldRule', confidence: 'medium', reason: `Content contains ${worldMatches} world-building descriptors` };
    }

    // Beat/outline patterns
    if ((bulletCount + numberedCount) > 10 && dialogueCount < 5) {
      return { suggestedType: 'beatMap', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Content is heavily structured with lists' };
    }
    if (/\b(act\s+[1-3i]+|inciting incident|climax|resolution|midpoint|turning point|pinch point)\b/i.test(sample)) {
      return { suggestedType: 'beatMap', confidence: 'medium', reason: 'Content contains story structure terminology' };
    }

    // Manuscript detection: long text with dialogue
    if (wordCount > 1000 && dialogueCount > 5) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'medium', reason: `Long text (${wordCount} words) with dialogue (${dialogueCount} instances)` };
    }
    if (wordCount > 3000) {
      return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'low', reason: `Long text (${wordCount} words), assumed to be manuscript` };
    }

    // Short text defaults to notes
    if (wordCount < 500) {
      return { suggestedType: 'note', confidence: 'low', reason: 'Short text, classified as notes by default' };
    }

    // Fallback
    return { suggestedType: 'manuscript', confidence: 'low', reason: 'Could not determine type; defaulting to manuscript' };
  }

    const existingCharacters = await this.storage.getCharacters(projectId) || [];
    const existingWorldRules = await this.storage.getWorldRules(projectId) || [];
    const

              importedPreview: ''
            });
          }
        }
      }
    }

  // ---- Private: Import Methods ----

  async _importManuscript(projectId, file, options, report, advance) {
    advance(`Importing manuscript: ${file.fileName}`);

    const chapters = this._splitIntoChapters(file.text, file.fileName);

    for (const chapter of chapters) {
      // Check if this chapter conflicts
      const conflict = (options.conflicts || []).find(
        c => c.type === 'manuscript' && c.name === chapter.title && c.fileName === file.fileName
      );

      if (conflict) {
        const resolution = conflict.resolution || 'skip';
        if (resolution === 'skip') {
          report.skipped++;
          continue;
        }
        if (resolution === 'overwrite') {
          await this.storage.updateChapter(projectId, conflict.existingId, {
            content: chapter.content,
            updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
          });
          report.chaptersImported++;
          continue;
        }
        if (resolution === 'merge') {
          const existing = await this.storage.getChapter(projectId, conflict.existingId);
          await this.storage.updateChapter(projectId, conflict.existingId, {
            content: (existing.content || '') + '\n\n---\n\n' + chapter.content,
            updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
          });
          report.chaptersImported++;
          continue;
        }
        // 'duplicate' falls through to create new
      }

      await this.storage.createChapter(projectId, {
        title: chapter.title,
        content: chapter.content,
        order: chapter.order,
        importedFrom: file.fileName,
        importedAt: new Date().toISOString()
      });
      report.chaptersImported++;
    }
  }

  async _importCharacters(projectId, file, options, report, advance) {
    advance(`Importing characters: ${file.fileName}`);

    const characters = this._parseCharacterFile(file.text);

    for (const char of characters) {
      const conflict = (options.conflicts || []).find(
        c => c.type === 'character' && c.name === char.name && c.fileName === file.fileName
      );

      if (conflict) {
        const resolution = conflict.resolution || 'skip';
        if (resolution === 'skip') {
          report.skipped++;
          continue;
        }
        if (resolution === 'overwrite') {
          await this.storage.updateCharacter(projectId, conflict.existingId, {
            ...char,
            updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
          });
          report.charactersImported++;
          continue;
        }
        if (resolution === 'merge') {
          const existing = await this.storage.getCharacter(projectId, conflict.existingId);
          await this.storage.updateCharacter(projectId, conflict.existingId, {
            description: (existing.description || '') + '\n\n' + (char.description || ''),
            notes: (existing.notes || '') + '\n\n' + (char.notes || ''),
            updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
          });
          report.charactersImported++;
          continue;
        }
      }

      await this.storage.createCharacter(projectId, {
        ...char,
        importedFrom: file.fileName,
        importedAt: new Date().toISOString()
      });
      report.charactersImported++;
    }
  }

          const existing = await this.storage.getWorldRule(projectId, conflict.existingId);
          await this.storage.updateWorldRule(projectId, conflict.existingId, {
            description: (existing.description || '') +

  async _importBeatMap(projectId, file, options, report, advance) {
    advance(`Importing beat map: ${file.fileName}`);

    for (const beat of beats) {

  async _importNote(projectId, file, options, report, advance) {
    advance(`Importing note: ${file.fileName}`);

    await this.storage.createNote(projectId, {
      title: file.fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, ''),
      content: file.text,
      importedFrom: file.fileName,
      importedAt: new Date().toISOString()
    });
    report.notesImported++;
  }

  // ---- Private: Parsing Helpers ----

  _splitIntoChapters(text, fileName) {
    const chapters = [];

    // Try to split by chapter headings
    const chapterPattern = /^(?:#{1,3}\s+)?(?:chapter\s+\d+[:\s]*|ch\.?\s*\d+[:\s]*)/gmi;
    const headingPattern = /^#{1,3}\s+.+$/gm;

    // First try explicit chapter markers
    const chapterSplits = text.split(/(?=^(?:#{1,3}\s+)?chapter\s+\d+)/gmi);

    if (chapterSplits.length > 1) {
      chapterSplits.forEach((chunk, i) => {
        const trimmed = chunk.trim();
        if (!trimmed) return;

        const firstLine = trimmed.split('\n')[0].trim();
        const title = firstLine.replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim() || `Chapter ${i + 1}`;

        chapters.push({
          title,
          content: trimmed,
          order: i
        });
      });
      return chapters;
    }

    // Try splitting by markdown headings
    const headingSplits = text.split(/(?=^#{1,2}\s+.+$)/gm);

    if (headingSplits.length > 1) {
      headingSplits.forEach((chunk, i) => {
        const trimmed = chunk.trim();
        if (!trimmed) return;

        const firstLine = trimmed.split('\n')[0].trim();
        const title = firstLine.replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();

        chapters.push({
          title,
          content: trimmed,
          order: i
        });
      });
      return chapters;
    }

    // Try splitting by scene breaks (*** or ---)
    const sceneSplits = text.split(/\n\s*(?:\*\s*\*\s*\*|---+|___+)\s*\n/);

    if (sceneSplits.length > 1 && sceneSplits.length <= 100) {
      sceneSplits.forEach((chunk, i) => {
        const trimmed = chunk.trim();
        if (!trimmed) return;

        chapters.push({
          title: `Section ${i + 1}`,
          content: trimmed,
          order: i
        });
      });
      return chapters;
    }

    // No clear structure - import as single chapter
    chapters.push({
      title: fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, ''),
      content: text,
      order: 0
    });

    return chapters;
  }

  _parseCharacterFile(text) {
    const characters = [];

    // Try to split by headings (each heading = a character)
    const sections = text.split(/(?=^#{1,3}\s+.+$)/gm).filter(s => s.trim());

    if (sections.length > 1 || (sections.length === 1 && /^#{1,3}\s+/.test(sections[0]))) {
      for (const section of sections) {
        const lines = section.trim().split('\n');
        const nameLine = lines[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();

        const body = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim();

          role: /\brole\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i,
          appearance: /\b(?:appearance|looks?|physical)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i,
          personality: /\b(?:personality|temperament)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i,
          backstory: /\b(?:backstory|background|history)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i,
          motivation: /\b(?:motivation|goal|want|desire)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i,
          traits: /\b(?:traits?|characteristics?)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i
        };

        for (const [field, pattern] of Object.entries(fieldPatterns)) {
          const match = body.match(pattern);
          if (match) {
            char[field] = match

    // Try field-based parsing (Name: ..., Age: ..., separated by blank lines)
    const blocks = text.split(/\n\s*\n/).filter(b => b.trim());

    for (const block of blocks) {

        const roleMatch = block.match(/\brole\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/i);

        description: text.trim()
      });

  _parseWorldRulesFile(text) {
    const rules = [];

    // Split by headings
    const sections = text.split(/(?=^#{1,3}\s+.+$)/gm).filter(s => s.trim());

    if (sections.length > 1 || (sections.length === 1 && /^#{1,3}\s+/.test(sections[0]))) {
      for (const section of sections) {
        const lines = section.trim().split('\n');
        const title = lines[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();
        if (!title) continue;

        const description = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim();
        rules.push({
          title,
          description,
          category: this._guessWorldRuleCategory(title, description)
        });
      }
      return rules;
    }

    // Split by numbered items or bullet sections
    const blocks = text.split(/\n\s*\n/).filter(b => b.trim());

    for (const block of blocks) {
      const firstLine = block.trim().split('\n')[0].replace(/^[\d\.\)\-\*•]+\s*/, '').trim();
      if (firstLine.length > 0 && firstLine.length < 100) {
        rules.push({
          title: firstLine,
          description: block.trim(),
          category: this._guessWorldRuleCategory(firstLine, block)
        });
      }
    }

    if (rules.length === 0) {
      rules.push({
        title: 'Imported World Rules',
        description: text.trim(),
        category: 'general'
      });
    }

    return rules;
  }

  _parseBeatMapFile(text) {
    const beats = [];

    // Try numbered list
    const numberedPattern = /^\s*(\d+)[\.\)]\s*(.+)/gm;
    let match;
    while ((match = numberedPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      beats.push({
        order: parseInt(match[1], 10),
        title: match[2].trim(),
        description: '',
        type: 'beat'
      });
    }

    if (beats.length > 0) return beats;

    // Try bullet list
    const bulletPattern = /^\s*[-*•]\s+(.+)/gm;
    let order = 0;
    while ((match = bulletPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      order++;
      beats.push({
        order,
        title: match[1].trim(),
        description: '',
        type: 'beat'
      });
    }

    if (beats.length > 0) return beats;

    // Try heading-based
    const sections = text.split(/(?=^#{1,3}\s+.+$)/gm).filter(s => s.trim());
    sections.forEach((section, i) => {
      const lines = section.trim().split('\n');
      const title = lines[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();
      const description = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim();
      if (title) {
        beats.push({
          order: i + 1,
          title,
          description,
          type: this._guessBeatType(title)
        });
      }
    });

    if (beats.length === 0) {
      beats.push({
        order: 1,
        title: 'Imported Beat Map',
        description: text.trim(),
        type: 'beat'
      });
    }

    return beats;
  }

  _extractChapterTitles(text) {
    const titles = [];
    const pattern = /^(?:#{1,3}\s+)?(?:chapter\s+\d+[:\s]*)(.*)/gmi;
    let match;
    while ((match = pattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      const title = match[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim();
      if (title) titles.push(title);
    }

    if (titles.length === 0) {
      const headingPattern = /^#{1,2}\s+(.+)$/gm;
      while ((match = headingPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
        titles.push(match[1].trim());
      }
    }

    return titles;
  }

  _extractChapterContent(text, title) {
    const escapedTitle = title.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^#{1,3}\\s+)?${escapedTitle}\\s*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n#{1,3}\\s+|$)`, 'mi');
    const match = text.match(pattern);
    return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
  }

  _extractCharacterNames(text) {
    const names = [];

    // From headings
    const headingPattern = /^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/gm;
    let match;
    while ((match = headingPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      names.push(match[1].trim());
    }

    // From Name: fields
    const nameFieldPattern = /\b(?:name|character)\s*[:—-]\s*(.+)/gi;
    while ((match = nameFieldPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      const name = match[1].trim();
      if (!names.includes(name)) names.push(name);
    }

    return names;
  }

  _extractCharacterBlock(text, name) {
    const escapedName = name.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^#{1,3}\\s+)?${escapedName}\\s*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n#{1,3}\\s+|$)`, 'mi');
    const match = text.match(pattern);
    return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
  }

  _extractRuleNames(text) {
    const names = [];
    const headingPattern = /^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/gm;
    let match;
    while ((match = headingPattern.exec(text)) !== null) {
      names.push(match[1].trim());
    }
    return names;
  }

  _guessWorldRuleCategory(title, description) {
    const combined = (title + ' ' + description).toLowerCase();
    if (/magic|spell|enchant|arcane|mana|power/i.test(combined)) return 'magic';
    if (/geography|terrain|land|continent|ocean|mountain|city|town/i.test(combined)) return 'geography';
    if (/history|timeline|era|epoch|war|battle|founding/i.test(combined)) return 'history';
    if (/culture|custom|tradition|ritual|festival|social/i.test(combined)) return 'culture';
    if (/species|race|creature|beast|monster|fauna|flora/i.test(combined)) return 'species';
    if (/technology|tech|invention|machine|device/i.test(combined)) return 'technology';
    if (/politic|government|kingdom|empire|republic|faction|guild/i.test(combined)) return 'politics';
    if (/religion|god|deity|faith|worship|temple|church/i.test(combined)) return 'religion';
    if (/economy|trade|currency|merchant|market/i.test(combined)) return 'economy';
    if (/language|tongue|dialect|script|writing/i.test(combined)) return 'language';
    return 'general';
  }

  _guessBeatType(title) {
    const t = title.toLowerCase();
    if (/opening|hook|beginning|start/i.test(t)) return 'opening';
    if (/inciting|catalyst|trigger/i.test(t)) return 'inciting_incident';
    if (/first.*plot|plot.*point.*1|turning.*point.*1/i.test(t)) return 'plot_point_1';
    if (/midpoint|mid.*point|middle/i.test(t)) return 'midpoint';
    if (/second.*plot|plot.*point.*2|turning.*point.*2/i.test(t)) return 'plot_point_2';
    if (/climax|crisis|final.*battle/i.test(t)) return 'climax';
    if (/resolution|denouement|ending|conclusion/i.test(t)) return 'resolution';
    if (/pinch/i.test(t)) return 'pinch_point';
    return 'beat';
  }

  _calculateTotalSteps(files, options) {
    let steps = files.length; // One step per file import
    if (options.extractCharacters) steps++;
    if (options.extractWorldRules) steps++;
    if (options.deriveBeatMap) steps++;
    if (options.buildSceneInventory) steps++;
    if (options.checkContinuity) steps++;
    steps++; // Final "complete" step
    return steps;
  }

  // ---- Private: AI Analysis Methods ----

    const truncated = manuscriptText.length > maxChars

      : manuscriptText;

- traits: Key personality traits observed
- relationships: Any relationships with other characters

Return your response as a JSON array of character objects.

MANUSCRIPT:

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON array. No other text.`;

    const response = await this.llm.generate(prompt, {
      temperature: 0.3,
      maxTokens

  async _aiBuildSceneInventory(projectId, manuscriptText) {
    const maxChars = 30000;
    const truncated = manuscriptText.length > maxChars
      ? manuscriptText.substring(0, maxChars) + '\n\n[... truncated for analysis ...]'

- sceneNumber: Sequential number
- title: A brief descriptive title
- setting: Where the scene takes place
- timeOfDay: When it occurs (if discernible)
- characters: Array of character names present
- summary: A 1-2 sentence summary of what happens
- purpose: The narrative purpose of this scene (e.g., "introduces conflict", "character development", "advances plot")
- wordCountEstimate: Rough estimate of the scene's length in words

Return your response as a JSON array of scene objects.

MANUSCRIPT:
${truncated}

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON array. No other text.`;

    const response = await this.llm.generate(prompt, {
      temperature: 0.3,
      maxTokens: 4000
    });

  async _aiCheckContinuity(projectId, manuscriptText) {
    const maxChars = 30000;
    const truncated = manuscriptText.length > maxChars
      ? manuscriptText.substring(0, maxChars) + '\n\n[... truncated for analysis ...]'
      : manuscriptText;

    // Also load existing world rules and characters for cross-reference
    let contextBlock = '';
    try {
      const characters = await this.storage.getCharacters(projectId) || [];
      const worldRules = await this.storage.getWorldRules(projectId) || [];

      if (characters.length > 0) {
        contextBlock += '\n\nESTABLISHED CHARACTERS:\n';
        for (const c of characters.slice(0, 30)) {
          contextBlock += `- ${c.name}: ${(c.description || '').substring(0, 100)}\n`;
        }
      }

      if (worldRules.length > 0) {
        contextBlock += '\n\nESTABLISHED WORLD RULES:\n';
        for (const r of worldRules.slice(0, 30)) {
          contextBlock += `- ${r.title}: ${(r.description || '').substring(0, 100)}\n`;
        }
      }
    } catch (e) {
      // Non-critical, continue without context
    }

    const prompt = `Analyze the following manuscript for continuity issues, contradictions, and inconsistencies. Look for:
- Character inconsistencies (name spelling changes, contradictory descriptions, out-of-character behavior)
- Timeline problems (events out of order, impossible timing)
- Setting contradictions (locations described differently)
- World rule violations (if established rules are provided below)
- Plot holes or logical inconsistencies
- Factual contradictions within the text

For each issue found, provide:
- severity: "high", "medium", or "low"
- type: The category of issue (character, timeline, setting, world_rule, plot_hole, factual)
- description: What the issue is
- location: Where approximately in the text this occurs
- suggestion: How it might be fixed
${contextBlock}

MANUSCRIPT:
${truncated}

Return your response as a JSON array of issue objects. If no issues are found, return an empty array [].
Respond ONLY with a valid JSON array. No other text.`;

    const response = await this.llm.generate(prompt, {
      temperature: 0.3,
      maxTokens: 4000
    });

    try {
      const cleaned = response.replace(/```json\n?/g, '').replace(/```\n?/g, '').trim();
      const issues = JSON.parse(cleaned);

      if (!Array.isArray(issues)) {
        throw new Error('Expected JSON array');
      }

      return issues;
    } catch (err) {
      throw new Error(`Failed to parse AI continuity check: ${err.message}`);
    }
  }
}

// src/components/import/ImportWizard.jsx
import React, { useState, useCallback } from 'react';
import FileUploadStep from './FileUploadStep';
import FileClassificationStep from './FileClassificationStep';
import ConflictResolutionStep from './ConflictResolutionStep';
import AnalysisOptionsStep from './AnalysisOptionsStep';
import ImportProgressStep from './ImportProgressStep';
import ImportReportStep from './ImportReportStep';

const STEPS = {
  UPLOAD: 0,
  CLASSIFY: 1,
  CONFLICTS: 2,
  ANALYSIS: 3,
  PROGRESS: 4,
  REPORT: 5
};

const STEP_LABELS = [
  'Upload Files',
  'Classify Files',
  'Resolve Conflicts',
  'Analysis Options',
  'Importing...',
  'Results'
];

export default function ImportWizard({ projectId, orchestrator, onComplete, onCancel }) {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(STEPS.UPLOAD);
  const [files, setFiles] = useState([]);
  const [classifications, setClassifications] = useState([]);
  const [conflicts, setConflicts] = useState([]);
  const [analysisOptions, setAnalysisOptions] = useState({
    extractCharacters: false,
    extractWorldRules: false,
    deriveBeatMap: false,
    buildSceneInventory: false,
    checkContinuity: false,
    autoSaveExtracted: true
  });
  const [progress, setProgress] = useState({ current: 0, total: 1, message: '' });
  const [report, setReport] = useState(null);
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);

  // Step 1: Files uploaded
  const handleFilesUploaded = useCallback(async (uploadedFiles) => {
    setFiles(uploadedFiles);
    setError(null);

    try {
      // Read file contents
      const readFiles = await Promise.all(
        uploadedFiles.map(async (file) => {
          const text = await readFileAsText(file);
          return {
            fileName: file.name,
            text,
            size: file.size,
            type: file.type || guessFileType(file.name)
          };
        })
      );

      // Classify files
      const classified = orchestrator.classifyFiles(readFiles);
      setClassifications(classified);
      setCurrentStep(STEPS.CLASSIFY);
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Failed to read files: ${err.message}`);
    }
  }, [orchestrator]);

  // Step 2: Classifications confirmed
  const handleClassificationsConfirmed = useCallback(async (confirmedClassifications) => {
    setClassifications(confirmedClassifications);
    setError(null);

    try {
      // Detect conflicts
      const detected = await orchestrator.detectConflicts(projectId, confirmedClassifications);

      if (detected.length > 0) {
        setConflicts(detected);
        setCurrentStep(STEPS.CONFLICTS);
      } else {
        // Skip conflict step, check if any manuscripts for analysis
        const hasManuscripts = confirmedClassifications.some(c => c.category === 'manuscript');
        if (hasManuscripts) {
          setCurrentStep(STEPS.ANALYSIS);
        } else {
          // Go straight to import
          startImport(confirmedClassifications, [], analysisOptions);
        }
      }
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Failed to detect conflicts: ${err.message}`);
    }
  }, [orchestrator, projectId, analysisOptions]);

  // Step 3: Conflicts resolved
  const handleConflictsResolved = useCallback((resolvedConflicts) => {
    setConflicts(resolvedConflicts);
    setError(null);

    const hasManuscripts = classifications.some(c => c.category === 'manuscript');
    if (hasManuscripts) {
      setCurrentStep(STEPS.ANALYSIS);
    } else {
      startImport(classifications, resolvedConflicts, analysisOptions);
    }
  }, [classifications, analysisOptions]);

  // Step 4: Analysis options confirmed
  const handleAnalysisConfirmed = useCallback((options) => {
    setAnalysisOptions(options);
    setError(null);
    startImport(classifications, conflicts, options);
  }, [classifications, conflicts]);

  // Start the import
  const startImport = useCallback(async (classifiedFiles, resolvedConflicts, options) => {
    setCurrentStep(STEPS.PROGRESS);
    setError(null);

    try {
      const importOptions = {
        ...options,
        conflicts: resolvedConflicts
      };

      const result = await orchestrator.executeImport(
        projectId,
        classifiedFiles,
        importOptions,
        (progressData) => {
          setProgress(progressData);
        }
      );

      setReport(result);
      setCurrentStep(STEPS.REPORT);
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Import failed: ${err.message}`);
      setCurrentStep(STEPS.REPORT);
      setReport({
        chaptersImported: 0,
        charactersImported: 0,
        worldRulesImported: 0,
        beatsImported: 0,
        notesImported: 0,
        skipped: 0,
        errors: [err.message],
        analysisResults: {}
      });
    }
  }, [orchestrator, projectId]);

  const handleBack = useCallback(() => {
    if (currentStep > STEPS.UPLOAD && currentStep < STEPS.PROGRESS) {
      // Skip conflict step on back if no conflicts
      if (currentStep === STEPS.ANALYSIS && conflicts.length === 0) {
        setCurrentStep(STEPS.CLASSIFY);
      } else {
        setCurrentStep(currentStep - 1);

---

      } else {

  }, [currentStep, conflicts]);

  const handleDone = useCallback(() => {
    if (onComplete) onComplete(report);
  }, [onComplete, report]);

  return (
    <div className="import-wizard">
      <div className="import-wizard__header">
        <h2>Import Files</h2>
        <StepIndicator
          steps={STEP_LABELS}
          currentStep={currentStep}
        />
      </div>

      {error && (
        <div className="import-wizard__error">
          <span className="import-wizard__error-icon">⚠️</span>
          <span>{error}</span>
          <button onClick={() => setError(null)}>Dismiss</button>
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="import-wizard__body">
        {currentStep === STEPS.UPLOAD && (
          <FileUploadStep
            onFilesSelected={handleFilesUploaded}
            onCancel={onCancel}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === STEPS.CLASSIFY && (
          <FileClassificationStep
            classifications={classifications}
            onConfirm={handleClassificationsConfirmed}
            onBack={() => setCurrentStep(STEPS.UPLOAD)}
            onCancel={onCancel}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === STEPS.CONFLICTS && (
          <ConflictResolutionStep
            conflicts={conflicts}
            onResolved={handleConflictsResolved}
            onBack={() => setCurrentStep(STEPS.CLASSIFY)}
            onCancel={onCancel}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === STEPS.ANALYSIS && (
          <AnalysisOptionsStep
            options={analysisOptions}
            onConfirm={handleAnalysisConfirmed}
            onBack={handleBack}
            onCancel={onCancel}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === STEPS.PROGRESS && (
          <ImportProgressStep
            progress={progress}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === STEPS.REPORT && (
          <ImportReportStep
            report={report}
            onDone={handleDone}
          />
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

function StepIndicator({ steps, currentStep }) {
  return (
    <div className="step-indicator">
      {steps.map((label, i) => (
        <div
          key={i}
          className={`step-indicator__step ${
            i === currentStep ? 'step-indicator__step--active' :
            i < currentStep ? 'step-indicator__step--completed' :
            'step-indicator__step--pending'
          }`}
        >
          <div className="step-indicator__dot">
            {i < currentStep ? '✓' : i + 1}
          </div>
          <div className="step-indicator__label">{label}</div>
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

function readFileAsText(file) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const reader = new FileReader();
    reader.onload = () => resolve(reader.result);
    reader.onerror = () => reject(new Error(`Failed to read ${file.name}`));
    reader.readAsText(file);
  });
}

function guessFileType(fileName) {
  const ext = fileName.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
  const typeMap = {
    txt: 'text/plain',
    md: 'text/markdown',
    markdown: 'text/markdown',
    doc: 'application/msword',
    docx: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
    rtf: 'application/rtf',
    json: 'application/json',
    csv: 'text/csv'
  };
  return typeMap[ext] || 'text/plain';
}
```

// src/components/import/FileUploadStep.jsx
import React, { useState, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';

const ACCEPTED_EXTENSIONS = ['.txt', '.md', '.markdown', '.rtf', '.doc', '.docx', '.json', '.csv'];
const MAX_FILE_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB
const MAX_FILES = 50;

export default function FileUploadStep({ onFilesSelected, onCancel }) {
  const [dragActive, setDragActive] = useState(false);
  const [selectedFiles, setSelectedFiles] = useState([]);
  const [validationErrors, setValidationErrors] = useState([]);
  const fileInputRef = useRef(null);

  const validateFiles = useCallback((fileList) => {
    const errors = [];
    const valid = [];

    if (fileList.length > MAX_FILES) {
      errors.push(`Too many files. Maximum is ${MAX_FILES}.`);
      return { valid: [], errors };
    }

    for (const file of fileList) {
      const ext = '.' + file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();

      if (!ACCEPTED_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext)) {
        errors.push(`"${file.name}" has unsupported format (${ext}). Supported: ${ACCEPTED_EXTENSIONS.join(', ')}`);
        continue;
      }

      if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
        errors.push(`"${file.name}" is too large (${(file.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)}MB). Maximum is 10MB.`);
        continue;
      }

      if (file.size === 0) {
        errors.push(`"${file.name}" is empty.`);
        continue;
      }

      valid.push(file);
    }

    return { valid,

    // Merge with existing, avoiding duplicates by name
    setSelectedFiles(prev => {

  const handleDrag = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    if (e.type === 'dragenter' || e.type === 'dragover') {

    } else if (e.type === 'dragleave') {
      setDragActive(false);

  }, []);

  const handleDrop = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    setDragActive(false);

    if (e.dataTransfer.files && e.dataTransfer.files.length > 0) {
      handleFiles(e.dataTransfer.files);
    }
  }, [handleFiles]);

  const handleInputChange = useCallback((e) => {
    if (e.target.files && e.target.files.length > 0) {
      handleFiles(e.target.files);
    }
  }, [handleFiles]);

  const removeFile = useCallback((index) => {
    setSelectedFiles(prev => prev.filter((_, i) => i !== index));
  }, []);

  const handleContinue = useCallback(() => {
    if (selectedFiles.length > 0) {
      onFilesSelected(selectedFiles);
    }
  }, [selectedFiles, onFilesSelected]);

  const formatSize = (bytes) => {
    if (bytes < 1024) return `${bytes} B`;
    if (bytes < 1024 * 1024) return `${(bytes / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`;
    return `${(bytes / (1024 * 1024)).toFixed(1)} MB`;
  };

  return (
    <div className="file-upload-step">
      <div
        className={`file-upload-step__dropzone ${dragActive ? 'file-upload-step__dropzone--active' : ''}`}
        onDragEnter={handleDrag}
        onDragLeave={handleDrag}
        onDragOver={handleDrag}
        onDrop={handleDrop}
        onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()}
      >
        <div className="file-upload-step__dropzone-content">
          <span className="file-upload-step__icon">📁</span>
          <p className="file-upload-step__primary-text">
            Drag & drop files here, or click to browse
          </p>
          <p className="file-upload-step__secondary-text">
            Supports: {ACCEPTED_EXTENSIONS.join(', ')} — Max {MAX_FILES} files, 10MB each
          </p>
        </div>
        <input
          ref={fileInputRef}
          type="file"
          multiple
          accept={ACCEPTED_EXTENSIONS.join(',')}
          onChange={handleInputChange}
          style={{ display: 'none' }}
        />
      </div>

      {validationErrors.length > 0 && (
        <div className="file-upload-step__errors">
          {validationErrors.map((err, i) => (
            <div key={i} className="file-upload-step__error">⚠️ {err}</div>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {selectedFiles.length > 0 && (
        <div className="file-upload-step__file-list">
          <h3>{selectedFiles.length} file{selectedFiles.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} selected</h3>
          <ul>
            {selectedFiles.map((file, i) => (
              <li key={i} className="file-upload-step__file-item">
                <span className="file-upload-step__file-name">{file.name}</span>
                <span className="file-upload-step__file-size">{formatSize(file.size)}</span>
                <button
                  className="file-upload-step__remove-btn"
                  onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); removeFile(i); }}
                  title="Remove file"
                >
                  ✕
                </button>
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="import-wizard__actions">
        <button className="btn btn--secondary" onClick={onCancel}>Cancel</button>
        <button
          className="btn btn--primary"
          onClick={handleContinue}
          disabled={selectedFiles.length === 0}
        >
          Continue →
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

import React, { useState, useCallback } from 'react';

const CATEGORIES = [
  { value: 'manuscript', label: 'Manuscript / Chapter(s)', icon: '📖' },
  { value: 'characters', label: 'Character Notes', icon: '👤' },
  { value: 'worldRules', label: 'World Rules / Lore', icon: '🌍' },
