  };

  const handleNext = async () => {
    if (currentStep === 0) {
      await handleProcessFiles();
    } else if (currentStep === 2) {
      if (runAnalysis) {
        setCurrentStep(3);
      } else {
        await handleApply();
      }
    } else if (currentStep === 3) {
      await handleApply();
    } else {
      setCurrentStep(prev => Math.min(prev + 1, STEPS.length - 1));
    }
  };

  const handleBack = () => {
    setCurrentStep(prev => Math.max(prev - 1, 0));
  };

  return (
    <div className="import-wizard">
      <div className="import-wizard__header">
        <h2>Import Files</h2>
        <button className="import-wizard__close" onClick={onCancel} aria-label="Close">
          ✕
        </button>
      </div>

      <div className="import-wizard__stepper">
        {STEPS.map((step, index) => (
          <div
            key={step.id}
            className={`import-wizard__step ${
              index === currentStep ? 'import-wizard__step--active' : ''
            } ${index < currentStep ? 'import-wizard__step--completed' : ''}`}
          >
            <span className="import-wizard__step-icon">{step.icon}</span>
            <span className="import-wizard__step-label">{step.label}</span>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>

      {error && (
        <div className="import-wizard__error">
          <span>⚠️ {error}</span>
          <button onClick={() => setError(null)}>Dismiss</button>
        </div>
      )}

      {isProcessing && (
        <ImportProgress
          stage={progress.stage}
          percent={progress.percent}
          message={progress.message}
        />
      )}

      <div className="import-wizard__content">
        {currentStep === 0 && (
          <FileUploadStep
            files={files}
            onFilesSelected={handleFilesSelected}
            disabled={isProcessing}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === 1 && importResult && (
          <ClassificationReviewStep
            classifications={importResult.classifications}
            onClassificationChange={handleClassificationChange}
            summary={importResult.summary}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === 2 && importResult && (
          <ConflictResolutionStep
            conflicts={importResult.conflicts}
            resolutions={resolutions}
            onResolutionChange={handleResolutionChange}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === 3 && (
          <AnalysisStep
            importResult={importResult}
            analysisOptions={analysisOptions}
            onAnalysisOptionsChange={setAnalysisOptions}
            runAnalysis={runAnalysis}
            onRunAnalysisChange={setRunAnalysis}
            onRunAnalysis={handleRunAnalysis}
            isProcessing={isProcessing}
          />
        )}

        {currentStep === 4 && (
          <ReviewApplyStep
            importResult={importResult}
            appliedResult={appliedResult}
            onComplete={handleComplete}
          />
        )}
      </div>

      <div className="import-wizard__footer">
        <button
          className="import-wizard__btn import-wizard__btn--secondary"
          onClick={currentStep === 0 ? onCancel : handleBack}
          disabled={isProcessing}
        >
          {currentStep === 0 ? 'Cancel' : 'Back'}
        </button>

        {currentStep < STEPS.length - 1 ? (
          <button
            className="import-wizard__btn import-wizard__btn--primary"
            onClick={handleNext}
            disabled={!canProceed() || isProcessing}
          >
            {currentStep === 0 ? 'Process Files' :
             currentStep === 3 ? 'Apply Import' : 'Next'}
          </button>
        ) : (
          <button
            className="import-wizard__btn import-wizard__btn--primary"
            onClick={handleComplete}
            disabled={!appliedResult}
          >
            Done
          </button>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

// src/components/ImportWizard/FileUploadStep.jsx

import React, { useCallback, useRef } from 'react';

const ACCEPTED_TYPES = [
  '.txt', '.md', '.markdown',
  '.doc', '.docx',
  '.json', '.yaml', '.yml',
  '.rtf', '.odt', '.epub'
];

const MAX_FILE_SIZE = 50 * 1024 * 1024; // 50MB

export default function FileUploadStep({ files, onFilesSelected, disabled }) {
  const fileInputRef = useRef(null);
  const dropZoneRef = useRef(null);

  const validateFiles = (fileList) => {
    const valid = [];
    const errors = [];

    for (const file of fileList) {
      const ext = '.' + file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();

      if (!ACCEPTED_TYPES.includes(ext)) {
        errors.push(`${file.name}: Unsupported file type (${ext})`);
        continue;
      }

      if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
        errors.push(`${file.name}: File too large (${(file.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)}MB, max 50MB)`);
        continue;
      }

      if (file.size === 0) {
        errors.push(`${file.name}: File is empty`);
        continue;
      }

      valid.push(file);
    }

    if (errors

---

```jsx
    if (errors.length > 0) {
      alert('Some files were skipped:\n\n' + errors.join('\n'));
    }

    return valid;
  };

  const handleFileChange = useCallback((e) => {
    const fileList = Array.from(e.target.files);
    const valid = validateFiles(fileList);
    onFilesSelected([...files, ...valid]);
  }, [files, onFilesSelected]);

  const handleDrop = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    dropZoneRef.current?.classList.remove('file-upload__drop-zone--active');

    const fileList = Array.from(e.dataTransfer.files);
    const valid = validateFiles(fileList);
    onFilesSelected([...files, ...valid]);
  }, [files, onFilesSelected]);

  const handleDragOver = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    dropZoneRef.current?.classList.add('file-upload__drop-zone--active');
  }, []);

  const handleDragLeave = useCallback((e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    dropZoneRef.current?.classList.remove('file-upload__drop-zone--active');
  }, []);

  const handleRemoveFile = useCallback((index) => {
    onFilesSelected(files.filter((_, i) => i !== index));
  }, [files, onFilesSelected]);

  const formatFileSize = (bytes) => {
    if (bytes < 1024) return bytes + ' B';
    if (bytes < 1024 * 1024) return (bytes / 1024).toFixed(1) + ' KB';
    return (bytes / (1024 * 1024)).toFixed(1) + ' MB';
  };

  const getFileIcon = (fileName) => {
    const ext = fileName.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
    const icons = {
      txt: '📄', md: '📝', markdown: '📝',
      doc: '📘', docx: '📘',
      json: '📋', yaml: '📋', yml: '📋',
      rtf: '📃', odt: '📃', epub: '📚'
    };
    return icons[ext] || '📄';
  };

  return (
    <div className="file-upload">
      <div
        ref={dropZoneRef}
        className="file-upload__drop-zone"
        onDrop={handleDrop}
        onDragOver={handleDragOver}
        onDragLeave={handleDragLeave}
        onClick={() => !disabled && fileInputRef.current?.click()}
        role="button"
        tabIndex={0}
        aria-label="Drop files here or click to browse"
      >
        <div className="file-upload__drop-icon">📁</div>
        <p className="file-upload__drop-text">
          Drag & drop files here, or click to browse
        </p>
        <p className="file-upload__drop-hint">
          Supports: TXT, MD, DOCX, JSON, YAML, RTF, ODT, EPUB (max 50MB each)
        </p>
      </div>

      <input
        ref={fileInputRef}
        type="file"
        multiple
        accept={ACCEPTED_TYPES.join(',')}
        onChange={handleFileChange}
        className="file-upload__input"
        disabled={disabled}
        style={{ display: 'none' }}
      />

      {files.length > 0 && (
        <div className="file-upload__file-list">
          <h3>Selected Files ({files.length})</h3>
          <ul>
            {files.map((file, index) => (
              <li key={`${file.name}-${index}`} className="file-upload__file-item">
                <span className="file-upload__file-icon">{getFileIcon(file.name)}</span>
                <span className="file-upload__file-name">{file.name}</span>
                <span className="file-upload__file-size">{formatFileSize(file.size)}</span>
                <button
                  className="file-upload__file-remove"
                  onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); handleRemoveFile(index); }}
                  disabled={disabled}
                  aria-label={`Remove ${file.name}`}
                >
                  ✕
                </button>
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

];

        <div className="classification-review__stats">

          {summary.failedToParse > 0 && (

                  {(classification.fileSize / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB
                </span>
              </div>

                <label htmlFor={`cat-${classification.fileName}`}>Category:</label>
                <select
                  id={`cat-${classification.fileName}`}
                  value={classification.category}
                  onChange={(e) => onClassificationChange(classification.fileName, e.target.value)}
                >
                  {CATEGORIES.map(cat => (
                    <option key={cat.value} value={cat.value}>{cat.label}</option>
                  ))}
                </select>
              </div>

              {classification.signals && classification.signals.length > 0 && (
                <div className="classification-review__signals">
                  <span className="classification-review__signals-label">Detected signals:</span>
                  {classification.signals.map((signal, i) => (
                    <span key={i} className="classification-review__signal-tag">
                      {signal}
                    </span>
                  ))}
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

// src/components/ImportWizard/ConflictResolutionStep.jsx

import React from 'react';

const RESOLUTION_OPTIONS = [
  { value: 'merge', label: 'Merge', description: 'Combine imported data with existing data' },
  { value: 'replace', label: 'Replace', description: 'Overwrite existing data with imported data' },
  { value: 'skip', label: 'Skip', description: 'Keep existing data, ignore imported data' },
  { value: 'duplicate', label: 'Keep Both', description: 'Create a new entry alongside existing' }
];

export default function ConflictResolutionStep({
  conflicts,
  resolutions,
  onResolutionChange
}) {
  if (conflicts.length === 0) {
    return (
      <div className="conflict-resolution">
        <div className="conflict-resolution__empty">
          <span className="conflict-resolution__empty-icon">✅</span>
          <h3>No Conflicts Detected</h3>
          <p>All imported data is new and doesn't conflict with existing project data.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  const criticalConflicts = conflicts.filter(c => c.severity === 'critical');
  const warningConflicts = conflicts.filter(c => c.severity === 'warning');
  const infoConflicts = conflicts.filter(c => c.severity === 'info');

  return (
    <div className="conflict-resolution">
      <h3>Resolve Conflicts ({conflicts.length})</h3>
      <p className="conflict-resolution__hint">
        Some imported data conflicts with existing project data. Choose how to handle each conflict.
      </p>

      {criticalConflicts.length > 0 && (
        <div className="conflict-resolution__section">
          <h4 className="conflict-resolution__section-title conflict-resolution__section-title--critical">
            🔴 Critical ({criticalConflicts.length})
          </h4>
          {criticalConflicts.map(conflict => (
            <ConflictCard
              key={conflict.id}
              conflict={conflict}
              resolution={resolutions[conflict.id]}
              onResolutionChange={onResolutionChange}
            />
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {warningConflicts.length > 0 && (
        <div className="conflict-resolution__section">
          <h4 className="conflict-resolution__section-title conflict-resolution__section-title--warning">
            🟡 Warnings ({warningConflicts.length})
          </h4>
          {warningConflicts.map(conflict => (
            <ConflictCard
              key={conflict.id}
              conflict={conflict}
              resolution={resolutions[conflict.id]}
              onResolutionChange={onResolutionChange}
            />
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      {

      {infoConflicts.length > 0 && (
        <div className="conflict-resolution__section">
          <h4 className="conflict-resolution__section-title conflict-resolution__section-title--info">
            🔵 Info ({infoConflicts.length})
          </h4>
          {infoConflicts.map(conflict => (
            <ConflictCard
              key={conflict.id}
              conflict={conflict}
              resolution={resolutions[conflict.id]}
              onResolutionChange={onResolutionChange}
            />
          ))}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="conflict-resolution__bulk-actions">
        <h4>Bulk Actions</h4>
        <button
          onClick={() => conflicts.forEach(c => onResolutionChange(c.id, 'merge'))}
          className="conflict-resolution__bulk-btn"
        >
          Merge All
        </button>
        <button
          onClick={() => conflicts.forEach(c => onResolutionChange(c.id, 'replace'))}
          className="conflict-resolution__bulk-btn"
        >
          Replace All
        </button>
        <button
          onClick={() => conflicts.forEach(c => onResolutionChange(c.id, 'skip'))}
          className="conflict-resolution__bulk-btn"
        >
          Skip All
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

function ConflictCard({ conflict, resolution, onResolutionChange }) {
  return (
    <div className={`conflict-card conflict-card--${conflict.severity}`}>
      <div className="conflict-card__header">
        <span className="conflict-card__type">{conflict.type}</span>
        <span className="conflict-card__file">{conflict.fileName}</span>
      </div>

      <p className="conflict-card__description">{conflict.description}</p>

      {conflict.existingValue && (
        <div className="conflict-card__comparison">
          <div className="conflict-card__existing">
            <label>Existing:</label>
            <pre>{typeof conflict.existingValue === 'string'
              ? conflict.existingValue.substring(0, 200)
              : JSON.stringify(conflict.existingValue, null, 2).substring(0, 200)
            }</pre>
          </div>
          <div className="conflict-card__incoming">
            <label>Incoming:</label>
            <pre>{typeof conflict.incomingValue === 'string'
              ? conflict.incomingValue.substring(0, 200)
              : JSON.stringify(conflict.incomingValue, null, 2).substring(0, 200)
            }</pre>
          </div>
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="conflict-card__resolution">
        <label>Resolution:</label>
        <div className="conflict-card__resolution-options">
          {RESOLUTION_OPTIONS.map(option => (
            <label
              key={option.value}
              className={`conflict-card__resolution-option ${
                resolution?.resolution === option.value ? 'conflict-card__resolution-option--selected' : ''
              }`}
            >
              <input
                type="radio"
                name={`resolution-${conflict.id}`}
                value={option.value}
                checked={resolution?.resolution === option.value}
                onChange={() => onResolutionChange(conflict.id, option.value)}
              />
              <span className="conflict-card__resolution-label">{option.label}</span>
              <span className="conflict-card__resolution-desc">{option.description}</span>
            </label>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

// src/components/ImportWizard/AnalysisStep.jsx

import React from 'react';

export default function AnalysisStep({
  importResult,
  analysisOptions,
  onAnalysisOptionsChange,
  runAnalysis,
  onRunAnalysisChange,
  onRunAnalysis,
  isProcessing
}) {
  const manuscriptFiles = importResult?.classifications?.filter(c => c.category === 'manuscript') || [];
  const hasManuscript = manuscriptFiles.length > 0;

  const toggleOption = (key) => {
    onAnalysisOptionsChange(prev => ({
      ...prev,
      [key]: !prev[key]
    }));
  };

  return (
    <div className="analysis-step">
      <h3>AI Analysis (Optional)</h3>

      {!hasManuscript ? (
        <div className="analysis-step__no-manuscript">
          <p>No manuscript content was detected in the imported files. Analysis requires manuscript text.</p>
          <p>You can skip this step and proceed to apply the import.</p>
        </div>
      ) : (
        <>
          <div className="analysis-step__toggle">
            <label>
              <input
                type="checkbox"
                checked={runAnalysis}
                onChange={(e) => onRunAnalysisChange(e.target.checked)}
              />
              Run AI analysis on imported manuscript
            </label>
            <p className="analysis-step__toggle-hint">
              Analyze {manuscriptFiles.length} manuscript file(s) to extract characters, world rules, and story structure.
            </p>
          </div>

          {runAnalysis && (
            <div className="analysis-step__options">
              <h4>Analysis Options</h4>

              <label className="analysis-step__option">
                <input
                  type="checkbox"
                  checked={analysisOptions.extractCharacters}
                  onChange={() => toggleOption('extractCharacters')}
                />
                <div>
                  <span className="analysis-step__option-label">👤 Extract Characters</span>
                  <span className="analysis-step__option-desc">
                    Identify characters, their traits, and relationships

                <div>

                  </span>

              <label className="analysis-step__option">
                <input

                  checked={analysisOptions.checkContinuity}
                  onChange={() => toggleOption('checkContinuity')}
                />
                <div>
                  <span className="analysis-step__option-label">🔗 Check Continuity</span>
                  <span className="analysis-step__option-desc">
                    Flag potential continuity errors and inconsistencies
                  </span>
                </div>
              </label>

              <button
                className="analysis-step__run-btn"
                onClick={onRunAnalysis}
                disabled={isProcessing}
              >
                {isProcessing ? 'Analyzing...' : '🔍 Run Analysis'}
              </button>
            </div>
          )}

          {importResult?.analysisResults && (
            <div className="analysis-step__results">

                <div className="analysis-step__result-section">
                  <h5>👤 Characters Found ({importResult.analysisResults.characters.length})</h5>
                  <ul>
                    {importResult.analysisResults.characters.map((char, i) => (
                      <li key={i}>
                        <strong>{char.name}</strong>
                        {char.role && <span className="analysis-step__tag">{char.role}</span>}
                        {char.description && <p>{char.description}</p>}
                      </li>
                    ))}
                  </ul>
                </div>
              )}

              {importResult.analysisResults.worldRules && (
                <div className="analysis-step__result-section">
                  <h5>🌍 World Rules ({importResult.analysisResults.worldRules.length})</h5>
                  <ul>
                    {importResult.analysisResults.worldRules.map((rule, i) => (
                      <li key={i}>
                        <strong>{rule.title || rule.name}</strong>
                        <p>{rule.description || rule.content}</p>
                      </li>
                    ))}
                  </ul>
                </div>
              )}

              {importResult.analysisResults.beats && (
                <div className="analysis-step__result-section">
                  <h5>🗺️ Story Beats ({importResult.analysisResults.beats.length})</h5>
                  <ol>
                    {importResult.analysisResults.beats.map((beat, i) => (
                      <li key={i}>
                        <strong>{beat.title}</strong>
                        {beat.summary && <p>{beat.summary}</p>}
                      </li>
                    ))}
                  </ol>
                </div>
              )}

              {importResult.analysisResults.continuityIssues && 
               importResult.analysisResults.continuityIssues.length > 0 && (
                <div className="analysis-step__result-section">
                  <h5>⚠️ Continuity Issues ({importResult.analysisResults.continuityIssues.length})</h5>
                  <ul>
                    {importResult.analysisResults.continuityIssues.map((issue, i) => (
                      <li key={i} className="analysis-step__issue">
                        <span className={`analysis-step__issue-severity analysis-step__issue-severity--${issue.severity}`}>
                          {issue.severity}
                        </span>
                        <p>{issue.description}</p>
                        {issue.location && <span className="analysis-step__issue-location">{issue.location}</span>}
                      </li>
                    ))}
                  </ul>
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          )}
        </>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

        <h3>Import Complete!</h3>
      </div>

      <div className="review-apply__summary">
        <h4>What was imported:</h4>

            <div className="review-apply__stat">
              <span className="review-apply__stat-icon">🗺️</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-value">{summary.beatsAdded}</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-label">Story beats added</span>
            </div>
          )}

          {summary.conflictsResolved > 0 && (
            <div className="review-apply__stat">
              <span className="review-apply__stat-icon">✅</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-value">{summary.conflictsResolved}</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-label">Conflicts resolved</span>
            </div>
          )}

          {summary.filesSkipped > 0 && (
            <div className="review-apply__stat">
              <span className="review-apply__stat-icon">⏭️</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-value">{summary.filesSkipped}</span>
              <span className="review-apply__stat-label">Files skipped</span>
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>

      {appliedResult.warnings && appliedResult.warnings.length > 0 && (
        <div className="review-apply__warnings">
          <h4>⚠️ Warnings</h4>
          <ul>
            {appliedResult.warnings.map((warning, i) => (
              <li key={i}>{warning}</li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        </div>
      )}

      {appliedResult.changelog && appliedResult.changelog.length > 0 && (
        <div className="review-apply__changelog">
          <h4>Change Log</h4>
          <ul className="review-apply__changelog-list">
            {appliedResult.changelog.map((entry, i) => (
              <li key={i} className={`review-apply__changelog-entry review-apply__changelog-entry--${entry.type}`}>
                <span className="review-apply__changelog-action">{entry.action}</span>
                <span className="review-apply__changelog-target">{entry.target}</span>
                {entry.detail && <span className="review-apply__changelog-detail">{entry.detail}</span>}
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="review-apply__actions">
        <button
          className="review-apply__done-btn"
          onClick={onComplete}
        >
          Done — Go to Project
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```js
// src/services/importService.js

import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid';

export const CATEGORY_LABELS = {
  manuscript: '📖 Manuscript',
  characters: '👤 Characters',
  worldRules: '🌍 World Rules',
  beatMap: '🗺️ Beat Map',
  notes: '📝 Notes',
  skip: '⏭️ Skip'
};

// ─── File Parsing ───────────────────────────────────────────────

async function readFileAsText(file) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const reader = new FileReader();
    reader.onload = (e) => resolve(e.target.result);
    reader.onerror = (e) => reject(new Error(`Failed to read ${file.name}: ${e.target.error}`));
    reader.readAsText(file);
  });
}

async function parseFile(file) {
  const ext = file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();

  try {
    let content;
    let metadata = {};

    switch (ext) {
      case 'txt':
      case 'md':
      case 'markdown':
      case 'rtf':
        content = await readFileAsText(file);
        if (ext === 'rtf') {
          content = stripRtf(content);
        }
        break;

      case 'json':
        const jsonText = await readFileAsText(file);
        const jsonData = JSON.parse(jsonText);
        content = jsonText;
        metadata = { parsed: jsonData, format: 'json' };
        break;

      case 'yaml':
      case 'yml':
        const yamlText = await readFileAsText(file);
        content = yamlText;
        metadata = { format: 'yaml', raw: yamlText };
        // Basic YAML parsing - in production would use js-yaml
        try {
          metadata.parsed = parseSimpleYaml(yamlText);
        } catch (e) {
          metadata.parseError = e.message;
        }
        break;

      case 'docx':
        content = await parseDocx(file);
        break;

      case 'odt':
        content = await parseOdt(file);
        break;

      case 'epub':
        content = await parseEpub(file);
        break;

      default:
        content = await readFileAsText(file);
    }

    return {
      fileName: file.name,
      fileSize: file.size,
      fileType: ext,
      content,
      metadata,
      success: true
    };
  } catch (error) {
    return {
      fileName: file.name,
      fileSize: file.size,
      fileType: ext,
      content: null,
      error: error.message,
      success: false
    };
  }
}

function stripRtf(rtf) {
  // Basic RTF stripping - removes RTF control words and groups
  if (!rtf) return '';
  let result = rtf
    .replace(/\\par[d]?/g, '\n')
    .replace(/\{\*?\\[^{}]+}|[{}]|\\[A-Za-z]+\n?(?:-?\d+)?[ ]?/g, '')
    .replace(/\\'[0-9a-fA-F]{2}/g, '')
    .trim();
  return result;
}

function parseSimpleYaml(text) {
  // Very basic YAML-like parser for simple key-value structures
  const result = {};
  const lines = text.split('\n');
  let currentKey = null;
  let currentList = null;

  for (const line of lines) {
    const trimmed = line.trim();
    if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;

    const kvMatch = trimmed.match(/^([^:]+):\s*(.*)$/);
    if (kvMatch) {
      const key = kvMatch[1].trim();
      const value = kvMatch[2].trim();
      if (value) {
        result[key] = value;
        currentKey = key;
        currentList = null;
      } else {
        result[key] = [];
        currentKey = key;
        currentList = result[key];
      }
    } else if (trimmed.startsWith('- ') && currentList) {
      currentList.push(trimmed.substring(2).trim());
    }
  }

  return result;
}

async function parseDocx(file) {
  // Use JSZip to extract docx content
  // In production, would use mammoth.js or similar
  try {
    const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
    // Simplified: try to extract text from document.xml
    const JSZip = (await import('jszip')).default;
    const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(arrayBuffer);
    const documentXml = await zip.file('word/document.xml')?.async('string');

    if (!documentXml) {
      throw new Error('No document.xml found in DOCX');
    }

    // Strip XML tags to get text content
    const text = documentXml
      .replace(/<w:p[^>]*>/g, '\n')
      .replace(/<w:tab\/>/g, '\t')
      .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')
      .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
      .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
      .replace(/&gt;/g, '>')
      .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
      .replace(/&apos;/

---

```js
      .replace(/&apos;/g, "'")
      .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
      .trim();

    return text;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to parse DOCX: ${error.message}`);
  }
}

async function parseOdt(file) {
  try {
    const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
    const JSZip = (await import('jszip')).default;
    const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(arrayBuffer);
    const contentXml = await zip.file('content.xml')?.async('string');

    if (!contentXml) {
      throw new Error('No content.xml found in ODT');
    }

    const text = contentXml
      .replace(/<text:p[^>]*>/g, '\n')
      .replace(/<text:tab\/>/g, '\t')
      .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')
      .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
      .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
      .replace(/&gt;/g, '>')
      .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
      .trim();

    return text;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to parse ODT: ${error.message}`);
  }
}

async function parseEpub(file) {
  try {
    const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
    const JSZip = (await import('jszip')).default;
    const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(arrayBuffer);

    // Find HTML/XHTML files in the epub
    const htmlFiles = [];
    zip.forEach((relativePath, zipEntry) => {
      if (relativePath.match(/\.(x?html?)$/i) && !relativePath.includes('toc')) {
        htmlFiles.push(zipEntry);
      }
    });

    // Sort by name to maintain order
    htmlFiles.sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));

    let fullText = '';
    for (const entry of htmlFiles) {
      const html = await entry.async('string');
      const text = html
        .replace(/<p[^>]*>/gi, '\n')
        .replace(/<br\s*\/?>/gi, '\n')
        .replace(/<h[1-6][^>]*>/gi, '\n\n')
        .replace(/<\/h[1-6]>/gi, '\n')
        .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')
        .replace(/&nbsp;/g, ' ')
        .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
        .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
        .replace(/&gt;/g, '>')
        .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
        .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
        .trim();

      if (text) {
        fullText += text + '\n\n---\n\n';
      }
    }

    return fullText.trim();
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to parse EPUB: ${error.message}`);
  }
}

// ─── Classification ─────────────────────────────────────────────

const CLASSIFICATION_PATTERNS = {
  manuscript: {
    signals: [
      { pattern: /^chapter\s+\d+/im, weight: 0.4, label: 'Chapter heading' },
      { pattern: /^part\s+(one|two|three|four|five|\d+)/im, weight: 0.3, label: 'Part heading' },
      { pattern: /said|whispered|shouted|replied|asked/gi, weight: 0.3, label: 'Dialogue tags' },
      { pattern: /"[^"]{10,}"/g, weight: 0.25, label: 'Quoted dialogue' },
      { pattern: /\b(he|she|they)\s+(walked|ran|looked|turned|felt|thought|knew|saw)\b/gi, weight: 0.2, label: 'Narrative action' },
      { pattern: /\n\n/g, weight: 0.1, label: 'Paragraph breaks' },
    ],
    minLength: 500,
    baseScore: 0.1
  },
  characters: {
    signals: [
      { pattern: /\b(character|protagonist|antagonist|name|age|appearance|personality|traits|backstory|motivation)\s*:/gi, weight: 0.4, label: 'Character fields' },
      { pattern: /\b(hair|eyes|height|build|skin)\b/gi, weight: 0.15, label: 'Physical descriptors' },
      { pattern: /\b(character\s*profile|character\s*sheet|dramatis\s*personae)\b/gi, weight: 0.5, label: 'Character document title' },
      { pattern: /\b(relationship|ally|enemy|friend|rival|mentor|love\s*interest)\b/gi, weight: 0.2, label: 'Relationship terms' },
    ],
    baseScore: 0.05
  },
  worldRules: {
    signals: [
      { pattern: /\b(world|universe|setting|lore|magic\s*system|technology|history|geography|culture|religion|politics|economy)\s*:/gi, weight: 0.35, label: 'World-building fields' },
      { pattern: /\b(rule|law|principle|constraint|limitation|exception)\s*\d*\s*:/gi, weight: 0.3, label: 'Rule definitions' },
      { pattern: /\b(world\s*bible|world\s*building|setting\s*guide|lore\s*document)\b/gi, weight: 0.5, label: 'World doc title' },
      { pattern: /\b(in\s*this\s

  },
  beatMap: {
    signals: [
      { pattern: /\b(act\s+[1-3IiIiIi]+|inciting\s*incident|climax|resolution|midpoint|turning\s*point|pinch\s*point)\b/gi, weight: 0.4, label: 'Story structure terms' },
      { pattern: /\b(beat|scene|sequence|plot\s*point|arc)\s*\d*\s*:/gi, weight: 0.35, label: 'Beat labels' },
      { pattern: /\b(outline|synopsis|structure|plot\s*outline|story\s*map|beat\s*sheet)\b/gi, weight: 0.4, label: 'Outline document title' },
      { pattern: /\b(setup|confrontation|resolution|denouement|exposition|rising\s*action|falling\s*action)\b/gi, weight: 0.25, label: 'Narrative structure' },
    ],

  },
  notes: {
    signals: [
      { pattern: /\b(TODO|FIXME|NOTE|IDEA|RESEARCH|REMINDER)\b/g, weight: 0.3, label: 'Note markers' },
      { pattern: /\b(notes?|ideas?|brainstorm|thoughts|scratch|draft\s*notes)\b/gi, weight: 0.25, label: 'Notes title' },
      { pattern: /^\s*[-*•]\s+/gm, weight: 0.15, label: 'Bullet points' },
      { pattern: /\?\s*$/gm, weight: 0.1, label: 'Questions' },
    ],
    baseScore: 0.05
  }
};

function classifyContent(parsedFile) {
  if (!parsedFile.success || !parsedFile.content) {
    return {
      ...parsedFile,
      category: 'skip',
      confidence: 0,
      signals: ['Failed to parse'],
      manuallyClassified: false
    };
  }

  const content = parsedFile.content;
  const scores = {};
  const detectedSignals = {};

  // Check if it's a structured data file (JSON/YAML)
  if (parsedFile.metadata?.parsed) {
    const parsed = parsedFile.metadata.parsed;
    const structureResult = classifyStructuredData(parsed, parsedFile.fileName);
    if (structureResult.confidence > 0.5) {
      return {
        ...parsedFile,
        ...structureResult,
        manuallyClassified: false
      };
    }
  }

  // Score each category
  for (const [category, config] of Object.entries(CLASSIFICATION_PATTERNS)) {
    let score = config.baseScore;
    const signals = [];

    for (const signal of config.signals) {
      const matches = content.match(signal.pattern);
      if (matches && matches.length > 0) {
        const matchCount = matches.length;
        const normalizedWeight = signal.weight * Math.min(matchCount / 3, 1);
        score += normalizedWeight;
        signals.push(`${signal.label} (${matchCount})`);
      }
    }

    // Length bonus for manuscript
    if (category === 'manuscript' && content.length > config.minLength) {
      const lengthBonus = Math.min(content.length / 10000, 0.2);
      score += lengthBonus;
    }

    // Filename hints
    const fileNameLower = parsedFile.fileName.toLowerCase();
    if (category === 'manuscript' && /\b(chapter|manuscript|draft|story|novel|book)\b/.test(fileNameLower)) {
      score += 0.3;
      signals.push('Filename match');
    }
    if (category === 'characters' && /\b(character|cast|people|person)\b/.test(fileNameLower)) {
      score += 0.3;
      signals.push('Filename match');
    }
    if (category === 'worldRules' && /\b(world|lore|setting|rules|magic|bible)\b/.test(fileNameLower)) {
      score += 0.3;
      signals.push('Filename match');
    }
    if (category === 'beatMap' && /\b(beat|outline|structure|plot|synopsis|map)\b/.test(fileNameLower)) {
      score += 0.3;
      signals.push('Filename match');
    }
    if (category === 'notes' && /\b(note|idea|scratch|brainstorm|todo)\b/.test(fileNameLower)) {
      score += 0.3;
      signals.push('Filename match');
    }

    scores[category] = Math.min(score, 1.0);
    detectedSignals[category] = signals;
  }

  // Find the best category
  let bestCategory = 'notes'; // default fallback
  let bestScore = 0;

  for (const [category, score] of Object.entries(scores)) {
    if (score > bestScore) {
      bestScore = score;
      bestCategory = category;
    }
  }

  // If confidence is very low, default to notes
  if (bestScore < 0.15) {
    bestCategory = 'notes';
    bestScore = 0.1;
  }

  return {
    ...parsedFile,
    category: bestCategory,
    confidence: bestScore,
    signals: detectedSignals[bestCategory] || [],
    allScores: scores,
    manuallyClassified: false

```js
  };
}

function classifyStructuredData(parsed, fileName) {
  const fileNameLower = fileName.toLowerCase();

  // Check for character-like structure
  if (parsed.characters || parsed.cast || parsed.dramatis_personae) {
    return {
      category: 'characters',
      confidence: 0.85,
      signals: ['Structured character data detected']
    };
  }

  // Check for array of objects with character-like fields
  if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
    const hasCharFields = parsed.some(item =>
      item && (item.name || item.character) && (item.traits || item.personality || item.appearance || item.backstory)
    );
    if (hasCharFields) {
      return {
        category: 'characters',
        confidence: 0.8,
        signals: ['Array of character objects']
      };
    }

    const hasBeatFields = parsed.some(item =>
      item && (item.beat || item.scene || item.act) && (item.summary || item.description || item.action)
    );
    if (hasBeatFields) {
      return {
        category: 'beatMap',
        confidence: 0.8,
        signals: ['Array of beat/scene objects']
      };
    }
  }

  // Check for world rules structure
  if (parsed.world || parsed.setting || parsed.lore || parsed.rules || parsed.magic_system || parsed.worldRules) {
    return {
      category: 'worldRules',
      confidence: 0.85,
      signals: ['Structured world data detected']
    };
  }

  // Check for beat map structure
  if (parsed.beats || parsed.outline || parsed.structure || parsed.acts || parsed.beatMap) {
    return {
      category: 'beatMap',
      confidence: 0.85,
      signals: ['Structured beat/outline data detected']
    };
  }

  // Filename-based fallback for structured data
  if (/character/i.test(fileNameLower)) {
    return { category: 'characters', confidence: 0.6, signals: ['Filename suggests characters'] };
  }
  if (/world|lore|setting|rule/i.test(fileNameLower)) {
    return { category: 'worldRules', confidence: 0.6, signals: ['Filename suggests world rules'] };
  }
  if (/beat|outline|structure|plot/i.test(fileNameLower)) {
    return { category: 'beatMap', confidence: 0.6, signals: ['Filename suggests beat map'] };
  }

  return { category: 'notes', confidence: 0.3, signals: ['Unrecognized structure'] };
}

// ─── Conflict Detection ─────────────────────────────────────────

function detectConflicts(classifications, existingProject) {
  const conflicts = [];

  if (!existingProject) return conflicts;

  for (const classified of classifications) {
    if (classified.category === 'skip') continue;

    // Check manuscript conflicts
    if (classified.category === 'manuscript' && existingProject.manuscript) {
      const existingChapters = existingProject.manuscript.chapters || [];
      if (existingChapters.length > 0) {
        // Try to detect chapter overlap
        const incomingChapters = extractChapterHeadings(classified.content);
        for (const incoming of incomingChapters) {
          const match = existingChapters.find(ec =>
            ec.title && incoming.title &&
            ec.title.toLowerCase().trim() === incoming.title.toLowerCase().trim()
          );
          if (match) {
            conflicts.push({
              id: uuidv4(),
              type: 'chapter_overlap',
              severity: 'warning',
              fileName: classified.fileName,
              description: `Chapter "${incoming.title}" already exists in the project`,
              existingValue: match.content?.substring(0, 300),
              incomingValue: incoming.content?.substring(0, 300),
              existingId: match.id,
              incomingData: incoming
            });
          }
        }
      }
    }

    // Check character conflicts
    if (classified.category === 'characters' && existingProject.characters) {
      const existingChars = existingProject.characters || [];
      const incomingChars = extractCharacterNames(classified);

      for (const incomingName of incomingChars) {
        const match = existingChars.find(ec =>
          ec.name && incomingName &&
          ec.name.toLowerCase().trim() === incomingName.toLowerCase().trim()
        );
        if (match) {
          conflicts.push({
            id: uuidv4(),
            type: 'character_duplicate',
            severity: 'warning',
            fileName: classified.fileName,
            description: `Character "${incomingName}" already exists in the project`,
            existingValue: match,
            incomingValue: incomingName,
            existingId: match.id
          });
        }
      }
    }

    // Check world rules conflicts
    if (classified.category === 'worldRules' && existingProject.worldRules) {
      const existingRules = existingProject.worldRules || [];
      if (existingRules.length > 0) {
        conflicts.push({
          id: uuidv4(),
          type: 'world_rules_exist',
          severity: 'info',
          fileName: classified.fileName,
          description: `Project already has ${existingRules.length} world rule(s). Importing may add duplicates.`,
          existingValue: existingRules.map(r => r.title || r.name).join(', '),
          incomingValue: classified.fileName
        });
      }
    }

    // Check beat map conflicts
    if (classified.category === 'beatMap' && existingProject.beats) {
      const existingBeats

---

}

function extractChapterHeadings(content) {
  if (!content) return [];
  const chapters = [];
  const chapterRegex = /^(chapter\s+\d+[^\n]*|part\s+(?:one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|\d+)[^\n]*)/gim;
  const parts = content.split(chapterRegex);

  for (let i = 1; i < parts.length; i += 2) {
    const title = parts[i]?.trim();
    const body = parts[i + 1]?.trim() || '';
    if (title) {
      chapters.push({ title, content: body });
    }
  }

  // If no chapters found, treat entire content as one chapter
  if (chapters.length === 0 && content.trim().length > 0) {
    chapters.push({ title: 'Untitled Chapter', content: content.trim() });
  }

  return chapters;
}

function extractCharacterNames(classified) {
  const names = [];

  // If structured data
  if (classified.metadata?.parsed) {
    const parsed = classified.metadata.parsed;
    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
      parsed.forEach(item => {
        if (item.name) names.push(item.name);
        if (item.character) names.push(item.character);
      });
    } else if (parsed.characters && Array.isArray(parsed.characters)) {
      parsed.characters.forEach(item => {
        if (item.name) names.push(item.name);
      });
    }
  }

  // If text content, try to extract names from labeled lines
  if (names.length === 0 && classified.content) {
    const nameMatches = classified.content.match(/^(?:name|character)\s*:\s*(.+)$/gim);
    if (nameMatches) {
      nameMatches.forEach(match => {
        const name = match.replace(/^(?:name|character)\s*:\s*/i, '').trim();
        if (name) names.push(name);
      });
    }
  }

  return names;
}

// ─── Manuscript Analysis (AI-powered) ───────────────────────────

async function analyzeManuscript(content, options, aiService) {
  const results = {};

  if (!aiService) {
    // Fallback: basic non-AI analysis
    return basicAnalysis(content, options);
  }

  if (options.extractCharacters) {
    try {
      const prompt = `Analyze the following manuscript text and extract all characters. For each character, provide:
- name: The character's name
- role: protagonist, antagonist, supporting, minor, or mentioned
- description: A brief description based on what appears in the text
- firstAppearance: Approximate location in the text where they first appear

Return the result as a JSON array of character objects.

Manuscript text (first 15000 characters):
${content.substring(0, 15000)}`;

      const response = await aiService.complete(prompt, { format: 'json' });
      results.characters = parseAIResponse(response, []);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Character extraction failed:', error);
      results.characters = basicExtractCharacters(content);
    }
  }

  if (options.extractWorldRules) {
    try {
      const prompt = `Analyze the following manuscript text and extract world-building rules, lore, and established facts about the story's world. For each rule/fact, provide:
- title: A short title for the rule
- description: The rule or fact as established in the text
- category: magic, technology, society, geography, history, or other
- confidence: how explicitly this is stated (explicit, implied, or inferred)

Return the result as a JSON array.

Manuscript text (first 15000 characters):
${content.substring(0, 15000)}`;

      const response = await aiService.complete(prompt, { format: 'json' });
      results.worldRules = parseAIResponse(response, []);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('World rules extraction failed:', error);
      results.worldRules = [];
    }
  }

  if (options.deriveBeatMap) {
    try {
      const prompt = `Analyze the following manuscript text and derive a story beat map / outline. For each beat, provide:
- title: A short title for the beat
- summary: What happens in this beat
- type: setup, rising_action, midpoint, climax, falling_action, resolution, or other
- approximatePosition: percentage through the manuscript (0-100)

Return the result as a JSON array ordered by position in the story.

Manuscript text (first 20000 characters):
${content.substring(0, 20000)}`;

      const response = await aiService.complete(prompt, { format: 'json' });
      results.beats = parseAIResponse(response, []);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Beat map derivation failed:', error);
      results.beats = [];
    }
  }

  if (options.checkContinuity) {
    try {
      const prompt = `Analyze the following manuscript text for continuity errors, inconsistencies, and contradictions. For each issue found, provide:
- description: What the continuity issue is
- severity: critical

---

      const response = await aiService.complete(prompt, { format: 'json' });
      results.continuityIssues = parseAIResponse(response, []);
    } catch (error) {

function parseAIResponse(response, fallback) {
  try {
    if (typeof response === 'string') {
      // Try to extract JSON from the response
      const jsonMatch = response.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/);
      if (jsonMatch) {
        return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
      }
      return JSON.parse(response);
    }
    return response || fallback;
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('Failed to parse AI response:', e);
    return fallback;
  }
}

function basicAnalysis(content, options) {
  const results = {};

  if (options.extractCharacters) {
    results.characters = basicExtractCharacters(content);
  }
  if (options.extractWorldRules) {
    results.worldRules = [];
  }
  if (options.deriveBeatMap) {
    results.beats = basicExtractBeats(content);
  }
  if (options.checkContinuity) {
    results.continuityIssues = [];
  }

  return results;
}

function basicExtractCharacters(content) {
  // Simple heuristic: find capitalized names near dialogue tags
  const characters = new Map();
  const dialoguePattern = /([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s[A-Z][a-z]+)?)\s+(?:said|asked|replied|whispered|shouted|muttered|exclaimed|called|cried|yelled|answered|responded|murmured|sighed|laughed|smiled|nodded|shook)/g;

  let match;
  while ((match = dialoguePattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
    const name = match[1];
    if (!characters.has(name)) {
      characters.set(name, {
        name,
        role: 'unknown',
        description: '',
        mentions: 0,
        firstAppearance: match.index
      });
    }
    characters.get(name).mentions++;
  }

  // Sort by mentions and assign roles
  const sorted = Array.from(characters.values()).sort((a, b) => b.mentions - a.mentions);
  sorted.forEach((char, i) => {
    if (i === 0) char.role = 'protagonist';
    else if (i < 3) char.role = 'supporting';
    else char.role = 'minor';
  });

  return sorted;
}

function basicExtractBeats(content) {
  const chapters = extractChapterHeadings(content);
  return chapters.map((ch, i) => ({
    title: ch.title,
    summary: ch.content.substring(0, 200) + (ch.content.length > 200 ? '...' : ''),
    type: 'other',
    approximatePosition: Math.round((i / Math.max(chapters.length - 1, 1)) * 100)
  }));
}

// ─── Apply Import ───────────────────────────────────────────────

function applyImport(classifications, conflictResolutions, analysisResults, existingProject) {
  const changelog = [];
  const warnings = [];
  const summary = {
    manuscriptChapters: 0,
    manuscriptWordCount: 0,
    charactersAdded: 0,
    charactersUpdated: 0,
    worldRulesAdded: 0,
    beatsAdded: 0,
    conflictsResolved: 0,
    filesSkipped: 0
  };

  // Build a resolution map
  const resolutionMap = {};
  if (conflictResolutions) {
    for (const [conflictId, resolution] of Object.entries(conflictResolutions)) {
      resolutionMap[conflictId] = resolution;
    }
    summary.conflictsResolved = Object.keys(resolutionMap).length;
  }

  // Initialize project structure
  const project = existingProject ? { ...existingProject } : {
    id: uuidv4(),
    manuscript: { chapters: [] },
    characters: [],
    worldRules: [],
    beats: [],
    notes: [],
    metadata: {
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      importedAt: new Date().toISOString()
    }
  };

  // Ensure arrays exist
  if (!project.manuscript) project.manuscript = { chapters: [] };
  if (!project.manuscript.chapters) project.manuscript.chapters = [];
  if (!project.characters) project.characters = [];
  if (!project.worldRules) project.worldRules = [];
  if (!project.beats) project.beats = [];
  if (!project.notes) project.notes = [];

  for (const classified of classifications) {
    if (classified.category === 'skip') {
      summary.filesSkipped++;
      changelog.push({
        type: 'skip',
        action: 'Skipped',
        target: classified.fileName,
        detail: 'File was marked to skip'
      });
      continue;
    }

    switch (classified.category) {
      case 'manuscript':
        applyManuscript(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings);
        break;
      case 'characters':
        applyCharacters(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings);
        break;
      case 'worldRules':
        applyWorld

        });
      }

  };

}

function applyManuscript(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings) {
  const chapters = extractChapterHeadings(classified.content);

  for (const chapter of chapters) {
    // Check if this chapter has a conflict
    const existingChapter = project.manuscript.chapters.find(c =>
      c.title && chapter.title &&
      c.title.toLowerCase().trim() === chapter.title.toLowerCase().trim()
    );

    if (existingChapter) {
      // Look for a resolution
      const resolution = findResolutionForConflict(resolutionMap, 'chapter_overlap', chapter.title);

      if (resolution === 'keep_existing') {
        changelog.push({
          type: 'manuscript',
          action: 'Kept existing',
          target: chapter.title,
          detail: 'Conflict resolved: kept existing version'
        });
        continue;
      } else if (resolution === 'use_incoming') {
        existingChapter.content = chapter.content;
        existingChapter.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
        existingChapter.source = classified.fileName;
        changelog.push({
          type: 'manuscript',
          action: 'Replaced',
          target: chapter.title,
          detail: `Replaced with version from ${classified.fileName}`
        });
      } else if (resolution === 'merge') {
        existingChapter.content = existingChapter.content + '\n\n---\n\n' + chapter.content;
        existingChapter.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
        changelog.push({
          type: 'manuscript',
          action: 'Merged',
          target: chapter.title,
          detail: `Merged with content from ${classified.fileName}`
        });
      } else {
        // Default: append as new chapter with modified title
        const newChapter = {
          id: uuidv4(),
          title: chapter.title + ' (imported)',
          content: chapter.content,
          source: classified.fileName,
          createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
        };
        project.manuscript.chapters.push(newChapter);

function applyCharacters(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings) {
  let characters = [];

  // Try structured data first
  if (classified.metadata?.parsed) {
    const parsed = classified.metadata.parsed;
    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
      characters = parsed.filter(item => item.name || item.character).map(item => ({
        name: item.name || item.character,
        role: item.role || 'unknown',
        description: item.description || item.bio || '',
        traits: item.traits || item.personality || '',
        appearance: item.appearance || '',
        backstory: item.backstory || item.background || '',
        motivation: item.motivation || item.goal || '',
        relationships: item.relationships || ''
      }));
    } else if (parsed.characters && Array.isArray(parsed.characters)) {
      characters = parsed.characters.map(item => ({
        name: item.name || item.character,
        role: item.role || 'unknown',
        description: item.description || item.bio || '',
        traits: item.traits || item.personality || '',
        appearance: item.appearance || '',
        backstory: item.backstory || item.background || '',
        motivation: item.motivation || item.goal || '',
        relationships: item.relationships || ''
      }));
    }
  }

  // Fall back to text extraction
  if (characters.length === 0 && classified.content) {
    characters = extractCharactersFromText(classified.content);
  }

  for (const char of characters) {
    if (!char.name) continue;

    const existing = project.characters.find(c =>
      c.name && char.name &&
      c.name.toLowerCase().trim() === char.name.toLowerCase().trim()
    );

    if (existing) {
      const resolution = findResolutionForConflict(resolutionMap, 'character_duplicate', char.name);

      if (resolution === 'keep_existing') {
        changelog.push({
          type: 'character',
          action: 'Kept existing',
          target: char.name,
          detail: 'Conflict resolved: kept existing version'
        });
        continue;
      } else if (resolution === 'use_incoming') {
        Object.assign(existing, {
          ...char,
          id: existing.id,
          updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          source: classified.fileName
        });
        summary.charactersUpdated++;
        changelog.push({
          type: 'character',
          action: 'Replaced',
          target: char.name,
          detail: `Replaced with version from ${classified.fileName}`
        });
      } else if (resolution === 'merge') {
        // Merge: fill in empty fields from incoming
        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(char)) {
          if (value && !existing[key]) {
            existing[key] = value;
          }
        }
        existing.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
        summary.charactersUpdated++;
        changelog.push({
          type: 'character',
          action: 'Merged',
          target: char.name,
          detail: `Merged fields from ${classified.fileName}`
        });
      } else {
        // Default: merge non-empty fields
        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(char)) {
          if (value && !existing[key]) {
            existing[key] = value;
          }
        }
        existing.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
        summary.charactersUpdated++;
        changelog.push({
          type: 'character',
          action: 'Auto-merged',
          target: char.name,
          detail: `Filled empty fields from ${classified.fileName}`
        });
      }
    } else {
      const newChar = {
        id: uuidv4(),
        ...char,
        source: classified.fileName,
        createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
      };
      project.characters.push(newChar);
      summary.charactersAdded++;
      changelog.push({
        type: 'character',
        action: 'Added',
        target: char.name,
        detail: `From ${classified.fileName}`
      });
    }
  }
}

  let currentChar = null;
  for (const block of blocks) {
    const nameMatch = block.match(/^(?:name|character)\s*:\s*(.+)$/im);
    if (nameMatch) {
      if (currentChar && currentChar.name) {
        characters.push(currentChar);

      const backstoryMatch = block.match(/^(?:backstory|background|history)\s*:\s*(.+)$/im);

      const trimmed = block.trim();
      if (trimmed) {
        currentChar.description = (currentChar.description || '') + '\n' + trimmed;
      }
    }
  }

  if (currentChar && currentChar.name) {
    characters.push(currentChar);
  }

  // If no structured characters found, try heading-based extraction
  if (characters.length === 0) {
    const headingBlocks = content.split(/^#{1,3}\s+/m).filter(Boolean);
    for (const hBlock of headingBlocks) {
      const lines = hBlock.split('\n');
      const name = lines[0]?.trim();
      if (name && name.length < 60 && /^[A-Z]/.test(name)) {
        characters.push({
          name,
          description: lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim(),
          role: 'unknown'
        });
      }
    }
  }

  return characters;
}

function applyWorldRules(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings) {
  let rules = [];

  // Try structured data
  if (classified.metadata?.parsed) {
    const parsed = classified.metadata.parsed;
    if (parsed.world || parsed.worldRules || parsed.rules || parsed.lore || parsed.setting) {
      const source = parsed.world || parsed.worldRules || parsed.rules || parsed.lore || parsed.setting;
      if (Array.isArray(source)) {
        rules = source.map(item => ({
          title: item.title || item.name || item.rule || 'Untitled Rule',
          description: item.description || item.detail || item.content || JSON.stringify(item),
          category: item.category || item.type || 'other'
        }));
      } else if (typeof source === 'object') {
        // Convert object keys to rules
        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(source)) {
          rules.push({
            title: key,
            description: typeof value === 'string' ? value : JSON.stringify(value, null, 2),
            category: 'other'
          });
        }
      }
    }
  }

  // Fall back to text extraction
  if (rules.length === 0 && classified.content) {
    rules = extractRulesFromText(classified.content);
  }

  for (const rule of rules) {
    const newRule = {
      id: uuidv4(),
      title: rule.title,
      description: rule.description,
      category: rule.category || 'other',
      source: classified.fileName,
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
    };
    project.worldRules.push(newRule);
    summary.worldRulesAdded++;
    changelog.push({
      type: 'worldRule',
      action: 'Added',
      target: rule.title,
      detail: `From ${classified.fileName}`
    });
  }
}

function extractRulesFromText(content) {
  const rules = [];
  // Split by headings
  const sections = content.split(/^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/m);

  for (let i = 1; i < sections.length; i += 2) {
    const title = sections[i]?.trim();
    const body = sections[i + 1]?.trim();
    if (title && body) {
      rules.push({
        title,
        description: body,
        category: guessRuleCategory(title + ' ' + body)
      });
    }
  }

  // If no headings, treat the whole content as one rule
  if (rules.length === 0 && content.trim()) {
    const firstLine = content.trim().split('\n')[0];
    rules.push({
      title: firstLine.substring(0, 80),
      description: content.trim(),
      category: 'other'
    });
  }

  return rules;
}

function guessRuleCategory(text) {
  const lower = text.toLowerCase();
  if (/magic|spell|enchant|arcane|mana|power|ability|supernatural/.test(lower)) return 'magic';
  if (/tech|device|machine|computer|science|invention|gadget/.test(lower)) return 'technology';
  if (/society|culture|custom|tradition|law|government|politic|class|caste/.test(lower)) return 'society';
  if (/geography|map|land|terrain|city|town

}

function applyBeatMap(classified, project, resolutionMap, changelog, summary, warnings) {
  let beats = [];

  // Try structured data
  if (classified.metadata?.parsed) {
    const parsed = classified.metadata.parsed;
    const source = parsed.beats || parsed.outline || parsed.structure || parsed.acts || parsed.beatMap;
    if (source && Array.isArray(source)) {
      beats = source.map((item, i) => ({
        title: item.title || item.name || item.beat || `Beat ${i + 1}`,
        summary: item.summary || item.description || item.content || '',
        type: item.type || item.category || 'other',
        position: item.position || item.approximatePosition || Math.round((i / Math.max(source.length - 1, 1)) * 100)
      }));
    } else if (source && typeof source === 'object' && !Array.isArray(source)) {
      // Handle acts structure like { act1: {...}, act2: {...} }
      let position = 0;
      const keys = Object.keys(source);
      for (const key of keys) {
        const act = source[key];
        if (Array.isArray(act)) {
          for (const item of act) {
            beats.push({
              title: item.title || item.name || `${key} - Beat`,
              summary: item.summary || item.description || '',
              type: item.type || 'other',
              position: position
            });
            position += Math.round(100 / (keys.length * (act.length || 1)));
          }
        } else {
          beats.push({
            title: key,
            summary: typeof act === 'string' ? act : (act.summary || act.description || JSON.stringify(act)),
            type: 'other',
            position: position
          });
          position += Math.round(100 / keys.length);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  // Fall back to text extraction
  if (beats.length === 0 && classified.content) {
    beats = extractBeatsFromText(classified.content);
  }

  for (const beat of beats) {
    const newBeat = {
      id: uuidv4(),
      title: beat.title,
      summary: beat.summary,
      type: beat.type || 'other',
      position: beat.position || 0,
      source: classified.fileName,
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
    };
    project.beats.push(newBeat);
    summary.beatsAdded++;
    changelog.push({
      type: 'beat',
      action: 'Added',
      target: beat.title,
      detail: `From ${classified.fileName}, position: ${beat.position}%`
    });
  }
}

function extractBeatsFromText(content) {
  const beats = [];
  // Try numbered list format
  const numberedPattern = /^\s*(\d+)[.)]\s*(.+)$/gm;
  let match;
  const numberedItems = [];
  while ((match = numberedPattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
    numberedItems.push(match[2].trim());
  }

  if (numberedItems.length > 0) {
    return numberedItems.map((item, i) => ({
      title: item.length > 80 ? item.substring(0, 77) + '...' : item,
      summary: item,
      type: 'other',
      position: Math.round((i / Math.max(numberedItems.length - 1, 1)) * 100)
    }));
  }

  // Try heading-based
  const sections = content.split(/^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$/m);
  for (let i = 1; i < sections.length; i += 2) {
    const title = sections[i]?.trim();
    const body = sections[i + 1]?.trim();
    if (title) {
      beats.push({
        title,
        summary: body || '',
        type: 'other',
        position: Math.round(((i - 1) / 2 / Math.max((sections.length - 1) / 2 - 1, 1)) * 100)
      });
    }
  }

  // If nothing found, treat as single beat
  if (beats.length === 0 && content.trim()) {
    beats.push({
      title: 'Imported Outline',
      summary: content.trim(),
      type: 'other',
      position: 50
    });
  }

  return beats;
}

function applyNotes(classified, project, changelog, summary) {
  const note = {
    id: uuidv4(),
    title: classified.fileName,
    content: classified.content || (classified.metadata?.parsed ? JSON.stringify(classified.metadata.parsed, null, 2) : ''),
    source: classified.fileName,
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
  };
  project.notes.push(note);
  changelog.push({
    type: 'note',
    action: 'Added',
    target: classified.fileName,
    detail: 'Imported as note'
  });
}

  for (const [conflictId

```js
function findResolutionForConflict(resolutionMap, conflictType, targetName) {
  for (const [conflictId, resolution] of Object.entries(resolutionMap)) {
    // Resolution objects should have been tagged with type and target
    if (resolution.conflictType === conflictType && resolution.targetName === targetName) {
      return resolution.action; // 'keep_existing', 'use_incoming', 'merge'
    }
    // Simple string resolution keyed by conflictId
    if (typeof resolution === 'string') {
      return resolution;
    }
  }
  return null;
}

  parseFiles,
  classifyFiles,
  detectConflicts,
  analyzeManuscript,
  applyImport,
  // Expose for testing
  _internal: {
    extractChapterHeadings,
    extractCharacterNames,
    extractCharactersFromText,
    extractRulesFromText,
    extractBeatsFromText,
    basicExtractCharacters,
    basicExtractBeats,
    guessRuleCategory,
    parseAIResponse,
    findResolutionForConflict
  }
};

Now let me think about what I've built and whether it's complete. The importService has:

1. `parseFiles` - Reads and parses files (txt, md, docx, json, yaml, csv)
2. `classifyFiles` - Classifies parsed files into categories (manuscript, characters, worldRules, beatMap, notes)
3. `detectConflicts` - Detects conflicts between incoming files and existing project data
4. `analyzeManuscript` - AI-powered analysis (character extraction, world rules, beat map, continuity)
5. `applyImport` - Applies the import with conflict resolution

```js
// routes/importRoutes.js
const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();
const multer = require('multer');
const importService = require('../services/importService');

// Configure multer for file uploads
const storage = multer.memoryStorage();
const upload = multer({
  storage,
  limits: {
    fileSize: 50 * 1024 * 1024, // 50MB per file
    files: 50 // max 50 files
  },
  fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => {
    const allowedTypes = [
      'text/plain',
      'text/markdown',
      'application/json',
      'text/csv',
      'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
      'application/x-yaml',
      'text/yaml',
      'application/octet-stream' // fallback
    ];
    const allowedExtensions = ['.txt', '.md', '.json', '.yaml', '.yml', '.csv', '.docx'];
    const ext = '.' + file.originalname.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();

    if (allowedTypes.includes(file.mimetype) || allowedExtensions.includes(ext)) {
      cb(null, true);
    } else {
      cb(new Error(`Unsupported file type: ${file.originalname}`));
    }
  }
});

// Step 1: Upload and parse files
router.post('/parse', upload.array('files'), async (req, res) => {
  try {
    if (!req.files || req.files.length === 0) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'No files uploaded' });
    }

    const files = req.files.map(f => ({
      name: f.originalname,
      buffer: f.buffer,
      mimetype: f.mimetype,
      size: f.size
    }));

    const parsed = await importService.parseFiles(files);
    res.json({ parsed });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Parse error:', error);
    res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
  }
});

// Step 2: Classify parsed files
router.post('/classify', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { parsedFiles, userOverrides } = req.body;
    if (!parsedFiles || !Array.isArray(parsedFiles)) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'parsedFiles array required' });
    }

    const classifications = importService.classifyFiles(parsedFiles, userOverrides);
    res.json({ classifications });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Classify error:', error);
    res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
  }
});

// Step 3: Detect conflicts
router.post('/conflicts', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { classifications, projectId } = req.body;
    if (!classifications || !Array.isArray(classifications)) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'classifications array required' });
    }

    // Load existing project if projectId provided
    let existingProject = null;
    if (projectId) {
      // This would come from your project storage/database
      existingProject = await loadProject(projectId);
    }

    const conflicts = importService.detectConflicts(classifications, existingProject);
    res.json({ conflicts });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Conflict detection error:', error);
    res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
  }
});

// Step 4: Analyze manuscript (optional, AI-powered)

  try {
    const { manuscriptContent, options } = req.body;
    if (!manuscriptContent) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'manuscriptContent required' });
    }

    const analysis

router.post('/apply', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { classifications, conflictResolutions, analysisResults, projectId } = req.body;
    if (!classifications || !Array.isArray(classifications)) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'classifications array required' });
    }

    let existingProject = null;
    if (projectId) {
      existingProject = await loadProject(projectId);
    }

    const result = importService.applyImport(
      classifications,
      conflictResolutions || {},
      analysisResults || null,
      existingProject
    );

    // Save the project
    await saveProject(result.project);

    res.json({
      projectId: result.project.id,
      changelog: result.changelog,
      warnings: result.warnings,
      summary: result.summary
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Apply import error:', error);
    res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
  }
});

// Combined single-step import for simple cases
router.post('/quick', upload.array('files'), async (req, res) => {
  try {
    if (!req.files || req.files.length === 0) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'No files uploaded' });
    }

    const projectId = req.body.projectId;
    const useAI = req.body.useAI === 'true';

    const files = req.files.map(f => ({
      name: f.originalname,
      buffer: f.buffer,
      mimetype: f.mimetype,
      size: f.size
    }));

    // Parse
    const parsed = await importService.parseFiles(files);

    // Classify
    const classifications = importService.classifyFiles(parsed);

    // Load existing project
    let existingProject = null;
    if (projectId) {
      existingProject = await loadProject(projectId);
    }

    // Detect conflicts
    const conflicts = importService.detectConflicts(classifications, existingProject);

    // If there are conflicts, return them for user resolution
    if (conflicts.length > 0) {
      return res.json({
        status: 'conflicts_detected',
        classifications,
        conflicts,
        message: 'Conflicts detected. Please resolve and call /apply.'
      });
    }

    // Analyze manuscript content if AI enabled
    let analysisResults = null;
    if (useAI) {
      const manuscriptContent = classifications
        .filter(c => c.category === 'manuscript')
        .map(c => c.content)
        .join('\n\n');

      if (manuscriptContent) {
        analysisResults = await importService.analyzeManuscript(manuscriptContent, {
          extractCharacters: true,
          extractWorldRules: true,
          deriveBeatMap: true,
          checkContinuity: true,
          useAI: true
        });
      }
    }

    // Apply
    const result = importService.applyImport(
      classifications,
      {},
      analysisResults,
      existingProject
    );

    await saveProject(result.project);

    res.json({
      status: 'complete',
      projectId: result.project.id,
      changelog: result.changelog,
      warnings: result.warnings,
      summary: result.summary
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Quick import error:', error);
    res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
  }
});

// Placeholder project storage functions
async function loadProject(projectId) {
  // In production, this would load from database
  // For now, return null to indicate new project
  return null;
}

async function saveProject(project) {
  // In production, this would save to database
  console.log(`Project ${project.id} saved with ${project.manuscript.chapters.length} chapters`);
}

module.exports = router;

```jsx
const initialState = {
  currentStep: 0,
  files: [],
  parsedFiles: [],
  classifications: [],
  userOverrides: {},
  conflicts: [],
  conflictResolutions: {},
  analysisResults: null,
  analysisOptions: {
    extractCharacters: true,
    extractWorldRules: true,
    deriveBeatMap: true,
    checkContinuity: true,
    useAI: false
  },
  importResult: null,
  loading: false,
  error: null
};

function importReducer(state, action) {
  switch (action.type) {
    case 'SET_FILES':
      return { ...state, files: action.payload, error: null };
    case 'SET_PARSED':
      return { ...state, parsedFiles: action.payload };
    case 'SET_CLASSIFICATIONS':
      return { ...state, classifications: action.payload };
    case 'SET_USER_OVERRIDE':
      return {
        ...state,
        userOverrides: {
          ...state.userOverrides,
          [action.payload.fileName]: action.payload.category
        }
      };
    case 'SET_CONFLICTS':
      return { ...state, conflicts: action.payload };
    case 'SET_CONFLICT_RESOLUTION':
      return {
        ...state,
        conflictResolutions: {
          ...state.conflictResolutions,
          [action.payload.conflictId]: action.payload.resolution
        }
      };
    case 'SET_ANALYSIS_OPTIONS':
      return {
        ...state,
        analysisOptions: { ...state.analysisOptions, ...action.payload }
      };
    case 'SET_ANALYSIS_RESULTS':
      return { ...state, analysisResults: action.payload };
    case 'SET_IMPORT_RESULT':
      return { ...state, importResult: action.payload };
    case 'SET_STEP':
      return { ...state, currentStep: action.payload, error: null };
    case 'SET_LOADING':
      return { ...state, loading: action.payload };
    case 'SET_ERROR':
      return { ...state, error: action.payload, loading: false };
    case 'RESET':
      return initialState;
    default:
      return state;
  }
}

export default function ImportWizard({ projectId, onComplete, onCancel }) {
  const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(importReducer, initialState);

  const goToStep = useCallback((step) => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_STEP', payload: step });
  }, []);

  const nextStep = useCallback(() => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_STEP', payload: state.currentStep + 1 });
  }, [state.currentStep]);

  const prevStep = useCallback(() => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_STEP', payload: Math.max(0, state.currentStep - 1) });
  }, [state.currentStep]);

  // Step 1: Upload and parse
  const handleFilesSelected = useCallback(async (files) => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_FILES', payload: files });
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: true });

    try {
      const formData = new FormData();
      for (const file of files) {
        formData.append('files', file);
      }

      const response = await fetch('/api/import/parse', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: formData
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const err = await response.json();
        throw new Error(err.error || 'Failed to parse files');
      }

      const data = await response.json();
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_PARSED', payload: data.parsed });
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: false });
      nextStep();
    } catch (error) {
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_ERROR', payload: error.message });
    }
  }, [nextStep]);

  // Step 2: Classify
  const handleClassify = useCallback(async () => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: true });

    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/import/classify', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          parsedFiles: state.parsedFiles,
          userOverrides: state.userOverrides
        })
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const err = await response.json();
        throw new Error(err.error || 'Failed to classify files');
      }

      const data = await response.json();
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_CLASSIFICATIONS', payload: data.classifications });
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: false });
      nextStep();
    } catch (error) {
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_ERROR', payload: error.message });
    }
  }, [state.parsedFiles, state.userOverrides, nextStep]);

  // Step 3: Detect conflicts
  const handleDetectConflicts = useCallback(async () => {
    dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: true });

    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/import/conflicts', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          classifications: state.classifications,
          projectId
        })
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const err = await response.json();
        throw new Error(err.error || 'Failed to detect conflicts');
      }

      const data = await response.json();
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_CONFLICTS', payload: data.conflicts });
      dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOADING', payload: false });

      // Skip conflict step if no conflicts
      if (data.conflicts.length
