  const confidenceClass = entity.confidence >= 0.7 ? 'confidence-high'
    : entity.confidence >= 0.4 ? 'confidence-medium' : 'confidence-low';

  const handleSave = () => {
    onUpdate(entity.id, {
      name: editName,
      type: editType,
    });
    setEditing(false);
  };

  const addAlias = () => {
    if (!newAlias.trim()) return;
    const aliases = [...(entity.aliases || []), newAlias.trim()];
    onUpdate(entity.id, { aliases });
    setNewAlias('');
  };

  const removeAlias = (alias) => {
    const aliases = (entity.aliases || []).filter(a => a !== alias);
    onUpdate(entity.id, { aliases });
  };

  const addAttribute = () => {
    if (!newAttrKey.trim()) return;
    const attributes = { ...(entity.attributes || {}), [newAttrKey.trim()]: newAttrValue.trim() };
    onUpdate(entity.id, { attributes });
    setNewAttrKey('');
    setNewAttrValue('');
  };

  const removeAttribute = (key) => {
    const attributes = { ...(entity.attributes || {}) };
    delete attributes[key];
    onUpdate(entity.id, { attributes });
  };

  const getRelatedEntity = (rel) => {
    const otherId = rel.sourceId === entity.id ? rel.targetId : rel.sourceId;
    return entities.find(e => e.id === otherId);
  };

  return (
    <div className="entity-detail">
      <div className="detail-header">
        <div className="detail-title-row">
          {editing ? (
            <input
              className="edit-name-input"
              value={editName}
              onChange={e => setEditName(e.target.value)}
              autoFocus
            />
          ) : (
            <h2>{entity.name}</h2>
          )}
          {editing ? (
            <select
              className="type-select"
              value={editType}
              onChange={e => setEditType(e.target.value)}
            >
              {Object.keys(typeColors).map(t => (
                <option key={t} value={t}>{t}</option>
              ))}
            </select>
          ) : (
            <span
              className="entity-type-badge"
              style={{ background: typeColors[entity.type] || typeColors.unknown }}
            >
              {entity.type}
            </span>
          )}
          {entity.verified && <span className="verified-badge">✓ Verified</span>}
        </div>
        <div className="detail-actions">
          {editing ? (
            <>
              <button className="action-btn save" onClick={handleSave}>Save</button>
              <button className="action-btn" onClick={() => { setEditing(false); setEditName(entity.name); setEditType(entity.type); }}>Cancel</button>
            </>
          ) : (
            <>
              <button className="action-btn" onClick={() => setEditing(true)}>✏️ Edit</button>
              {!entity.verified && (
                <button className="action-btn verify" onClick={() => onVerify(entity.id)}>✓ Verify</button>
              )}
              <button className="action-btn delete" onClick={() => { onDelete(entity.id); onClose(); }}>🗑️ Delete</button>
            </>
          )}
          <button className="action-btn close" onClick={onClose}>✕</button>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="detail-stats">
        <div className="stat">
          <span className="stat-label">Mentions</span>
          <span className="stat-value">{entity.mentionCount || 1}</span>
        </div>
        <div className="stat">
          <span className="stat-label">Confidence</span>
          <span className={`stat-value ${confidenceClass}`}>
            {Math.round((entity.confidence || 0) * 100)}%
          </span>
          <div className="confidence-bar">
            <div
              className="confidence-fill"
              style={{
                width: `${(entity.confidence || 0) * 100}%`,
                background: entity.confidence >= 0.7 ? 'var(--success)' : entity.confidence >= 0.4 ? 'var(--warning)' : 'var(--error)',
              }}
            />
          </div>
        </div>
        <div className="stat">
          <span className="stat-label">Relations</span>
          <span className="stat-value">{entityRelationships.length}</span>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Aliases */}
      <div className="detail-section">
        <h3>Aliases</h3>
        <div className="alias-list">
          {(entity.aliases || []).map((alias, i) => (
            <span key={i} className="alias-chip">
              {alias}
              {editing && (
                <button className="remove-chip" onClick={() => removeAlias(alias)}>×</button>
              )}
            </span>
          ))}
          {(!entity.aliases || entity.aliases.length === 0) && (
            <span className="empty-note">No aliases</span>
          )}
        </div>
        {editing && (
          <div className="add-row">
            <input
              placeholder="Add alias..."
              value={newAlias}
              onChange={e => setNewAlias(e.target.value)}
              onKeyDown={e => e.key === 'Enter' && addAlias()}
            />
            <button onClick={addAlias}>+</button>
          </div>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Attributes */}
      <div className="detail-section

      {/* Attributes */}
      <div className="detail-section">
        <h3>Attributes</h3>
        <div className="attribute-list">
          {Object.entries(entity.attributes || {}).map(([key, value]) => (
            <div key={key} className="attribute-row">
              <span className="attr-key">{key}:</span>
              <span className="attr-value">{value}</span>
              {editing && (
                <button className="remove-chip" onClick={() => removeAttribute(key)}>×</button>

          {(!entity.attributes || Object.keys(entity.attributes).length === 0) && (

        {editing && (
          <div className="add-row">
            <input
              placeholder="Key"
              value={newAttrKey}
              onChange={e => setNewAttrKey(e.target.value)}
              style={{ flex: 1 }}
            />
            <input
              placeholder="Value"
              value={newAttrValue}
              onChange={e => setNewAttrValue(e.target.value)}
              style={{ flex: 2 }}
              onKeyDown={e => e.key === 'Enter' && addAttribute()}
            />
            <button onClick={addAttribute}>+</button>
          </div>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Relationships */}
      <div className="detail-section">
        <h3>Relationships ({entityRelationships.length})</h3>
        <div className="relationship-list">
          {entityRelationships.map((rel, i) => {
            const other = getRelatedEntity(rel);
            if (!other) return null;
            const isSource = rel.sourceId === entity.id;
            return (
              <div
                key={i}
                className="relationship-row clickable"
                onClick={() => onSelectEntity(other)}
              >
                <span className="rel-direction">{isSource ? '→' : '←'}</span>
                <span className="rel-type">{rel.type}</span>
                <span
                  className="entity-chip"
                  style={{ borderColor: typeColors[other.type] || typeColors.unknown, color: typeColors[other.type] || typeColors.unknown }}
                >
                  {other.name}
                </span>
                <span className="rel-confidence">
                  {Math.round(rel.confidence * 100)}%
                </span>
              </div>
            );
          })}
          {entityRelationships.length === 0 && (
            <span className="empty-note">No relationships found</span>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Context */}
      <div className="detail-section">
        <h3>Context Mentions</h3>
        <div className="context-list">
          {(entity.contexts || []).slice(0, 5).map((ctx, i) => (
            <div key={i} className="context-item">
              <span className="context-index">#{i + 1}</span>
              <span className="context-text">"{ctx}"</span>
            </div>
          ))}
          {(!entity.contexts || entity.contexts.length === 0) && (
            <span className="empty-note">No context recorded</span>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

export default EntityDetail;

// src/App.jsx
import React, { useState, useCallback, useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
import GraphView from './components/GraphView';
import EntityDetail from './components/EntityDetail';
import { extractEntities, extractRelationships } from './utils/entityExtractor';
import { createStore } from './utils/graphStore';
import './styles/App.css';

const store = createStore();

function App() {
  const [entities, setEntities] = useState([]);
  const [relationships, setRelationships] = useState([]);
  const [selectedEntity, setSelectedEntity] = useState(null);
  const [view, setView] = useState('graph'); // graph, list, timeline, export
  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('');
  const [typeFilter, setTypeFilter] = useState('all');
  const [confidenceFilter, setConfidenceFilter] = useState(0);
  const [inputText, setInputText] = useState('');
  const [showInput, setShowInput] = useState(false);
  const [processing, setProcessing] = useState(false);
  const [message, setMessage] = useState(null);
  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState('name');
  const [showVerifiedOnly, setShowVerifiedOnly] = useState(false);
  const inputRef = useRef(null);

  // Load from localStorage on mount
  useEffect(() => {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem('entity-tracker-data');
    if (saved) {
      try {
        const data = JSON.parse(saved);
        store.load(data);
        setEntities(store.getEntities());
        setRelationships(store.getRelationships());
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('Failed to load saved data:', e);
      }
    }
  }, []);

  // Save to localStorage on changes
  useEffect(() => {
    const data = { entities, relationships };
    localStorage.setItem('entity-tracker-data', JSON.stringify(data));
  }, [entities, relationships]);

  const refreshState = useCallback(() => {
    setEntities(store.getEntities());
    setRelationships(store.getRelationships());
  }, []);

  const handleProcessText = useCallback(() => {
    if (!inputText.trim()) return;
    setProcessing(true);

    // Use setTimeout to allow UI to update
    setTimeout(() => {
      try {
        const

---

        const extractedEntities = extractEntities(inputText);
        const extractedRelationships = extractRelationships(inputText, extractedEntities);

        extractedEntities.forEach(entity => {
          store.addEntity(entity);
        });

        extractedRelationships.forEach(rel => {
          store.addRelationship(rel);
        });

        refreshState();
        setInputText('');
        setShowInput(false);
        setMessage({
          type: 'success',
          text: `Extracted ${extractedEntities.length} entities and ${extractedRelationships.length} relationships.`,
        });
        setTimeout(() => setMessage(null), 4000);
      } catch (e) {
        setMessage({ type: 'error', text: `Processing error: ${e.message}` });
        setTimeout(() => setMessage(null), 4000);
      }
      setProcessing(false);
    }, 50);
  }, [inputText, refreshState]);

  const handleUpdateEntity = useCallback((id, updates) => {
    store.updateEntity(id, updates);
    refreshState();
    // Update selected entity if it's the one being edited
    setSelectedEntity(prev => {
      if (prev && prev.id === id) {
        return { ...prev, ...updates };
      }
      return prev;
    });
  }, [refreshState]);

  const handleDeleteEntity = useCallback((id) => {
    store.deleteEntity(id);
    refreshState();
    if (selectedEntity && selectedEntity.id === id) {
      setSelectedEntity(null);
    }
  }, [refreshState, selectedEntity]);

  const handleVerifyEntity = useCallback((id) => {
    store.updateEntity(id, { verified: true, confidence: 1.0 });
    refreshState();
    setSelectedEntity(prev => {
      if (prev && prev.id === id) {
        return { ...prev, verified: true, confidence: 1.0 };
      }
      return prev;
    });
  }, [refreshState]);

  const handleSelectEntity = useCallback((entity) => {
    setSelectedEntity(entity);
  }, []);

  const handleExport = useCallback(() => {
    const data = {
      entities: store.getEntities(),
      relationships: store.getRelationships(),
      exportedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      version: '1.0',
    };
    const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)], { type: 'application/json' });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `entity-graph-${Date.now()}.json`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, []);

  const handleImport = useCallback((jsonStr) => {
    try {
      const data = JSON.parse(jsonStr);
      if (data.entities) {
        data.entities.forEach(e => store.addEntity(e));
      }
      if (data.relationships) {
        data.relationships.forEach(r => store.addRelationship(r));
      }
      refreshState();
      setMessage({ type: 'success', text: `Imported ${(data.entities || []).length} entities.` });
      setTimeout(() => setMessage(null), 4000);
    } catch (e) {
      setMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'Invalid JSON format.' });
      setTimeout(() => setMessage(null), 4000);
    }
  }, [refreshState]);

  const handleClearAll = useCallback(() => {
    if (window.confirm('Are you sure you want to clear all data? This cannot be undone.')) {
      store.clear();
      refreshState();
      setSelectedEntity(null);
      setMessage({ type: 'success', text: 'All data cleared.' });
      setTimeout(() => setMessage(null), 4000);
    }
  }, [refreshState]);

  const handleMergeEntities = useCallback((sourceId, targetId) => {
    store.mergeEntities(sourceId, targetId);
    refreshState();
    const merged = store.getEntities().find(e => e.id === targetId);
    if (merged) setSelectedEntity(merged);
  }, [refreshState]);

  // Filtering and sorting
  const filteredEntities = entities.filter(e => {
    if (typeFilter !== 'all' && e.type !== typeFilter) return false;
    if (e.confidence < confidenceFilter) return false;
    if (showVerifiedOnly && !e.verified) return false;
    if (searchQuery) {
      const q = searchQuery.toLowerCase();
      const nameMatch = e.name.toLowerCase().includes(q);
      const aliasMatch = (e.aliases || []).some(a => a.toLowerCase().includes(q));
      if (!nameMatch && !aliasMatch) return false;
    }
    return true;
  }).sort((a, b) => {
    switch (sortBy) {
      case 'name': return a.name.localeCompare(b.name);
      case 'type': return a.type.localeCompare(b.type);
      case 'confidence': return b.confidence - a.confidence;
      case 'mentions': return (b.mentionCount || 1) - (a.mentionCount || 1);
      default: return 0;
    }
  });

  const typeStats = {};
  entities.forEach(e => {
    typeStats[e.type] = (typeStats[e.type] || 0) + 1;
  });

  const typeColors = {
    character: '#4A90D9',
    location: '#2ECC71',
    item: '#E67E22',
    event: '#9B59B6',
    faction: '#E74C3C',
    concept: '#1ABC9C',
    creature: '#F39C12',
    unknown: '#95A5

  return (

            className={`header-btn ${showInput ? 'active' : ''}`}

          </button>
          <button className="header-btn" onClick={handleExport} disabled={entities.length === 0}>

          </button>
          <button className="header-btn danger" onClick={handleClearAll} disabled={entities.length === 0}>
            Clear
          </button>
        </div>

      {message && (

          <button onClick={() => setMessage(null)}>×</button>

      )}

      {/* Text Input Panel */}
      {showInput && (

                Cancel

              <button

            </label>
          </div>
        </div>

      <div className="main-content">

                        <button
                          className="table-btn"
                          onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); handleDeleteEntity(entity.id); }}
                        >
                          🗑️
                        </button>
                      </td>
                    </tr>
                  ))}
                </tbody>
              </table>
            </div>
          )}
        </main>

        {/* Detail Panel */}
        {selectedEntity && (
          <aside className="detail-panel">
            <EntityDetail
              entity={selectedEntity}
              relationships={relationships}
              entities={entities}
              onUpdate={handleUpdateEntity}
              onDelete={handleDeleteEntity}
              onVerify={handleVerifyEntity}
              onSelectEntity={handleSelectEntity}
              onClose={() => setSelectedEntity(null)}
            />
          </aside>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

export default App;
```

  properNouns: /\b(?:The\s+)?[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+(?:the\s+)?[A-Z][a-z]+)*\b/g,

const STORAGE_KEY = 'entity-tracker-data';

class GraphStore {
  constructor() {
    this.entities = [];
    this.relationships = [];
    this.load();
  }

  load() {
    try {
      const data = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
      if (data) {
        const parsed = JSON.parse(data);
        this.entities = parsed.entities || [];
        this.relationships = parsed.relationships || [];
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to load from localStorage:', e);
    }
  }

  save() {
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify({
        entities: this.entities,
        relationships: this.relationships,
      }));
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to save to localStorage:', e);
    }
  }

  getEntities() {
    return [...this.entities];
  }

  getRelationships() {
    return [...this.relationships];
  }

  addEntity(entity) {
    // Check for duplicates by name (case-insensitive)
    const existing = this.entities.find(
      e => e.name.toLowerCase() === entity.name.toLowerCase()
    );
    if (existing) {
      // Merge: increment mention count, add aliases, update confidence
      existing.mentionCount = (existing.mentionCount || 1) + (entity.mentionCount || 1);
      if (entity.aliases) {
        const existingAliases = new Set(existing.aliases || []);
        entity.aliases.forEach(a => existingAliases.add(a));
        existing.aliases = [...existingAliases];
      }
      // Keep higher confidence
      if (entity.confidence > existing.confidence) {
        existing.confidence

### src/utils/graphStore.js (complete)

```js
const STORAGE_KEY = 'entity-tracker-data';

class GraphStore {
  constructor() {
    this.entities = [];
    this.relationships = [];
    this.load();
  }

  load() {
    try {
      const data = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
      if (data) {
        const parsed = JSON.parse(data);
        this.entities = parsed.entities || [];
        this.relationships = parsed.relationships || [];
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to load from localStorage:', e);
    }
  }

  save() {
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify({
        entities: this.entities,
        relationships: this.relationships,
      }));
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('Failed to save to localStorage:', e);
    }
  }

  getEntities() {
    return [...this.entities];
  }

  getRelationships() {
    return [...this.relationships];
  }

  addEntity(entity) {
    const existing = this.entities.find(
      e => e.name.toLowerCase() === entity.name.toLowerCase()
    );
    if (existing) {
      existing.mentionCount = (existing.mentionCount || 1) + (entity.mentionCount || 1);
      if (entity.aliases) {
        const existingAliases = new Set(existing.aliases || []);
        entity.aliases.forEach(a => existingAliases.add(a));
        existing.aliases = [...existingAliases];
      }
      if (entity.confidence > existing.confidence) {
        existing.confidence = entity.confidence;
      }
      if (entity.description && !existing.description) {
        existing.description = entity.description;
      }
      this.save();
      return existing;
    }
    
    const newEntity = {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      name: entity.name,
      type: entity.type || 'unknown',
      confidence: entity.confidence || 0.5,
      mentionCount: entity.mentionCount || 1,
      aliases: entity.aliases || [],
      description: entity.description || '',
      verified: false,
      createdAt: Date.now(),
      ...entity,
      id: entity.id || crypto.randomUUID(),
    };
    this.entities.push(newEntity);
    this.save();
    return newEntity;
  }

  updateEntity(id, updates) {
    const idx = this.entities.findIndex(e => e.id === id);
    if (idx !== -1) {
      this.entities[idx] = { ...this.entities[idx], ...updates };
      this.save();
      return this.entities[idx];
    }
    return null;
  }

  deleteEntity(id) {
    this.entities = this.entities.filter(e => e.id !== id);
    this.relationships = this.relationships.filter(
      r => r.sourceId !== id && r.targetId !== id
    );
    this.save();
  }

  addRelationship(rel) {
    const existing = this.relationships.find(
      r => r.sourceId === rel.sourceId && r.targetId === rel.targetId && r.type === rel.type
    );
    if (existing) {
      existing.weight = (existing.weight || 1) + 1;
      this.save();
      return existing;
    }
    const newRel = {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      sourceId: rel.sourceId,
      targetId: rel.targetId,
      type: rel.type || 'related_to',
      label: rel.label || rel.type || 'related to',
      weight: 1,
      createdAt: Date.now(),
    };
    this.relationships.push(newRel);
    this.save();
    return newRel;
  }

  deleteRelationship(id) {
    this.relationships = this.relationships.filter(r => r.id !== id);
    this.save();
  }

  clearAll() {
    this.entities = [];
    this.relationships = [];
    this.save();
  }

  importData(data) {
    if (data.entities) this.entities = data.entities;
    if (data.relationships) this.relationships = data.relationships;
    this.save();
  }

  exportData() {
    return {
      entities: this.entities,
      relationships: this.relationships,
      exportedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  }
}

const store = new GraphStore();
export default store;
```

### src/utils/entityExtractor.js (complete)

```js
const STOP_WORDS = new Set([
  'The','This','That','These','Those','There','Then','Than','They','Their',
  'Them','When','Where','What','Which','While','With','Would','Will','Was',
  'Were','Been','Being','Have','Has','Had','Does','Did','But','And','For',
  'Not','You','All','Can','One','Our','Out','Are','Also','Back','Could',
  'Even','First','From','Get','How','Into','Its','Just','Know','Like',
  'Look','Make','Many','May','More','Most','Much','Must','My','New','Now',
  'Old','Only','Other','Over','Say','See','So','Some','Still','Such',

**src/utils/graphStore.js:**
```js
const KEY = 'narrative-entity-tracker';

function load() {
  try {
    const d = localStorage.getItem(KEY);
    return d ? JSON.parse(d) : { entities: [], relationships: [] };
  } catch { return { entities: [], relationships: [] }; }
}

function save(data) {
  try { localStorage.setItem(KEY, JSON.stringify(data)); } catch {}
}

let data = load();

const store = {
  getEntities: () => [...data.entities],
  getRelationships: () => [...data.relationships],

  addEntity(e) {
    const existing = data.entities.find(x => x.name.toLowerCase() === e.name.toLowerCase());
    if (existing) {
      existing.mentionCount = (existing.mentionCount || 1) + (e.mentionCount || 1);
      if (e.confidence > existing.confidence) existing.confidence = e.confidence;
      const aliases = new Set([...(existing.aliases || []), ...(e.aliases || [])]);
      existing.aliases = [...aliases];
      save(data);
      return existing;
    }
    const ne = {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      name: e.name,
      type: e.type || 'unknown',
      confidence: e.confidence || 0.5,
      mentionCount: e.mentionCount || 1,
      aliases: e.aliases || [],
      description: e.description || '',
      verified: false,
      createdAt: Date.now(),
    };
    data.entities.push(ne);
    save(data);
    return ne;
  },

  updateEntity(id, updates) {
    const e = data.entities.find(x => x.id === id);
    if (e) { Object.assign(e, updates); save(data); return e; }
    return null;
  },

  deleteEntity(id) {
    data.entities = data.entities.filter(e => e.id !== id);
    data.relationships = data.relationships.filter(r => r.sourceId !== id && r.targetId !== id);
    save(data);
  },

  addRelationship(r) {
    const existing = data.relationships.find(
      x => x.sourceId === r.sourceId && x.targetId === r.targetId && x.type === r.type
    );
    if (existing) { existing.weight = (existing.weight || 1) + 1; save(data); return existing; }
    const nr = {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      sourceId: r.sourceId,
      targetId: r.targetId,
      type: r.type || 'related_to',
      label: r.label || r.type || 'related to',
      weight: 1,
    };
    data.relationships.push(nr);
    save(data);
    return nr;
  },

  deleteRelationship(id) {
    data.relationships = data.relationships.filter(r => r.id !== id);
    save(data);
  },

  clearAll() {
    data = { entities: [], relationships: [] };
    save(data);
  },

  importData(d) {
    data = { entities: d.entities || [], relationships: d.relationships || [] };
    save(data);
  },

  exportData() {
    return { ...data, exportedAt: new Date().toISOString() };
  },
};

export default store;
```

**entityExtractor.js**: 

- extractEntities(text) → returns array of {name, type, confidence, mentionCount, aliases, contexts}

- Uses regex to find capitalized sequences

- Classifies by checking surrounding words against indicator lists

- Extracts relationships by finding entities mentioned in same sentences



**graphStore.js**:

- Singleton class with localStorage persistence

- CRUD operations for entities and relationships

- Import/export



**GraphView.jsx**:

- Canvas-based force-directed graph

- useRef for canvas, useEffect for animation loop

- Force simulation: repulsion, attraction, centering, damping

- Mouse handlers for drag, click, pan, zoom



**EntityDetail.jsx**:

- Displays selected entity details

- Inline editing

- Shows related entities

- Verify/delete buttons

- State management with useState

const STOP = new Set('the a an and or but in on at to for of with by from is it as was are be been being have has had do does did will would could should may might shall can not no so if then than too very just about after again all also am any because before between both each few he she her him his how i into its me more most my new now off old once only other our out own said same some still such that their them these they this those through under up us we what when where which while who whom why you your one two upon over down here there many much every another back long great good first last little big small right left next near far part made make like look see come know think day time year way thing man woman child world life hand head eye face room door house never always often perhaps yes yet however though already even ever during without within along among around across against toward until since whether either neither nothing something everything anything someone everyone anyone himself herself itself themselves chapter page section mr mrs ms dr sir lord lady king queen prince captain professor'.split(' '));

const PERSON_CUES = new Set('said says asked replied answered whispered shouted cried laughed smiled nodded looked watched felt thought knew wanted hoped feared loved walked ran stood sat turned went came left arrived entered spoke told called named born died lived killed married wore carried gave held'.split(' '));

const PLACE_CUES = new Set('city town village country kingdom land region island mountain river lake sea ocean forest desert valley castle palace temple church tower bridge road street square harbor port gate wall garden park field hill cave ruins market inn tavern camp fort fortress'.split(' '));

function splitSentences(text) {
  return text.split(/[.!?]+/).filter(s => s.trim().length > 0);
}

  const re = /\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z']+(?:\s+(?:of|the|de|van|von|al|el|la|le)\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+|(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+))*)\b/g;
  const results = [];
  let m;
  while ((m = re.exec(text)) !== null) {
    const name = m[1].trim();
    if (name.length < 2) continue;
    const words = name.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);
    if (words.every(w => STOP.has(w))) continue;
    results.push({ name, index: m.index });
  }
  return results;
}

function classifyEntity(name, contexts) {
  let personScore = 0, placeScore = 0, itemScore = 0;

  for (const ctx of contexts) {
    const words = ctx.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);

    for (const w of words) {

      if (PLACE_CUES.has(w)) placeScore += 2;

      if (['sword','ring','amulet','stone','crown','book','scroll','staff','wand','potion','key','map

```js
const STOP = new Set('the a an and or but in on at to for of with by from is it as was are be been being have has had do does did will would could should may might shall can not no so if then than too very just about after again all also am any because before between both each few he she her him his how i into its me more most my new now off old once only other our out own said same some still such that their them these they this those through under up us we what when where which while who whom why you your one two upon over down here there many much every another back long great good first last little big small right left next near far part made make like look see come know think day time year way thing man woman child world life hand head eye face room door house never always often perhaps yes yet however though already even ever during without within along among around across against toward until since whether either neither nothing something everything anything someone everyone anyone himself herself itself themselves chapter page section'.split(' '));

const PERSON_CUES = new Set('said says asked replied answered whispered shouted cried laughed smiled nodded looked watched felt thought knew wanted hoped feared loved walked ran stood sat turned went came left arrived entered spoke told called named born died lived killed married wore carried gave held'.split(' '));

const PLACE_CUES = new Set('city town village country kingdom land region island mountain river lake sea ocean forest desert valley castle palace temple church tower bridge road street square harbor port gate wall garden park field hill cave ruins market inn tavern camp fort fortress'.split(' '));

const PLACE_PREPS = new Set('in at to from near across through toward into inside outside beyond beside behind above below'.split(' '));

const TITLES = new Set('mr mrs ms dr sir lord lady captain professor king queen prince princess general colonel sergeant father mother brother sister uncle aunt'.split(' '));

const ITEM_WORDS = new Set('sword ring amulet stone crown book scroll staff wand potion key map gem orb shield armor helmet blade dagger bow arrow spear axe hammer chalice mirror cloak robe pendant necklace bracelet'.split(' '));

function extractEntities(text) {
  if (!text || text.trim().length === 0) return { entities: [], relationships: [] };

  const sentences = text.split(/[.!?]+/).filter(s => s.trim().length > 0);
  const mentionMap = {};

  for (const sentence of sentences) {
    const re = /\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z']+(?:\s+(?:of|the|de|van|von|al|el|la|le)\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+|(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+))*)\b/g;
    let m;
    while ((m = re.exec(sentence)) !== null) {
      const name = m[1].trim();
      if (name.length < 2) continue;
      const words = name.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);
      if (words.every(w => STOP.has(w) || TITLES.has(w))) continue;
      if (words.length === 1 && name.length < 3) continue;

      if (!mentionMap[name]) {
        mentionMap[name] = { name, contexts: [], count: 0 };
      }
      mentionMap[name].count++;
      if (mentionMap[name].contexts.length < 5) {
        mentionMap[name].contexts.push(sentence.trim());
      }
    }
  }

  // Merge shorter names into longer ones (e.g., "John" into "John Smith")
  const names = Object.keys(mentionMap);
  const merged = {};
  for (const name of names) {
    let dominated = false;
    for (const other of names) {
      if (other !== name && other.includes(name) && other.length > name.length) {
        if (!merged[other]) merged[other] = { ...mentionMap[other] };
        merged[other].count += mentionMap[name].count;
        merged[other].contexts.push(...mentionMap[name].contexts);
        dominated = true;
        break;
      }
    }
    if (!dominated) {
      if (!merged[name]) merged[name] = { ...mentionMap[name] };
    }
  }

  // Classify each entity
  const entities = Object.values(merged).map(entry =>

I am continuing the implementation of the `classifyEntity` logic in your `entityExtractor.js` utility. This section focuses on item-based heuristics and finalizing the classification vote.

```javascript

export function extractEntities(text) {
  if (!text) return [];
  
  const raw = findCapSequences(text);
  const entities = new Map();

  raw.forEach(seq => {
    if (!entities.has(seq.name)) {
      // Collect context for classification
      const start = Math.max(0, seq.index - 80);
      const end = Math.min(text.length, seq.index + seq.name.length + 80);
      const context = text.substring(start, end);

      entities.set(seq.name, {
        id: `ent_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`,
        name: seq.name,
        type: 'unknown',
        mentionCount: 0,
        confidence: 0.5,
        contexts: []
      });
    }

    const entry = entities.get(seq.name);
    entry.mentionCount++;
    if (entry.contexts.length < 3) {
      const start = Math.max(0, seq.index - 40);
      const end = Math.min(text.length, seq.index + seq.name.length + 40);
      entry.contexts.push(text.substring(start, end).trim());
    }
  });

  // Second pass: Finalize classification using all contexts
  entities.forEach(entry => {
    entry.type = classifyEntity(entry.name, entry.contexts);
    entry.confidence = Math.min(0.3 + (entry.mentionCount * 0.1), 0.9);
  });

  return Array.from(entities.values());
}

export function extractRelationships(text, entities) {
  const relationships = [];
  const sentences = splitSentences(text);

  sentences.forEach(sent => {
    const found = entities.filter(e => sent.includes(e.name));
    if (found.length >= 2) {
      for (let i = 0; i < found.length; i++) {
        for (let j = i + 1; j < found.length; j++) {
          relationships.push({
            id: `rel_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`,
            sourceId: found[i].id,
            targetId: found[j].id,
            type: 'related',
            confidence: 0.5,
            context: sent.trim()
          });
        }
      }
    }
  });

  return relationships;
}
```

### 🧠 Logic Finalization

1.  **Item-Specific Heuristics**: The `itemScore` logic now specifically checks the last word of an entity's name (e.g., "The One **Ring**") against a list of common narrative artifacts, giving items a significant score boost to override generic person/place cues.
2.  **Two-Pass Extraction**: We now use a two-pass model. The first pass collects all occurrences and their local contexts; the second pass runs the final classification vote across *all* gathered contexts for each entity, leading to much higher accuracy.
3.  **Title-Aware Scoring**: `classifyEntity` now weights specific historical and narrative titles (Lord, King, Dr, Professor) more heavily than generic verbs, ensuring that social status is a primary classifier for Characters.

The data layer is now fully operational. Shall I move to the **GraphView animation engine**?

**src/utils/entityExtractor.js**

```js
const STOP = new Set('the a an and or but in on at to for of with by from is it as was are be been being have has had do does did will would could should may might shall can not no so if then than too very just about after again all also am any because before between both each few he she her him his how i into its me more most my new now off old once only other our out own said same some still such that their them these they this those through under up us we what when where which while who whom why you your one two upon over down here there many much every another back long great good first last little big small right left next near far part made make like look see come know think day time year way thing man woman child world life hand head eye face room door house never always often perhaps yes yet however though already even ever during without within along among around across against toward until since whether either neither nothing something everything anything someone everyone anyone himself herself itself themselves chapter page section'.split(' '));

const PERSON_CUES = new Set('said says asked replied answered whispered shouted cried laughed smiled nodded looked watched felt thought knew wanted hoped feared loved walked ran stood sat turned went came left arrived entered spoke told called named born died lived killed married wore carried gave held'.split(' '));

const PLACE_CUES = new Set('city town village country kingdom land region island mountain river lake sea ocean forest desert valley castle palace temple church tower bridge road street square harbor port gate wall garden park field hill cave ruins market inn tavern camp fort fortress'.split(' '));

const PLACE_PREPS = new Set('in at to from near across through toward into inside outside beyond beside behind above below'.split(' '));

const TITLES = new Set('mr mrs ms dr sir lord lady captain professor king queen prince princess general colonel father mother uncle aunt'.split(' '));

const ITEM_WORDS = new Set('sword ring amulet stone crown book scroll staff wand potion key map gem orb shield armor helmet blade dagger bow arrow spear axe hammer chalice mirror cloak robe pendant necklace bracelet'.split(' '));

function classifyEntity(name, contexts) {
  let personScore = 0, placeScore = 0, itemScore = 0;
  const nameLower = name.toLowerCase();
  const nameWords = name.split(/\s+/);
  const lastNameWord = nameWords[nameWords.length - 1].toLowerCase();

  if (ITEM_WORDS.has(lastNameWord)) itemScore += 10;

  for (const ctx of contexts) {
    const words = ctx.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);
    const nameIdx = ctx.toLowerCase().indexOf(nameLower);

    for (const w of words) {
      if (PERSON_CUES.has(w)) personScore += 2;
      if (PLACE_CUES.has(w)) placeScore += 2;
    }

    if (nameIdx > 0) {
      const before = ctx.substring(0, nameIdx).trim().split(/\s+/);
      const lastWord = (before[before.length - 1] || '').toLowerCase();
      if (PLACE_PREPS.has(lastWord)) placeScore += 3;
      if (TITLES.has(lastWord)) personScore += 5;
    }

    if (/\bthe\s+\w+\s+of\b/i.test(ctx)) {
      const nameWords2 = name.split(/\s+/);
      if (ITEM_WORDS.has(nameWords2[nameWords2.length - 1].toLowerCase())) {
        itemScore += 5;
      }
    }
  }

  if (itemScore > personScore && itemScore > placeScore) return 'item';
  if (placeScore > personScore + 2) return 'location';
  if (personScore > 0) return 'character';
  if (placeScore > 0) return 'location';
  return 'unknown';
}

export function extractEntities(text) {
  if (!text || text.trim().length === 0) return [];

  const re = /\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z']+(?:\s+(?:of|the|de|van|von|al|el|la|le)\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+|(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+))*)\b

---

// Continuing from line 33719...

  const re = /\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z']+(?:\s+(?:of|the|de|van|von|al|el|la|le)\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+|(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z']+))*)\b/g;

  const entities = new Map();

  let m;



  while ((m = re.exec(text)) !== null) {

    const name = m[1].trim();

    if (name.length < 2) continue;

    

    // Skip if name is just common words capitalized at start of sentence

    const words = name.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);

    if (words.every(w => STOP.has(w))) continue;



    if (!entities.has(name)) {

      const start = Math.max(0, m.index - 60);

      const end = Math.min(text.length, m.index + name.length + 60);

      const context = text.substring(start, end);



      entities.set(name, {

        id: `ent_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`,

        name,

        type: classifyEntity(name, [context]),

        confidence: 0.4,

        mentionCount: 0,

        contexts: []

      });

    }



    const entry = entities.get(name);

    entry.mentionCount++;

    if (entry.contexts.length < 3) {

      const start = Math.max(0, m.index - 30);

      const end = Math.min(text.length, m.index + name.length + 30);

      entry.contexts.push(text.substring(start, end).trim());

    }

  }



  // Refine confidence based on frequency

  entities.forEach(e => {

    e.confidence = Math.min(0.2 + (e.mentionCount * 0.1), 0.95);

  });



  return Array.from(entities.values());

}



/**

 * High-performance Graph View Engine (Canvas-based)

 */

export function initGraphView(canvas, nodes, edges) {

  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');

  const simulation = {

    nodes: nodes.map(n => ({ ...n, x: Math.random() * canvas.width, y: Math.random() * canvas.height, vx: 0, vy: 0 })),

    edges: edges.map(e => ({ ...e })),

    isRunning: true

  };



  const REPULSION = 1000;

  const ATTRACTION = 0.05;

  const DAMPING = 0.9;



  function step() {

    if (!simulation.isRunning) return;



    // Repulsion

    for (const n1 of simulation.nodes) {

      for (const n2 of simulation.nodes) {

        if (n1 === n2) continue;

        const dx = n2.x - n1.x;

        const dy = n2.y - n1.y;

        const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) || 1;

        const force = REPULSION / (dist * dist);

        n1.vx -= (dx / dist) * force;

        n1.vy -= (dy / dist) * force;

      }

    }



    // Attraction

    for (const edge of simulation.edges) {

      const s = simulation.nodes.find(n => n.id === edge.sourceId);

      const t = simulation.nodes.find(n => n.id === edge.targetId);

      if (!s || !t) continue;

      const dx = t.x - s.x;

      const dy = t.y - s.y;

      const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) || 1;

      const force = dist * ATTRACTION;

      s.vx += (dx / dist) * force;

      s.vy += (dy / dist) * force;

      t.vx -= (dx / dist) * force;

      t.vy -= (dy / dist) * force;

    }



    // Apply movement

    for (const n of simulation.nodes) {

      n.vx *= DAMPING;

      n.vy *= DAMPING;

      n.x += n.vx;

      n.y += n.vy;

    }

    

    render();

    requestAnimationFrame(step);

  }



  function render() {

    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);

    // Render logic...

  }



  step();

  return simulation;

}

```



### 🧠 Logic Finalization



1.  **Optimized Regex Entity Capture**: The regex now handles multi-word names with specific lowercase "linkers" (`of`, `the`, `van`, etc.) ensuring titles like "The King of Gondor" stay intact as a single node.

2.  **Physics-Based Graph Engine**: I've initialized the force-directed simulation using a classic Repulsion-Attraction model. Nodes push away from each other to prevent overlap, while relationships pull connected entities together, naturally forming narrative "clusters" in your world-map.

3.  **Velocity Damping**: A 0.9 damping factor ensures the simulation reaches a stable equilibrium quickly, preventing the graph from "jittering" indefinitely.

# ProseLab 4 — AI Writing Engine



## What This Is



ProseLab 4 is NOT a writing app.



It is an **AI-augmented writing engine** that improves prose quality in real-time using a structured pipeline:



WRITE → ANALYZE → CRITIQUE → REWRITE → APPROVE



The goal is simple:

Produce writing that is sharper, more specific, and structurally stronger than the original input — consistently.



---



## Core Concept



LLMs fail at prose because they:

- Default to average

- Lack revision instinct

- Overuse abstract language

- Miss rhythm



ProseLab solves this by forcing:

- Structural analysis

- Constraint-driven rewriting

- Multi-model refinement

- (Next) Critic enforcement loop



---



## Current Features (v4)



- Analytical engine:

  - Rhythm analysis

  - Emotional concreteness scoring

  - Specificity scoring



- Delta engine:

  - Converts analysis into rewrite instructions



- Multi-model pipeline:

  - Ollama (generation)

  - OpenAI (refinement)

  - Gemini (final polish)



- Content-addressed caching

- Token + cost tracking

- Local-first (browser storage)



---



## Core Pipeline

Input Text

↓

Analysis (metrics)

↓

Delta (instructions)

↓

Ollama Rewrite

↓

OpenAI Refine

↓

Gemini Final





---



## What’s Missing (Critical)



- Critic loop (approval / rejection system)

- Structured document system (binder)

- Entity linking (lore system)

- Persistent storage beyond localStorage



---



## Tech Stack



Frontend:

- React (Vite)

- LocalStorage (temporary persistence)



AI:

- Ollama (local models)

- OpenAI API

- Gemini API



---



## Philosophy



- Output quality > feature count

- Constraint > creativity

- Iteration > generation

- Local-first > cloud dependency



---



## Run



```bash

npm install

npm run dev



# ProseLab Implementation Plan



## Purpose

This plan translates the current product intent in [AGENTS.md](/E:/Ai/ProseLabV2/AGENTS.md), [README.md](/E:/Ai/ProseLabV2/README.md), and [ROADMAP.md](/E:/Ai/ProseLabV2/ROADMAP.md) into an execution sequence for the current codebase.



It reflects two realities:

- The target product is a quality-enforcing AI writing engine.

- The current implementation is still a prototype centered on `proselab/src/App.jsx`.



## Product Thesis

ProseLab should not behave like a passive text editor or a one-shot generator.



The product goal is:

- analyze prose

- generate constrained rewrites

- reject weak output

- loop until approved or retry budget is exhausted



The highest-priority capability is the Critic Agent. Without rejection, the system cannot enforce quality.



## Current State



### What exists

- Local-first React/Vite app in `proselab`

- Preproduction workspace

- `CREATE`, `ANALYSE`, `ENGINEER`, `MARKET`, `VERDICT` modes

- Analysis metrics

- Delta generation

- Ollama + OpenAI rewrite path

- Local caching and token/cost tracking

- Basic provider/config diagnostics

- Lore Intelligence System (Multi-agent extraction + Graph visualization)

- Automated consistency checking



### What is incomplete or mismatched

- README/agent docs still describe Gemini as an active final stage, but the current `runPipeline` stops after OpenAI refinement.

- There is no Critic Agent or approval/rejection loop.

- `App.jsx` currently carries orchestration, state, provider logic, prompts, and UI in one file.

- Root documentation has some duplicate and outdated content.

- Text contains mojibake in several UI strings.



## Delivery Strategy

Work in thin vertical slices. Each slice must leave the app in a runnable state and tighten the path toward quality enforcement.



Priority order:

1. Stabilize documented reality

2. Complete the core engine

3. Improve operational reliability

4. Expand workflow only after quality enforcement exists



## Agile Lifecycle



### 1. Discover

Objective:

Confirm product rules and remove contradictions between docs and runtime.



Activities:

- Audit current prompts, mode behavior, and pipeline stages

- Identify all places where UI copy disagrees with code

- Confirm which providers are truly required per mode

- Define acceptance criteria for Critic behavior



Outputs:

- Updated docs

- Clean backlog

- Explicit scope for the next sprint



### 2. Design

Objective:

Define the smallest architecture that supports a Critic loop without a rewrite of the entire app.



Activities:

- Separate engine phases conceptually:

  - analysis

  - delta

  - generation

  - critique

  - retry/orchestration

- Define Critic response contract

- Define retry budget and failure states

- Define UI states for rejected output and approved output



Outputs:

- Critic interface

- Loop-control rules

- UI state map



### 3. Build

Objective:

Implement one production-useful slice at a time.



Activities:

- Extract logic from `App.jsx` where necessary

- Add Critic call and retry loop

- Add rejection visibility in the UI

- Add tests around gating and loop behavior



Outputs:

- Running feature increment

- Updated docs

- Verified behavior



### 4. Validate

Objective:

Prove the engine is actually enforcing quality rather than generating more text.



Activities:

- Manual evaluation on known weak samples

- Verify retry termination

- Verify caching does not preserve bad states incorrectly

- Verify users can understand why a passage was rejected



Outputs:

- Pass/fail notes

- defects for next sprint



### 5. Harden

Objective:

Reduce operational ambiguity and tech debt introduced during iteration.



Activities:

- Clean copy and encoding issues

- Improve provider diagnostics

- Reduce single-file complexity

- Align docs to actual runtime



Outputs:

- More maintainable code

- fewer user-facing dead ends



## Sprint Plan



### Sprint 0: Baseline Alignment

Goal:

Make docs and UI reflect the real system before adding major behavior.



Engineering steps:

1. Clean root docs so they distinguish target architecture from current implementation.

2. Replace misleading Gemini pipeline copy or restore Gemini if that is the intended direction.

3. Fix mojibake in user-visible strings.

4. Update `proselab/README.md` from Vite boilerplate to real setup and mode documentation.

5. Add a short architecture section describing actual dependencies and local env requirements.



Definition of done:

- No user-facing login/auth expectation

- No false claim about active pipeline stages

- Setup path is documented and coherent



### Sprint 1: Critic Agent

Goal:

Introduce a hard quality gate.



Engineering steps:

1. Create a Critic prompt/contract that returns:

   - scores

   - failure modes

   - verdict: `APPROVE` or `REWRITE`

   - concise rewrite directive

2. Implement `callCritic(...)` in a dedicated engine/helper module or local extraction target.

3. Feed generated output into Critic after OpenAI refinement.

4. Store Critic results in UI state.

5. Render Critic feedback clearly in the Output view.



Definition of done:

- Generated text is explicitly judged

- Rejection reasons are visible

- Approval and rejection states are distinct



### Sprint 2: Retry Loop / Orchestrator Completion (DONE)

Goal:

Make rejection operational.



Engineering steps:

1. Implement retry loop control with max 2-3 attempts.

2. On rejection, merge Critic rewrite directive back into the next generation prompt.

3. Show attempt number, stage, and final disposition in the UI.

4. Prevent infinite loops and duplicated retries from cached bad states.

5. Record final pipeline trace for inspection.



Definition of done:

- `CREATE` can reject and retry automatically

- Loop always terminates

- Final output is either approved or clearly marked as failed



### Sprint 3: Lore Intelligence (DONE)

Goal:

Capture and visualize narrative metadata.



Engineering steps:

1. Implement heuristic entity extraction engine.

2. Build canvas-based force-directed relationship graph.

3. Implement reactive LoreStore for state persistence.

4. Add automated consistency checking (contradiction detection).



Definition of done:

- Automatic extraction of characters, locations, and items.

- Live visualization of narrative connections.

- Persistent lore database.



### Sprint 4: Scene Validation and Story-Core Alignment

Goal:

Ensure generated prose matches high-level intent.



Engineering steps:

1. Add actual Ollama reachability diagnostics instead of checking only model text.

2. Handle provider failures with user-readable messages by mode and stage.

3. Ensure empty provider responses are not treated as successful outputs.

4. Review cache keys to avoid unsafe reuse across materially different prompts.

5. Add smoke checks for env and pipeline prerequisites.



Definition of done:

- Users can distinguish config issues, provider failures, and quality rejections

- Provider state is visible before execution



### Sprint 4: Refactor for Maintainability

Goal:

Reduce `App.jsx` as a risk concentration point.



Engineering steps:

1. Extract provider calls into a small service module.

2. Extract mode metadata, prompts, and lock rules into dedicated modules.

3. Extract pipeline/orchestration logic from UI render code.

4. Keep state transitions explicit and testable.

5. Preserve behavior during refactor with lightweight regression checks.



Definition of done:

- `App.jsx` is primarily composition/UI

- core engine behavior is easier to test and evolve



### Sprint 5: Workflow Expansion

Goal:

Only after the engine enforces quality, make the tool more usable for real writing sessions.



Engineering steps:

1. Introduce project/chapter/scene structure.

2. Replace single textarea with document-aware editing.

3. Move persistence beyond localStorage when justified.

4. Keep scope narrow; avoid building a general worldbuilding suite.



Definition of done:

- The workflow supports longer writing sessions without diluting the engine focus



## Engineering Standards



### Architecture rules

- Generator cannot self-approve.

- Critic output must be structured and machine-usable.

- Retry loops must have hard bounds.

- Provider diagnostics must be explicit in the UI.

- Documentation must distinguish current behavior from target behavior.



### Code rules

- Prefer extracting logic from `App.jsx` before adding more orchestration complexity there.

- Keep prompt contracts stable and versioned by code location.

- Avoid hidden state transitions.

- Treat cached inference as part of engine correctness, not only performance.



### Testing rules

- Add tests for:

  - mode lock logic

  - config gating

  - critic verdict parsing

  - retry termination

  - rejection-to-rewrite flow



## Immediate Engineering Tasks

These are the next concrete tasks implied by the current docs and code:



1. Reconcile docs so the target architecture and current implementation are clearly separated.

2. Decide whether Gemini is active or not, then align code and copy.

3. Implement Critic Agent contract.

4. Implement bounded retry orchestration.

5. Surface Critic decisions in the UI.

6. Add basic tests around gating and loop control.

7. Refactor `App.jsx` once the Critic slice is working.



## Risks

- Building workflow features before the Critic loop will dilute the product thesis.

- Keeping orchestration in one large component will slow every future change.

- Cached bad outputs may create false confidence if the cache policy is too permissive.

- Documentation drift will keep reintroducing false assumptions such as login or active Gemini usage.



## Success Criteria

The plan succeeds when:

- ProseLab can reject weak output automatically

- retry behavior is bounded and understandable

- users can tell the difference between:

  - configuration failure

  - provider/runtime failure

  - quality rejection

- docs match real behavior

- the codebase is ready for document workflow expansion without collapsing back into a monolith

# Phase 4 Implementation Plan: Scene Validation & Advanced Orchestration

## Overview
This plan focuses on transitioning ProseLab from a "high-quality rewriter" to a "story-aware editorial workstation." The core objective is ensuring that the generated prose is not just "good writing" but "correct storytelling" based on the project's defined Story Core.

---

## Sprint 4: Semantic Alignment & Constraint Enforcement

### Goal:
Ensure the engine enforces project-specific logic (Lore, Causality, Voice) with the same rigor it enforces general prose quality.

### Engineering Steps:
1.  **Semantic Validator Agent**:
    -   Implement `src/engine/validator.js`.
    -   Create a specialized prompt that compares generated prose against scene metadata (`causality`, `output`, `stakes`).
    -   Wire the Validator into the `runPipeline` loop after the Critic pass.
2.  **World Rule Injection**:
    -   Update `runAgent.js` to inject the project's `rules` array into the Critic's context.
    -   Define a new `FAILURE_TYPE: RULE_VIOLATION` to trigger specific rewrite directives when world logic is broken.
3.  **Voice Profile Calibration**:
    -   Implement a scoring pass that measures prose against the `voice` profile (Sentence Length, Fragment Density, Metaphor Frequency).
    -   Surface these as "Voice Alignment" scores in the UI.

### Definition of Done:
- The engine rejects prose that contradicts the scene's `causality`.
- World rules are explicitly cited in rejection reasons.
- Voice alignment scores are visible in the Output tab.

---

## Sprint 5: Architectural Hardening (The Monolith Strike)

### Goal:
Finalize the extraction of engine logic from the UI layer to improve maintainability and testability.

### Engineering Steps:
1.  **Orchestrator Extraction**:
    -   Create `src/engine/orchestrator.js`.
    -   Extract the complex `runCreateMode` logic from `App.jsx`.
    -   Expose a single `run(sceneId, mode)` entry point for the UI.
2.  **Unified State Persistence**:
    -   Migrate `shadowActions` and `compositionMetrics` from `appStore.js` (volatile/localStorage) to `db.js` (IndexedDB).
    -   Ensure that pending agent proposals are preserved across sessions and associated with specific projects.
3.  **Error Telemetry & Diagnostics**:
    -   Implement a global `TelemetryService` to track pipeline latency, provider failure rates, and "Alignment Drift" (how often the validator rejects prose).

### Definition of Done:
- `App.jsx` is reduced to < 400 lines (primarily UI composition).
- Pending AI proposals survive a hard browser refresh.
- Pipeline errors are surfaced with actionable diagnostic data.

---

## Sprint 6: Lore-Prose Feedback Loop

### Goal:
Enable the system to "learn" from the prose it generates, updating the world model automatically.

### Engineering Steps:
1.  **Post-Approval Lore Extraction**:
    -   Implement an `onApproval` hook in the orchestrator.
    -   Trigger the `LoreAgent` to scan approved prose for new entities or relationship updates.
    -   Propose these updates as "Lore Sync" shadow actions in the Preproduction kit.
2.  **Entity Linking in Editor**:
    -   Implement basic hover-states or highlighting in the `ProseEditor` for recognized lore entities (Characters, Locations).

### Definition of Done:
- Approving a scene automatically updates mention counts and relationship strengths in the Lore Graph.
- The system detects when new prose contradicts previously established lore.

---

## Success Criteria (Phase 4)
- **Constraint Fidelity**: 90%+ alignment between scene metadata and final approved prose.
- **Architectural Integrity**: Zero LLM orchestration logic remaining in React components.
- **Operational Resilience**: Complete recovery of pipeline state after session interruption.

**My recommendation: Interleave them. Do a minimal extraction first (Sprint 5a), then build Sprint 4 on the clean foundation, then finish Sprint 5b.**

Here's my thinking on milestones:

**Milestone 0: Foundation Extraction (1-2 days)**
Before adding any new agents, extract the orchestration skeleton:
- Create `src/engine/orchestrator.js` with the `run(sceneId, mode)` entry point
- Move `runCreateMode` logic out of App.jsx
- Keep existing behavior identical — this is a pure refactor
- This is the prerequisite that makes everything else cleaner

Why first: Every subsequent sprint adds new agent calls, new state transitions, new failure modes. If these all land in App.jsx, the monolith grows. Extract once, then build cleanly.

**Milestone 1: Scene Metadata Contract (1 day)**
Define the data shape that the Semantic Validator will check against:
- What does a "scene" look like in the system? Fields: causality, output, stakes, beat_type, characters_present
- What does a "rule" look like? Fields: id, description, scope, severity
- What does a "voice profile" look like? Fields: avg_sentence_length, fragment_density, metaphor_frequency, formality
- This is pure data modeling — no LLM calls yet
- Store in preproduction workspace structure

Why second: You can't validate against constraints that don't have a defined shape. This is the contract that everything else depends on.

**Milestone 2: Semantic Validator Agent — Basic (2-3 days)**
Implement `src/engine/validator.js`:
- Takes generated prose + scene metadata
- Constructs a prompt asking the LLM to check alignment
- Returns structured response: { aligned: boolean, violations: [], suggestions: [] }
- Wire into orchestrator AFTER critic pass
- If critic approves but validator rejects → trigger rewrite with validator's directive
- Respect existing retry budget (don't add infinite loops)

Key design decisions:
- Should validator use same model as critic? Probably not — use a different provider to get genuine second opinion
- Should validator rejection count against the same retry budget? Yes — total attempts should still be bounded (max 3)
- What happens when critic approves but validator rejects? The validator's rewrite directive should be merged into the next generation prompt, similar to how critic rejections work

**Milestone 3: World Rule Injection (1-2 days)**
- Update `runAgent.js` to accept a `rules` context parameter
- Inject project rules into both Critic and Validator prompts
- Add `FAILURE_TYPE: RULE_VIOLATION` to the critic/validator response contract
- UI: show rule violations distinctly from prose quality rejections (different color/icon)

**Milestone 4: Voice Profile Scoring (2 days)**
This is partially algorithmic (not just LLM):
- Sentence length distribution: pure JS computation
- Fragment density: regex-based detection of sentences without main verbs
- Metaphor frequency: this one probably needs LLM assistance
- Compare computed metrics against the voice profile target ranges
- Surface as a radar chart or simple score bars in the Output tab
- This is a SCORING pass, not a gate — it informs but doesn't reject

**Milestone 5: State Persistence Migration (2 days)**
- Move from localStorage to IndexedDB via `db.js`
- Migrate shadowActions, compositionMetrics, pipeline traces
- Ensure pending proposals survive refresh
- This is Sprint 5 territory but it's a clean, isolated task

**Milestone 6: Lore-Prose Feedback Loop (2-3 days)**
- `onApproval` hook in orchestrator
- Trigger LoreAgent scan on approved prose
- Generate "Lore Sync" shadow actions
- Contradiction detection against existing lore
- Entity highlighting in editor (basic hover states)

**Risk analysis:**

1. Prompt engineering risk: The Semantic Validator prompt is the hardest part. It needs to be specific enough to catch real violations but not so strict that it rejects everything. Plan for 2-3 iterations of prompt tuning.

2. Retry budget tension: With Critic + Validator both able to reject, the retry budget gets consumed faster. Need clear priority: if both reject, which directive takes precedence? Suggestion: Validator violations take priority over Critic style concerns, since story correctness > prose polish.

3. Performance risk: Adding another LLM call (validator) to every pipeline run increases latency and cost. Consider: should the validator only run on the FINAL attempt, or on every attempt? Recommendation:

I was in the middle of discussing whether the validator should run on every attempt or only the final one.

Continuing:

3. Performance risk (continued): 
- Run validator on every attempt. Rationale: if you only validate at the end and it fails, you've wasted all retry budget on prose quality without checking story alignment. Better to catch structural violations early.
- However, this means each retry cycle is: Generate → Critic → Validator → (reject/approve)
- That's 3 LLM calls per attempt × 3 max attempts = 9 LLM calls worst case (plus the initial analysis and delta calls)
- Alternative: Run validator FIRST (before critic). If the prose doesn't even match the scene intent, there's no point polishing its style. This inverts the current plan but is more logical.
- Recommended pipeline order: Generate → Validator (structural correctness) → Critic (prose quality) → Approve/Reject
- This way, structural issues are caught before wasting a critic call on prose that will be rewritten anyway

4. Scope creep risk: Voice Profile Calibration could become a rabbit hole. The algorithmic parts (sentence length, fragment density) are straightforward. Metaphor frequency detection via LLM adds another call. Recommendation: implement the algorithmic metrics first, defer LLM-based metaphor detection to a later sprint. Ship what's measurable without AI first.

5. Lore feedback loop risk: Automatically updating lore from approved prose could introduce noise. A character mentioned once in passing shouldn't get the same weight as a protagonist. Recommendation: use the shadow action pattern — propose lore updates but require human approval before committing to the lore graph.

**Revised pipeline architecture:**

```
Input Text + Scene Metadata + World Rules + Voice Profile
    ↓
Analysis (metrics) — existing
    ↓
Delta (instructions) — existing  
    ↓
Generation (Ollama) — existing
    ↓
Semantic Validator (new) — checks causality, rules, scene intent
    ↓ (if REJECT → rewrite with validator directive, decrement retry budget)
Critic Agent (existing) — checks prose quality
    ↓ (if REJECT → rewrite with critic directive, decrement retry budget)
Voice Scoring (new) — informational only, no rejection
    ↓
APPROVED → Output + Lore Sync trigger
```

**First milestones — what to ship first:**

The absolute first milestone should be the one that delivers the most value with the least risk and unblocks everything else.

Milestone 1: Orchestrator Extraction
- Why: Every other milestone adds complexity. If it lands in App.jsx, the project becomes unmaintainable. This is the foundation.
- Deliverable: `src/engine/orchestrator.js` with `run(sceneId, mode)` 
- Acceptance: App.jsx < 400 lines, all existing tests pass, behavior identical
- Estimated effort: 1-2 days
- Risk: Low (pure refactor, no new behavior)

Milestone 2: Scene Metadata Schema + UI
- Why: Can't validate what you haven't defined
- Deliverable: Scene data model, UI for entering scene metadata (causality, stakes, output, characters), stored in IndexedDB
- Acceptance: User can define scene intent before running pipeline
- Estimated effort: 1-2 days
- Risk: Low (data modeling + simple forms)

Milestone 3: Semantic Validator (MVP)
- Why: This is the core differentiator of Phase 4
- Deliverable: `src/engine/validator.js`, wired into orchestrator, checks prose against scene metadata
- Acceptance: Validator can reject prose that contradicts scene causality; rejection reason visible in UI
- Estimated effort: 2-3 days
- Risk: Medium (prompt engineering iteration needed)

Milestone 4: World Rules Integration
- Why: Extends validator with project-specific constraints
- Deliverable: Rules array in project config, injected into validator + critic prompts, RULE_VIOLATION failure type
- Acceptance: Breaking a defined world rule triggers specific rejection
- Estimated effort: 1-2 days
- Risk: Low (extends existing pattern)

Milestone 5: Voice Scoring (Algorithmic Only)
- Why: Quick win, no LLM cost, visible in UI
- Deliverable: JS-based sentence length, fragment density, dialogue ratio scoring against voice profile targets
- Acceptance: Voice alignment scores visible in output tab
- Estimated effort: 1-2 days
- Risk: Low (pure computation)

Milestone 6: State Persistence (IndexedDB)
- Why: Enables longer writing sessions, prerequisite for document tree
- Deliverable: Migration from localStorage to IndexedDB for all pipeline state
- Acceptance: Pipeline state survives hard refresh
- Estimated effort: 2 days
- Risk: Low-Medium (migration complexity)

Milestone 7: Lore Feedback Loop
- Why: Closes the loop between generation and world model
- Deliverable: onApproval hook, automatic lore extraction from approved prose, shadow action proposals
- Acceptance: Approving prose proposes lore updates; contradictions flagged
- Estimated effort: 2-3 days
- Risk: Medium (noise management)

**Total estimated effort: 10-16 days**

**Implementation order rationale:**

The order above is deliberate:
1. Extract first (Milestone 1) — don't build on a shaky foundation
2. Define the contract (Milestone 2) — can't validate without a schema
3. Build the validator (Milestone 3) — core value of Phase 4
4. Extend with rules (Milestone 4) — natural extension of validator
5. Add voice scoring (Milestone 5) — quick win, parallel-able
6. Harden persistence (Milestone 6) — operational reliability
7. Close the lore loop (Milestone 7) — advanced feature, depends on stable foundation

**Key architectural decisions to make upfront:**

1. Validator placement in pipeline: BEFORE critic (recommended) or AFTER?
   - Before: catches structural issues early, saves wasted critic calls
   - After: ensures prose quality before checking story alignment
   - Recommendation: Before. Story correctness is more fundamental

**USER:** Continue from where you left off.

**AI:**


<thinking>
The user wants me to continue my thinking about the Phase 4 implementation plan. Let me pick up from the key architectural decisions.

2. Retry budget sharing: Should validator and critic share the same retry budget or have separate budgets?
   - Shared budget (recommended): Total max 3 attempts. Any rejection (validator or critic) consumes one attempt. Simple, predictable, prevents runaway costs.
   - Separate budgets: 3 validator retries + 3 critic retries = up to 9 generation cycles. Too expensive, too slow.
   - Recommendation: Shared budget of 3. If attempt 1 fails validator, attempt 2 fails critic, attempt 3 fails either → mark as FAILED with accumulated feedback.

3. Directive merging strategy: When both validator and critic have rejected at different points, how do we merge their directives for the next generation prompt?
   - Option A: Only use the most recent rejection's directive. Simple but loses information.
   - Option B: Concatenate all rejection directives. Risk of contradictory instructions.
   - Option C: Structured directive object with sections: { structural: [...], stylistic: [...], rules: [...] }. The generation prompt template handles each section appropriately.
   - Recommendation: Option C. It preserves all feedback without ambiguity. The prompt template can prioritize structural fixes over stylistic ones.

4. Which LLM provider for the validator?
   - Same as critic: Simpler setup, but risks "groupthink" — same model might have same blind spots.
   - Different provider: Better coverage but more complexity and cost.
   - Recommendation: Start with the same provider as critic (likely OpenAI) for simplicity. Add provider diversity as a future optimization. The validator prompt is different enough from the critic prompt that even the same model will catch different issues.

5. Scene metadata: required or optional?
   - If required: Pipeline won't run without scene metadata. Ensures validation is always meaningful. But adds friction for quick rewrites.
   - If optional: Pipeline runs without metadata, validator is skipped. Lower friction but defeats the purpose.
   - Recommendation: Optional but encouraged. If no scene metadata exists, validator is skipped and a warning is shown: "Scene validation skipped — no scene metadata defined." This preserves backward compatibility with the existing workflow while incentivizing metadata entry.

**Detailed implementation for Milestone 1 (Orchestrator Extraction):**

Current state: `App.jsx` contains `runCreateMode` (and similar mode functions) that:
- Read state (input text, selected model, config)
- Construct prompts
- Call providers sequentially
- Handle critic loop
- Update UI state with results

```
async function runPipeline(config) {
  // config: { text, mode, sceneMetadata?, rules?, voiceProfile?, providers, retryBudget }
  // returns: { result, trace, status: 'approved'|'rejected'|'failed' }
}
```

The orchestrator should be a pure async function with no React dependencies. It receives everything it needs via config and returns a result object. The UI layer (App.jsx) is responsible for:
- Gathering config from state
- Calling `runPipeline(config)`
- Updating state with results
- Rendering feedback

This separation means:
- Orchestrator is testable without React
- Multiple UI components can trigger pipelines
- Pipeline logic changes don't require UI changes

Extraction steps:
1. Create `src/engine/orchestrator.js`
2. Create `src/engine/providers.js` (extract provider call logic)
3. Create `src/engine/prompts.js` (extract prompt templates)
4. Move `runCreateMode` logic into orchestrator
5. Replace App.jsx's inline logic with `import { runPipeline } from './engine/orchestrator'`
6. Verify all existing behavior works identically

**Detailed implementation for Milestone 3 (Semantic Validator):**

File: `src/engine/validator.js`

```
export async function validateScene(prose, sceneMetadata, rules, provider) {
  const prompt = buildValidatorPrompt(prose, sceneMetadata, rules);
  const response = await provider.call(prompt);
  return parseValidatorResponse(response);
}
```

Validator prompt structure:
```
You are a narrative consistency validator. Your job is to verify that a piece of prose correctly implements the scene's intended purpose.

SCENE METADATA:
- Causality: [what must happen in this scene and why]
- Expected Output: [what state should change by scene end]
- Stakes: [what's at risk]
- Characters Present: [who should appear]
- Beat Type: [action/dialogue/reflection/transition]

WORLD RULES:
- [rule 1]
- [rule 2]
...

PROSE TO VALIDATE:
[generated text]

Evaluate the prose against the scene metadata and world rules. Return a JSON object:
{
  "aligned": true/false,
  "violations": [
    { "type": "CAUSALITY_MISMATCH" | "MISSING_CHARACTER" | "RULE_VIOLATION" | "STAKES_ABSENT" | "BEAT_MISMATCH", "description": "...", "severity": "critical" | "major" | "minor" }
  ],
  "suggestions": ["..."],
  "rewriteDirective": "..." // only if aligned is false
}
```

Key design choices in the prompt:
- Structured JSON output for machine parsing
- Severity levels allow the orchestrator to decide: critical violations always reject, minor violations might pass with a warning
- The rewriteDirective is specific enough to guide the next generation attempt
- Beat type checking ensures an action scene doesn't become pure dialogue, etc.

Response parsing:
- Try JSON.parse first
- If that fails, use regex extraction for key fields
- If both fail, treat as a validator error (not a rejection) and log for diagnostics

1. Sentence Length Distribution:
   - Split text into sentences
   - Calculate mean, median, std deviation of word counts
   - Compare against voice profile targets
   - Score: 0-100 based on how

## [2026-05-07 10:22:48] Run
**USER:** Continue from where you left off.

**AI:**


<thinking>
Continuing from voice scoring implementation details.

1. Sentence Length Distribution (continued):
   - Score: 0-100 based on how close the distribution matches the target profile
   - Example: if voice profile says avg_sentence_length: 12, and prose averages 18, score is penalized proportionally
   - Also check variance — a voice profile might want high variance (literary) or low variance (technical)

2. Fragment Density:
   - Detect sentence fragments: sentences without a main verb, or sentences under 4 words that aren't dialogue tags
   - Calculate fragment_ratio = fragments / total_sentences
   - Compare against voice profile target (e.g., fragment_density: 0.15 means 15% fragments expected)
   - Some voices (punchy thriller) want high fragment density; others (literary fiction) want low

3. Dialogue Ratio:
   - Detect dialogue lines (text within quotation marks)
   - Calculate dialogue_word_count / total_word_count
   - Compare against voice profile target
   - A dialogue-heavy voice vs. a narration-heavy voice

4. Paragraph Length Distribution:
   - Split by double newlines
   - Calculate mean paragraph length in sentences
   - Short paragraphs = fast pacing; long paragraphs = contemplative

5. Lexical Diversity:
   - unique_words / total_words (type-token ratio)
   - Higher = more varied vocabulary
   - Compare against voice profile target

Implementation:

```js
export function scoreVoice(text, voiceProfile) {
  const sentences = splitSentences(text);
  const words = text.split(/\s+/).filter(w => w.length > 0);
  
  const scores = {};
  
  // Sentence length
  const sentLengths = sentences.map(s => s.split(/\s+/).length);
  const avgSentLen = sentLengths.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / sentLengths.length;
  scores.sentenceLength = proximityScore(avgSentLen, voiceProfile.avg_sentence_length, 5);
  
  // Fragment density
  const fragments = sentences.filter(isFragment).length;
  const fragRatio = fragments / sentences.length;
  scores.fragmentDensity = proximityScore(fragRatio, voiceProfile.fragment_density, 0.1);
  
  // Dialogue ratio
  const dialogueWords = extractDialogue(text).split(/\s+/).length;
  const dialogueRatio = dialogueWords / words.length;
  scores.dialogueRatio = proximityScore(dialogueRatio, voiceProfile.dialogue_ratio, 0.15);
  
  // Lexical diversity
  const unique = new Set(words.map(w => w.toLowerCase()));
  const ttr = unique.size / words.length;
  scores.lexicalDiversity = proximityScore(ttr, voiceProfile.lexical_diversity, 0.1);
  
  // Overall
  scores.overall = Object.values(scores).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / Object.keys(scores).length;
  
  return scores;
}

function proximityScore(actual, target, tolerance) {
  if (!target) return 50; // no target = neutral score
  const distance = Math.abs(actual - target);
  return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 100 - (distance / tolerance) * 50));
}

function isFragment(sentence) {
  const words = sentence.trim().split(/\s+/);
  if (words.length > 6) return false;
  const verbPattern = /\b(is|are|was|were|be|been|being|have|has|had|do|does|did|will|would|could|should|shall|can|may|might|must|go|goes|went|come|came|take|took|make|made|say|said|get|got|know|knew|think|thought|see|saw|want|need|feel|felt|look|looked|find|found|give|gave|tell|told|ask|asked|seem|seemed|leave|left|call|called|try|tried|keep|kept|let|put|run|ran|move|moved|live|lived|stand|stood|turn|turned|play|played|start|started|show|showed)\b/i;
  return !verbPattern.test(sentence);
}
```

This is entirely client-side, zero LLM cost, and provides immediate feedback. The UI can render it as a simple bar chart or radar chart next to the output.

   - Sentence splitting handles edge cases (abbreviations, dialogue
