```js
      if (estimatedTokens + entityTokens < maxTokenBudget) {
        sections.push({ priority: 3, label: 'ENTITIES', content: entitySection, tokens: entityTokens });
        estimatedTokens += entityTokens;
      } else {
        // Trim entities to fit budget
        const availableTokens = maxTokenBudget - estimatedTokens - 500; // reserve buffer
        const trimmedSection = this.trimEntities(entities, availableTokens);
        const trimmedTokens = this.estimateTokens(trimmedSection);
        sections.push({ priority: 3, label: 'ENTITIES', content: trimmedSection, tokens: trimmedTokens });
        estimatedTokens += trimmedTokens;
      }
    }

    // 4. Scene outline / beat sheet for current scene
    if (scene.outline) {
      const outlineSection = `## SCENE PLAN\n${scene.outline}`;
      const outlineTokens = this.estimateTokens(outlineSection);
      if (estimatedTokens + outlineTokens < maxTokenBudget) {
        sections.push({ priority: 2, label: 'SCENE PLAN', content: outlineSection, tokens: outlineTokens });
        estimatedTokens += outlineTokens;
      }
    }

    // 5. Previous scene summaries and trailing prose
    if (includePreviousScenes) {
      const previousContext = await this.getPreviousSceneContext(scene, project.id, maxTokenBudget - estimatedTokens - 500);
      if (previousContext) {
        const prevTokens = this.estimateTokens(previousContext);
        sections.push({ priority: 4, label: 'PREVIOUS CONTEXT', content: previousContext, tokens: prevTokens });
        estimatedTokens += prevTokens;
      }
    }

    // 6. Compendium entries (world rules, magic systems, etc.)
    if (includeCompendium) {
      const compendium = await this.getRelevantCompendium(scene, project.id);
      if (compendium) {
        const compTokens = this.estimateTokens(compendium);
        if (estimatedTokens + compTokens < maxTokenBudget) {
          sections.push({ priority: 5, label: 'WORLD RULES', content: compendium, tokens: compTokens });
          estimatedTokens += compTokens;
        }
      }
    }

    // 7. Scene-specific instructions from the writer
    if (scene.instructions) {
      const instrSection = `## WRITER INSTRUCTIONS\n${scene.instructions}`;
      const instrTokens = this.estimateTokens(instrSection);
      sections.push({ priority: 0, label: 'INSTRUCTIONS', content: instrSection, tokens: instrTokens });
      estimatedTokens += instrTokens;
    }

    // Sort by priority and assemble
    sections.sort((a, b) => a.priority - b.priority);

    return {
      sections,
      totalEstimatedTokens: estimatedTokens,
      formatted: sections.map(s => `## ${s.label}\n${s.content}`).join('\n\n---\n\n')
    };
  }

  formatVoiceProfile(voiceProfile) {
    if (typeof voiceProfile === 'string') return voiceProfile;
    if (voiceProfile.profile) {
      let result = voiceProfile.profile;
      if (voiceProfile.signatureMoves && voiceProfile.signatureMoves.length > 0) {
        result += `\n\nSignature moves: ${voiceProfile.signatureMoves.join('; ')}`;
      }
      if (voiceProfile.avoidances && voiceProfile.avoidances.length > 0) {
        result += `\nAvoid: ${voiceProfile.avoidances.join('; ')}`;
      }
      return result;
    }
    return JSON.stringify(voiceProfile);
  }

  formatStoryContext(project) {
    const parts = [];

    if (project.title) parts.push(`Title: ${project.title}`);
    if (project.genre) parts.push(`Genre: ${project.genre}`);
    if (project.synopsis) parts.push(`Synopsis: ${project.synopsis}`);
    if (project.themes && project.themes.length > 0) {
      parts.push(`Themes: ${project.themes.join(', ')}`);
    }
    if (project.pov) parts.push(`POV: ${project.pov}`);
    if (project.tense) parts.push(`Tense: ${project.tense}`);
    if (project.setting) parts.push(`Setting: ${project.setting}`);
    if (project.tone) parts.push(`Tone: ${project.tone}`);

    return parts.join('\n');
  }

  async getRelevantEntities(scene, projectId) {
    const allEntities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    if (allEntities.length === 0) return [];

    // Score entities by relevance to this scene
    const scored = allEntities.map(entity => {
      let score = 0;

      // Explicitly tagged in scene
      if (scene.entityIds && scene.entityIds.includes(entity.id)) {
        score += 100;
      }

      // Mentioned in scene outline or instructions
      const sceneText = `${scene.outline || ''} ${scene.instructions || ''} ${scene.content || ''}`.toLowerCase();
      if (sceneText.includes(entity.name.toLowerCase())) {
        score += 50;
      }

```js
      // Recently modified entities are more likely relevant
      if (entity.lastModifiedScene) {
        const sceneOrder = scene.order || 0;
        const entitySceneOrder = entity.lastModifiedSceneOrder || 0;
        const recency = Math.max(0, 10 - (sceneOrder - entitySceneOrder));
        score += recency * 3;
      }

      // Characters score higher than locations/objects by default
      if (entity.type === 'character') score += 10;
      if (entity.type === 'location') score += 5;

      // Entities with relationships to high-scoring entities get a boost
      // (computed in a second pass)

    });

    // Second pass: boost entities related to high-scoring ones
    const highScorers = new Set(
      scored.filter(s => s.score >= 50).map(s => s.entity.name.toLowerCase())
    );

    for (const item of scored) {
      if (item.entity.relationships) {
        for (const rel of item.entity.relationships) {
          if (highScorers.has(rel.targetName.toLowerCase())) {
            item.score += 20;
          }
        }
      }
    }

    // Sort by score, return top entities
    scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);

    // Return entities with score > 0, up to a reasonable limit
    return scored
      .filter(s => s.score > 0)
      .slice(0, 20)
      .map(s => s.entity);
  }

  formatEntities(entities) {
    if (entities.length === 0) return 'No tracked entities for this scene.';

    return entities.map(entity => {
      const parts = [`### ${entity.name} (${entity.type})`];

      if (entity.description) parts.push(entity.description);
      if (entity.physicalDescription) parts.push(`Physical: ${entity.physicalDescription}`);

      if (entity.permanentTraits && Object.keys(entity.permanentTraits).length > 0) {
        const traits = Object.entries(entity.permanentTraits)
          .map(([k, v]) => `${k}: ${v}`)
          .join(', ');
        parts.push(`Traits: ${traits}`);
      }

      if (entity.currentState && Object.keys(entity.currentState).length > 0) {
        const state = Object.entries(entity.currentState)
          .map(([k, v]) => `${k}: ${v}`)
          .join(', ');
        parts.push(`Current state: ${state}`);
      }

      if (entity.relationships && entity.relationships.length > 0) {
        const rels = entity.relationships
          .map(r => `${r.relationshipType} ${r.targetName}${r.description ? ` (${r.description})` : ''}`)
          .join('; ');
        parts.push(`Relationships: ${rels}`);
      }

      return parts.join('\n');
    }).join('\n\n');
  }

  trimEntities(entities, availableTokens) {
    // Progressively reduce detail until we fit
    let result = this.formatEntities(entities);
    let tokens = this.estimateTokens(result);

    if (tokens <= availableTokens) return result;

    // Level 1: Remove relationships
    const trimmed1 = entities.map(e => ({
      ...e,
      relationships: []
    }));
    result = this.formatEntities(trimmed1);
    tokens = this.estimateTokens(result);
    if (tokens <= availableTokens) return result;

    // Level 2: Remove state history, keep only current state
    const trimmed2 = trimmed1.map(e => ({
      ...e,
      stateHistory: undefined
    }));
    result = this.formatEntities(trimmed2);
    tokens = this.estimateTokens(result);
    if (tokens <= availableTokens) return result;

    // Level 3: Reduce to just names and one-line descriptions
    result = entities
      .map(e => `- ${e.name} (${e.type}): ${e.description?.slice(0, 100) || 'No description'}`)
      .join('\n');
    return result;
  }

  async getPreviousSceneContext(currentScene, projectId, tokenBudget) {
    const allScenes = await this.db.scenes
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .sortBy('order');

    const currentIndex = allScenes.findIndex(s => s.id === currentScene.id);
    if (currentIndex <= 0) return null;

    const parts = [];
    let tokensUsed = 0;

    // Include the trailing prose from the immediately previous scene (last ~500 words)
    const prevScene = allScenes[currentIndex - 1];
    if (prevScene && prevScene.content) {
      const trailingProse = this.getTrailingProse(prevScene.content, 500);
      const trailingSection = `### End of Previous Scene ("${prevScene.title || 'Untitled'}")\n${trailingProse}`;
      const trailingTokens = this.estimateTokens(trailingSection);

      if (tokensUsed + trailingTokens < tokenBudget) {
        parts.push(trailingSection);
        tokensUsed += trailingTokens;
      }
    }

    // Include summaries of earlier scenes, working backwards
    for (let i = currentIndex - 1; i >= 0 && tokensUsed < tokenBudget; i--) {
      const scene = allScenes[i];
      if (scene.summary) {
        const summarySection = `Scene ${scene.order + 1} ("${scene.title || 'Untitled'}"): ${scene

---

```js
        const summarySection = `Scene ${scene.order + 1} ("${scene.title || 'Untitled'}"): ${scene.summary}`;
        const summaryTokens = this.estimateTokens(summarySection);

        if (tokensUsed + summaryTokens < tokenBudget) {
          parts.unshift(summarySection); // prepend so they're in order
          tokensUsed += summaryTokens;
        } else {
          break;
        }
      }
    }

    if (parts.length === 0) return null;

    return parts.join('\n\n');
  }

  getTrailingProse(content, wordCount) {
    const words = content.split(/\s+/);
    if (words.length <= wordCount) return content;
    return '...' + words.slice(-wordCount).join(' ');
  }

  async getRelevantCompendium(scene, projectId) {
    const entries = await this.db.compendium
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    if (entries.length === 0) return null;

    const sceneText = `${scene.outline || ''} ${scene.instructions || ''} ${scene.content || ''} ${scene.title || ''}`.toLowerCase();

    const relevant = entries.filter(entry => {
      // Check if any keywords match
      if (entry.keywords) {
        for (const keyword of entry.keywords) {
          if (sceneText.includes(keyword.toLowerCase())) return true;
        }
      }
      // Check name match
      if (sceneText.includes(entry.name.toLowerCase())) return true;
      // Check if tagged for this scene
      if (entry.sceneIds && entry.sceneIds.includes(scene.id)) return true;
      // Check if it's a global rule (always included)
      if (entry.global) return true;
      return false;
    });

    if (relevant.length === 0) return null;

    return relevant
      .map(e => `### ${e.name}\n${e.content}`)
      .join('\n\n');
  }

  estimateTokens(text) {
    if (!text) return 0;
    // Rough estimate: ~4 characters per token for English
    return Math.ceil(text.length / 4);
  }
}

```js
// src/engine/generation-engine.js

import { ContextAssembler } from './context-assembler.js';
import { EntityExtractor } from './entity-extractor.js';
import { VoiceAnalyzer } from './voice-analyzer.js';

export class GenerationEngine {
  constructor(db, llmProvider) {
    this.db = db;
    this.llm = llmProvider;
    this.contextAssembler = new ContextAssembler(db);
    this.entityExtractor = new EntityExtractor(db, llmProvider);
    this.voiceAnalyzer = new VoiceAnalyzer(llmProvider);
  }

  async generateSceneDraft(sceneId, options = {}) {
    const scene = await this.db.scenes.get(sceneId);
    if (!scene) throw new Error('Scene not found');

    const project = await this.db.projects.get(scene.projectId);
    if (!project) throw new Error('Project not found');

    const {
      mode = 'draft',           // draft | continue | rewrite | expand
      customInstructions = '',
      maxTokens = 4000,
      temperature = 0.8,
      stream = true,
      contextBudget = 12000
    } = options;

    // Assemble context
    const context = await this.contextAssembler.assembleContext(scene, project, {
      maxTokenBudget: contextBudget
    });

    // Build the generation prompt
    const systemPrompt = this.buildSystemPrompt(project, context, mode);
    const userPrompt = this.buildUserPrompt(scene, mode, customInstructions);

    const messages = [
      { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
      { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }
    ];

    // If continuing, include existing content as assistant message
    if (mode === 'continue' && scene.content) {
      messages.push({ role: 'assistant', content: scene.content });
      messages.push({ role: 'user', content: 'Continue writing from where you left off. Maintain the same voice, pacing, and narrative thread. Do not repeat any content.' });
    }

    const response = await this.llm.callLLM({
      model: this.llm.getModel('generation'),
      messages,
      temperature,
      max_tokens: maxTokens,
      stream
    });

    if (stream) {
      return {
        stream: response.stream,
        context,
        finalize: async (generatedText) => {
          await this.postGeneration(scene, project, generatedText, mode);
        }
      };
    }

    // Non-streaming: process immediately
    await this.postGeneration(scene, project, response.content, mode);

    return {
      content: response.content,
      context,
      usage: response.usage
    };
  }

  buildSystemPrompt(project, context, mode) {
    const parts = [];

    parts.push(`You are a fiction writer working on a ${project.genre || 'literary fiction'} novel titled "${project.title || 'Untitled'}".`);

    if (mode === 'draft') {
      parts.push('Write the scene described below. Focus on vivid prose, authentic dialogue, and emotional resonance. Show, don\'t tell. Ground abstract emotions in physical sensation and concrete detail.');
    } else if (mode === 'continue') {
      parts.push('Continue the scene from where it left off. Maintain perfect consistency with everything written so far.');
    } else if (mode === '

---

    } else if (mode === 'rewrite') {
      parts.push('Rewrite the scene with improved prose quality while preserving the core narrative beats and character actions. Elevate the language, deepen the interiority, and sharpen the sensory details.');
    } else if (mode === 'expand') {
      parts.push('Expand the scene with additional detail, interiority, dialogue, or action. Weave new material seamlessly into the existing prose without disrupting the flow.');
    }

    // Add project-level writing rules
    if (project.writingRules && project.writingRules.length > 0) {
      parts.push('\n## WRITING RULES');
      for (const rule of project.writingRules) {
        parts.push(`- ${rule}`);
      }
    }

    // Add POV and tense constraints
    if (project.pov) parts.push(`\nNarrative POV: ${project.pov}`);
    if (project.tense) parts.push(`Tense: ${project.tense}`);

    // Add assembled context
    parts.push('\n' + context.formatted);

    // Critical instructions
    parts.push(`\n## CRITICAL RULES
- Write ONLY prose. No meta-commentary, no notes, no headers like "Scene 1".
- Never break the fourth wall or reference yourself as an AI.
- Maintain absolute consistency with all entity details, world rules, and established facts.
- Match the voice profile precisely. This is the author's voice, not yours.
- If dialogue is present, make each character's speech patterns distinct.
- End at a natural stopping point, not mid-sentence.`);

    return parts.join('\n');
  }

  buildUserPrompt(scene, mode, customInstructions) {
    const parts = [];

    if (scene.title) parts.push(`Scene: "${scene.title}"`);

    if (scene.outline && mode !== 'continue') {
      parts.push(`\nScene outline:\n${scene.outline}`);
    }

    if (scene.instructions) {
      parts.push(`\nWriter's notes:\n${scene.instructions}`);
    }

    if (customInstructions) {
      parts.push(`\nAdditional instructions:\n${customInstructions}`);
    }

    if (mode === 'rewrite' && scene.content) {
      parts.push(`\nOriginal text to rewrite:\n${scene.content}`);
    }

    if (mode === 'expand' && scene.content) {
      parts.push(`\nExisting text to expand:\n${scene.content}`);
    }

    if (mode === 'draft') {
      parts.push('\nWrite this scene now.');
    }

    return parts.join('\n');
  }

  async postGeneration(scene, project, generatedText, mode) {
    // 1. Update scene content
    let newContent;
    if (mode === 'continue') {
      newContent = (scene.content || '') + generatedText;
    } else if (mode === 'expand') {
      // The LLM should have woven it in; use the full output
      newContent = generatedText;
    } else {
      newContent = generatedText;
    }

    await this.db.scenes.update(scene.id, {
      content: newContent,
      lastGenerated: new Date().toISOString(),
      wordCount: this.countWords(newContent)
    });

    // 2. Auto-extract entity updates (async, non-blocking)
    this.extractAndUpdateEntities(scene.id, project.id, generatedText).catch(err => {
      console.warn('Entity extraction failed:', err);
    });

    // 3. Generate scene summary if we have enough content
    if (this.countWords(newContent) > 200) {
      this.generateSceneSummary(scene.id, newContent).catch(err => {
        console.warn('Summary generation failed:', err);
      });
    }
  }

  async extractAndUpdateEntities(sceneId, projectId, text) {
    const scene = await this.db.scenes.get(sceneId);
    const existingEntities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    const entityNames = existingEntities.map(e => ({
      name: e.name,
      type: e.type,
      aliases: e.aliases || []
    }));

    const extraction = await this.entityExtractor.extractFromProse(text, entityNames);

    if (!extraction) return;

    // Update existing entities with new state information
    if (extraction.existingEntityUpdates) {
      for (const update of extraction.existingEntityUpdates) {
        const entity = existingEntities.find(
          e => e.name.toLowerCase() === update.name.toLowerCase()
        );
        if (entity) {
          const stateUpdate = {
            ...entity.currentState,
            ...update.stateChanges
          };

          const history = entity.stateHistory || [];
          history.push({
            sceneId,
            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
            changes: update.stateChanges,
            context: update.context
          });

          await this.db.entities.update(entity.id, {
            currentState: stateUpdate,
            stateHistory: history,
            lastModifiedScene: sceneId,
            lastModifiedSceneOrder: scene.order
          });
        }
      }
    }

    // Flag new entities for user review (don't auto-create)
    if (extraction.newEntities && extraction.newEntities.length > 0) {
      await this.db.pendingEntities.bulkAdd(
        extraction.newEntities.map(e => ({
          ...e,
          projectId,
          sceneId,
          status: 'pending',
          createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
        }))
      );
    }
  }

  async generateSc

```js
  async generateSceneSummary(sceneId, content) {
    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: 'You are a precise story analyst. Summarize the given scene in 2-4 sentences. Focus on: key plot events, character actions and decisions, emotional shifts, and any information that future scenes need to maintain consistency. Be factual and specific.'
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Summarize this scene:\n\n${content}`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.callLLM({
      model: this.llm.getModel('utility'),
      messages,
      temperature: 0.3,
      max_tokens: 300,
      stream: false
    });

    await this.db.scenes.update(sceneId, {
      summary: response.content
    });

    return response.content;
  }

  countWords(text) {
    if (!text) return 0;
    return text.split(/\s+/).filter(w => w.length > 0).length;
  }
}

**Entity Extractor — parses prose for entity state changes.**

```js
// src/engine/entity-extractor.js

export class EntityExtractor {
  constructor(db, llmProvider) {
    this.db = db;
    this.llm = llmProvider;
  }

  async extractFromProse(text, knownEntities) {
    const entityList = knownEntities
      .map(e => `- ${e.name} (${e.type})${e.aliases.length > 0 ? ` [aliases: ${e.aliases.join(', ')}]` : ''}`)
      .join('\n');

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `You are a story continuity tracker. Analyze the prose below and extract:

1. State changes for known entities (emotional shifts, physical changes, location changes, new information revealed, relationship changes)
2. Any new named entities that appear (characters, locations, objects) that aren't in the known list

Known entities:
${entityList || '(none yet)'}

Respond in JSON format:
{
  "existingEntityUpdates": [
    {
      "name": "entity name",
      "stateChanges": { "key": "value" },
      "context": "brief quote or description of what happened"
    }
  ],
  "newEntities": [
    {
      "name": "entity name",
      "type": "character|location|object|faction|concept",
      "description": "brief description based on what's in the text",
      "suggestedAliases": []
    }
  ]
}

Only include meaningful state changes, not trivial actions. If nothing significant changed, return empty arrays.`
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: text
      }
    ];

    try {
      const response = await this.llm.callLLM({
        model: this.llm.getModel('utility'),
        messages,
        temperature: 0.2,
        max_tokens: 2000,
        stream: false,
        response_format: { type: 'json_object' }
      });

      return JSON.parse(response.content);
    } catch (err) {
      // Try to extract JSON from the response if it's wrapped in markdown
      if (err instanceof SyntaxError) {
        const jsonMatch = err.message?.match(/```json\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```/) ||
                          response?.content?.match(/```json\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```/);
        if (jsonMatch) {
          return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[1]);
        }
      }
      console.warn('Entity extraction parse error:', err);
      return null;
    }
  }

  async reconcileEntities(projectId) {
    // Find potential duplicates
    const entities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    const duplicates = [];

    for (let i = 0; i < entities.length; i++) {
      for (let j = i + 1; j < entities.length; j++) {
        const similarity = this.nameSimilarity(entities[i].name, entities[j].name);
        if (similarity > 0.8) {
          duplicates.push({
            entity1: entities[i],
            entity2: entities[j],
            similarity
          });
        }

        // Check if one entity's name matches another's alias
        const aliases1 = entities[i].aliases || [];
        const aliases2 = entities[j].aliases || [];

        if (aliases1.some(a => a.toLowerCase() === entities[j].name.toLowerCase()) ||
            aliases2.some(a => a.toLowerCase() === entities[i].name.toLowerCase())) {
          duplicates.push({
            entity1: entities[i],
            entity2: entities[j],
            similarity: 0.95,
            reason: 'alias_match'
          });
        }
      }
    }

    return duplicates;
  }

  nameSimilarity(a, b) {
    const la = a.toLowerCase();
    const lb = b.toLowerCase();
    if (la === lb) return 1;
    if (la.includes(lb) || lb.includes(la)) return 0.85;

    // Levenshtein-based similarity
    const distance = this.levenshtein(la, lb);
    const maxLen = Math.max(la.length, lb.length);
    return 1 - distance / maxLen;
  }

  levenshtein(a, b) {
    const matrix = [];
    for (let i = 0; i <= b.length; i++) matrix[i] = [i];
    for (let j = 0; j <= a.length; j++) matrix[0][j] = j;

    for (let i = 1;

---

```js
    for (let i = 1; i <= b.length; i++) {
      for (let j = 1; j <= a.length; j++) {
        if (b[i - 1] === a[j - 1]) {
          matrix[i][j] = matrix[i - 1][j - 1];
        } else {
          matrix[i][j] = Math.min(
            matrix[i - 1][j - 1] + 1,
            matrix[i][j - 1] + 1,
            matrix[i - 1][j] + 1
          );
        }
      }
    }
    return matrix[b.length][a.length];
  }
}

**Voice Analyzer — captures and maintains the author's writing style.**

```js
// src/engine/voice-analyzer.js

export class VoiceAnalyzer {
  constructor(llmProvider) {
    this.llm = llmProvider;
  }

  async analyzeVoice(sampleTexts) {
    // sampleTexts: array of strings (the author's existing prose)
    const combinedSample = sampleTexts
      .map((t, i) => `--- Sample ${i + 1} ---\n${t}`)
      .join('\n\n');

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `You are a literary style analyst. Analyze the writing samples below and produce a detailed voice profile that could be used to replicate this author's style. Cover:

1. **Sentence structure**: Average length, variation patterns, use of fragments, compound/complex tendencies
2. **Diction**: Register (formal/informal/mixed), vocabulary level, favorite word categories, any distinctive word choices
3. **Rhythm and pacing**: How scenes breathe, paragraph length patterns, use of white space
4. **Dialogue style**: Tag usage, dialect rendering, subtext patterns, how dialogue interleaves with action
5. **Figurative language**: Metaphor density, simile patterns, imagery preferences (visual/auditory/tactile/etc.)
6. **Interiority**: How internal thoughts are rendered, depth of psychological access, stream-of-consciousness tendencies
7. **Narrative distance**: Close/distant, when it shifts, how transitions work
8. **Signature moves**: Any distinctive techniques, recurring patterns, or stylistic fingerprints
9. **What this voice avoids**: Things this author clearly doesn't do

Respond in JSON:
{
  "profile": "A 2-3 paragraph prose description of the voice",
  "sentencePatterns": { "avgLength": "short/medium/long", "variation": "high/medium/low", "fragments": true/false },
  "diction": { "register": "...", "level": "...", "notes": "..." },
  "dialogueStyle": { "tagStyle": "...", "notes": "..." },
  "figurativeLanguage": { "density": "sparse/moderate/rich", "preferredTypes": [], "notes": "..." },
  "interiority": { "depth": "shallow/moderate/deep", "technique": "...", "notes": "..." },
  "narrativeDistance": { "default": "close/medium/distant", "notes": "..." },
  "signatureMoves": ["...", "..."],
  "avoidances": ["...", "..."],
  "examplePhrases": ["characteristic phrases or constructions from the samples"]
}`
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Analyze these writing samples:\n\n${combinedSample}`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.callLLM({
      model: this.llm.getModel('analysis'),
      messages,
      temperature: 0.3,
      max_tokens: 3000,
      stream: false,
      response_format: { type: 'json_object' }
    });

    try {
      return JSON.parse(response.content);
    } catch (e) {
      // Fallback: try to extract JSON
      const match = response.content.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
      if (match) return JSON.parse(match[0]);
      throw new Error('Failed to parse voice analysis');
    }
  }

  async refineVoice(existingProfile, newSample, feedback) {
    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `You are refining an author's voice profile based on new writing samples and/or feedback. 
        
Current profile:
${JSON.stringify(existingProfile, null, 2)}

Update the profile to incorporate new observations. If the author provided feedback about what the profile gets wrong, prioritize those corrections. Return the complete updated profile in the same JSON format.`
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `${newSample ? `New writing sample:\n${newSample}\n\n` : ''}${feedback ? `Author feedback on current profile:\n${feedback}` : 'Refine based on the new sample.'}`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.callLLM({
      model: this.llm.getModel('analysis'),
      messages,
      temperature: 0.3,
      max_tokens: 3000,
      stream: false,
      response_format: { type: 'json_object' }
    });

    return JSON.parse(response.content);
  }

  async compareVoice(originalSample, generatedSample) {
    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `Compare the voice/style of two text samples. The first is the author's original writing. The second is AI-generated text that should match the author's voice. 

Identify:
1. What matches well
2. What diverges
3. Specific suggestions for improving the match

Be precise and cite specific examples from both texts.

Respond in JSON:
{
  "overallMatch": 0-100,

**LLM Provider — abstraction layer for multiple AI backends.**

```js
// src/engine/llm-provider.js

export class LLMProvider {
  constructor(config = {}) {
    this.providers = {};
    this.defaultProvider = config.defaultProvider || 'openai';
    this.modelMap = config.modelMap || {
      generation: 'gpt-4o',
      analysis: 'gpt-4o-mini',
      utility: 'gpt-4o-mini'
    };
    this.apiKeys = config.apiKeys || {};
    this.baseUrls = config.baseUrls || {};
    this.requestLog = [];
  }

  configure(providerName, config) {
    this.providers[providerName] = config;
    if (config.apiKey) this.apiKeys[providerName] = config.apiKey;
    if (config.baseUrl) this.baseUrls[providerName] = config.baseUrl;
    if (config.models) {
      Object.assign(this.modelMap, config.models);
    }
  }

  getModel(purpose) {
    return this.modelMap[purpose] || this.modelMap.generation;
  }

  detectProvider(model) {
    if (model.startsWith('gpt-') || model.startsWith('o1') || model.startsWith('o3') || model.startsWith('o4')) return 'openai';
    if (model.startsWith('claude-')) return 'anthropic';
    if (model.startsWith('gemini-')) return 'google';
    if (model.startsWith('deepseek-')) return 'deepseek';
    if (model.startsWith('mistral-') || model.startsWith('codestral')) return 'mistral';
    // Check for custom/local providers
    if (this.baseUrls.custom) return 'custom';
    return this.defaultProvider;
  }

  async callLLM(options) {
    const {
      model,
      messages,
      temperature = 0.7,
      max_tokens = 4000,
      stream = false,
      response_format,
      stop
    } = options;

    const provider = this.detectProvider(model);
    const startTime = Date.now();

    let result;

    switch (provider) {
      case 'openai':
        result = await this.callOpenAI(model, messages, { temperature, max_tokens, stream, response_format, stop });
        break;
      case 'anthropic':
        result = await this.callAnthropic(model, messages, { temperature, max_tokens, stream, stop });
        break;
      case 'google':
        result = await this.callGoogle(model, messages, { temperature, max_tokens, stream, stop });
        break;
      case 'custom':
        result = await this.callOpenAICompatible(model, messages, { temperature, max_tokens, stream, response_format, stop });
        break;
      default:
        throw new Error(`Unknown provider: ${provider}`);
    }

    // Log the request
    this.requestLog.push({
      model,
      provider,
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      duration: Date.now() - startTime,
      inputTokens: result.usage?.prompt_tokens,
      outputTokens: result.usage?.completion_tokens,
      stream
    });

    // Keep log bounded
    if (this.requestLog.length > 1000) {
      this.requestLog = this.requestLog.slice(-500);
    }

    return result;
  }

  async callOpenAI(model, messages, options) {
    const apiKey = this.apiKeys.openai;
    if (!apiKey) throw new Error('OpenAI API key not configured');

    const baseUrl = this.baseUrls.openai || 'https://api.openai.com/v1';

    const body = {
      model,
      messages,
      temperature: options.temperature,
      max_tokens: options.max_tokens
    };

    if (options.response_format) body.response_format = options.response_format;
    if (options.stop) body.stop = options.stop;

    if (options.stream) {
      body.stream = true;
      body.stream_options = { include_usage: true };

      const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(body)
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const error = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: { message: response.statusText } }));
        throw new Error(`OpenAI API error: ${error.error?.message || response.statusText}`);
      }

      return {
        stream: this.createSSEStream(response.body),
        usage: null // will be in the final chunk
      };
    }

    const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',

  async callAnthropic(model, messages, options) {
    const apiKey = this.apiKeys.anthropic;
    if (!apiKey) throw new Error('Anthropic API key not configured');

    const baseUrl = this.baseUrls.anthropic || 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1';

    // Anthropic uses a different message format — separate system from messages
    const systemMessage = messages.find(m => m.role === 'system');
    const nonSystemMessages = messages.filter(m => m.role !== 'system');

    // Anthropic requires alternating user/assistant messages
    const anthropicMessages = this.normalizeMessagesForAnthropic(nonSystemMessages);

    const body = {
      model,
      max_tokens: options.max_tokens,
      temperature: options.temperature,
      messages: anthropicMessages
    };

    if (systemMessage) {
      body.system = systemMessage.content;
    }

    if (options.stop) body.stop_sequences = Array.isArray(options.stop) ? options.stop : [options.stop];

    if (options.stream) {
      body.stream = true;

      const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/messages`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'x-api-key': apiKey,
          'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01',
          'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true'
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(body)
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const error = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: { message: response.statusText } }));
        throw new Error(`Anthropic API error: ${error.error?.message || response.statusText}`);
      }

      return {
        stream: this.createAnthropicStream(response.body),
        usage: null
      };
    }

    const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/messages`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'x-api-key': apiKey,
        'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01',
        'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true'
      },
      body: JSON.stringify(body)
    });

    if (!response.ok) {
      const error = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: { message: response.statusText } }));
      throw new Error(`Anthropic API error: ${error.error?.message || response.statusText}`);
    }

    const data = await response.json();

    return {
      content: data.content[0].text,
      usage: {
        prompt_tokens: data.usage.input_tokens,
        completion_tokens: data.usage.output_tokens,
        total_tokens: data.usage.input_tokens + data.usage.output_tokens
      },
      finishReason: data.stop_reason
    };
  }

  normalizeMessagesForAnthropic(messages) {
    // Anthropic requires strict user/assistant alternation starting with user
    const normalized = [];
    let lastRole = null;

    for (const msg of messages) {
      if (msg.role === lastRole) {
        // Merge consecutive same-role messages
        normalized[normalized.length - 1].content += '\n\n' + msg.content;
      } else {
        normalized.push({ role: msg.role, content: msg.content });
        lastRole = msg.role;
      }
    }

    // Ensure starts with user
    if (normalized.length > 0 && normalized[0].role !== 'user') {
      normalized.unshift({ role: 'user', content: 'Begin.' });
    }

    return normalized;
  }

  async callGoogle(model, messages, options) {
    const apiKey = this.apiKeys.google;
    if (!apiKey) throw new Error('Google AI API key not configured');

    // Convert to Gemini format
    const systemMessage = messages.find(m => m.role === 'system');
    const nonSystemMessages = messages.filter(m => m.role !== 'system');

    const contents = nonSystemMessages.map(m => ({
      role: m.role === 'assistant' ? 'model' : 'user',
      parts: [{ text: m.content }]
    }));

    const body = {
      contents,
      generationConfig: {
        temperature: options.temperature,
        maxOutputTokens: options.max_tokens
      }
    };

    if (systemMessage) {
      body.systemInstruction = { parts: [{ text: systemMessage.content }] };
    }

    if (options.stop) {
      body.generationConfig.stopSequences = Array.isArray(options.stop) ? options.stop : [options.stop];
    }

    const endpoint = options.stream ? 'streamGenerateContent' : 'generateContent';
    const url = `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/${model}:${endpoint}?key=${apiKey}`;

    if (options.stream) {
      const response = await fetch(`${url}&alt=sse`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-

  async callOpenAICompatible(model, messages, options) {
    const apiKey = this.apiKeys.custom || 'not-needed';
    const baseUrl = this.baseUrls.custom;

    const body = {
      model,
      messages,
      temperature: options.temperature,
      max_tokens: options.max_tokens
    };

    if (options.response_format) body.response_format = options.response_format;
    if (options.stop) body.stop = options.stop;

    if (options.stream) {
      body.stream = true;

      const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(body)
      });

      if (!response.ok) {
        const error = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: { message: response.statusText } }));
        throw new Error(`API error: ${error.error?.message || response.statusText}`);
      }

      return {
        stream: this.createSSEStream(response.body),
        usage: null
      };
    }

    const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`
      },

    if (!response.ok) {
      const error = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: { message: response.statusText } }));

  // --- Streaming helpers ---

  async *createSSEStream(body) {
    const reader = body.getReader();
    const decoder = new TextDecoder();
    let buffer = '';

    try {
      while (true) {
        const { done, value } = await reader.read();
        if (done) break;

        buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
        const lines = buffer.split('\n');
        buffer = lines.pop() || '';

        for (const line of lines) {
          const trimmed = line.trim();
          if (!trimmed || !trimmed.startsWith('data: ')) continue;

          const data = trimmed.slice(6);
          if (data === '[DONE]') return;

          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(data);
            const delta = parsed.choices?.[0]?.delta?.content;
            if (delta) {
              yield {
                type: 'content',
                content: delta
              };
            }

            // Check for usage in final chunk
            if (parsed.usage) {
              yield {
                type: 'usage',
                usage: parsed.usage
              };
            }

            if (parsed.choices?.[0]?.finish_reason) {
              yield {
                type: 'finish',
                reason: parsed.choices[0].finish_reason
              };
            }
          } catch (e) {
            // Skip unparseable lines
          }
        }
      }
    } finally {
      reader.releaseLock();
    }
  }

  async *createAnthropicStream(body) {
    const reader = body.getReader();
    const decoder = new TextDecoder();
    let buffer = '';

    try {
      while (true) {
        const { done, value } = await reader.read();

        buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });

        for (const line of lines) {

          if (!trimmed || !trimmed.startsWith('data: ')) continue;

          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed.slice(6));

            if (parsed.type === 'content_block_delta' && parsed.delta?.text) {
              yield {
                type: 'content',
                content:

  async *createGoogleStream(body) {
    const reader = body.getReader();
    const decoder = new TextDecoder();
    let buffer = '';

    try {
      while (true) {
        const { done, value } = await reader.read();
        if (done) break;

        buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
        const lines = buffer.split('\n');
        buffer = lines.pop() || '';

        for (const line of lines) {
          const trimmed = line.trim();
          if (!trimmed || !trimmed.startsWith('data: ')) continue;

          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed.slice(6));
            const text = parsed.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text;
            if (text) {
              yield {
                type: 'content',
                content: text
              };
            }

            if (parsed.usageMetadata) {
              yield {
                type: 'usage',
                usage: {
                  prompt_tokens: parsed.usageMetadata.promptTokenCount || 0,
                  completion_tokens: parsed.usageMetadata.candidatesTokenCount || 0
                }
              };
            }

            if (parsed.candidates?.[0]?.finishReason) {
              yield {
                type: 'finish',
                reason: parsed.candidates[0].finishReason
              };
            }
          } catch (e) {
            // Skip unparseable lines
          }
        }
      }
    } finally {
      reader.releaseLock();
    }
  }

  getUsageStats() {
    const stats = {
      totalRequests: this.requestLog.length,
      totalInputTokens: 0,
      totalOutputTokens: 0,
      avgDuration: 0,
      byModel: {},
      byProvider: {}
    };

    let totalDuration = 0;

    for (const entry of this.requestLog) {
      stats.totalInputTokens += entry.inputTokens || 0;
      stats.totalOutputTokens += entry.outputTokens || 0;
      totalDuration += entry.duration || 0;

      if (!stats.byModel[entry.model]) {
        stats.byModel[entry.model] = { requests: 0, inputTokens: 0, outputTokens: 0 };
      }
      stats.byModel[entry.model].requests++;
      stats.byModel[entry.model].inputTokens += entry.inputTokens || 0;
      stats.byModel[entry.model].outputTokens += entry.outputTokens || 0;

      if (!stats.byProvider[entry.provider]) {
        stats.byProvider[entry.provider] = { requests: 0, inputTokens: 0, outputTokens: 0 };
      }
      stats.byProvider[entry.provider].requests++;
      stats.byProvider[entry.provider].inputTokens += entry.inputTokens || 0;
      stats.byProvider[entry.provider].outputTokens += entry.outputTokens || 0;
    }

    stats.avgDuration = stats.totalRequests > 0 ? totalDuration / stats.totalRequests : 0;

    return stats;
  }
}

**Context Assembler — builds the context window intelligently.**

```js
// src/engine/context-assembler.js

export class ContextAssembler {
  constructor(db) {
    this.db = db;
    // Rough token estimation: 1 token ≈ 4 chars for English
    this.charsPerToken = 4;
  }

  async assembleContext(scene, project, options = {}) {
    const {
      maxTokens = 100000,
      includeEntities = true,
      includeWorldRules = true,
      includePreviousScenes = true,
      includeVoiceProfile = true,
      customContext = null
    } = options;

    const sections = [];
    let tokenBudget = maxTokens;

    // 1. Voice profile (high priority, usually small)
    if (includeVoiceProfile && project.voiceProfile) {
      const voiceSection = this.formatVoiceProfile(project.voiceProfile);
      const voiceTokens = this.estimateTokens(voiceSection);
      if (voiceTokens < tokenBudget * 0.1) {
        sections.push({ priority: 1, label: 'VOICE PROFILE', content: voiceSection, tokens: voiceTokens });
        tokenBudget -= voiceTokens;
      }
    }

    // 2. World rules (high priority, usually small)
    if (includeWorldRules && project.worldRules && project.worldRules.length > 0) {
      const rulesSection = project.worldRules.map(r => `- ${r}`).join('\n');
      const rulesTokens = this.estimateTokens(rulesSection);
      if (rulesTokens < tokenBudget * 0.1) {
        sections.push({ priority: 2, label: 'WORLD RULES', content: rulesSection, tokens: rulesTokens });
        tokenB

```js
        tokenBudget -= rulesTokens;
      }
    }

    // 3. Entity states (medium-high priority)
    if (includeEntities) {
      const entities = await this.db.entities
        .where('projectId').equals(project.id)
        .toArray();

      if (entities.length > 0) {
        const entitySection = this.formatEntities(entities);
        const entityTokens = this.estimateTokens(entitySection);

        // Entities get up to 20% of budget
        if (entityTokens < tokenBudget * 0.2) {
          sections.push({ priority: 3, label: 'ENTITY STATES', content: entitySection, tokens: entityTokens });
          tokenBudget -= entityTokens;
        } else {
          // Trim to most relevant entities
          const relevantEntities = await this.getRelevantEntities(entities, scene);
          const trimmedSection = this.formatEntities(relevantEntities);
          const trimmedTokens = this.estimateTokens(trimmedSection);
          sections.push({ priority: 3, label: 'ENTITY STATES', content: trimmedSection, tokens: trimmedTokens });
          tokenBudget -= trimmedTokens;
        }
      }
    }

    // 4. Previous scene summaries and recent prose
    if (includePreviousScenes) {
      const chapterId = scene.chapterId;
      const chapter = await this.db.chapters.get(chapterId);

      if (chapter) {
        const allScenes = await this.db.scenes
          .where('chapterId').equals(chapterId)
          .sortBy('order');

        const currentIndex = allScenes.findIndex(s => s.id === scene.id);
        const previousScenes = allScenes.slice(0, currentIndex);

        // Get scenes from previous chapters too
        const allChapters = await this.db.chapters
          .where('projectId').equals(project.id)
          .sortBy('order');

        const chapterIndex = allChapters.findIndex(c => c.id === chapterId);
        const previousChapters = allChapters.slice(0, chapterIndex);

        // Build scene history: summaries for older scenes, full prose for recent
        const sceneHistory = [];

        // Previous chapters: summaries only
        for (const prevChapter of previousChapters) {
          const chapterScenes = await this.db.scenes
            .where('chapterId').equals(prevChapter.id)
            .sortBy('order');

          for (const s of chapterScenes) {
            if (s.summary) {
              sceneHistory.push({
                type: 'summary',
                chapterTitle: prevChapter.title,
                sceneTitle: s.title,
                content: s.summary
              });
            }
          }
        }

        // Current chapter previous scenes: summaries for older, full prose for most recent
        for (let i = 0; i < previousScenes.length; i++) {
          const s = previousScenes[i];
          const isRecent = i >= previousScenes.length - 2; // Last 2 scenes get full prose

          if (isRecent && s.content) {
            sceneHistory.push({
              type: 'prose',
              chapterTitle: chapter.title,
              sceneTitle: s.title,
              content: s.content
            });
          } else if (s.summary) {
            sceneHistory.push({
              type: 'summary',
              chapterTitle: chapter.title,
              sceneTitle: s.title,
              content: s.summary
            });
          }
        }

        // Format and fit within budget
        const historySection = this.formatSceneHistory(sceneHistory, tokenBudget * 0.5);
        const historyTokens = this.estimateTokens(historySection);

        if (historySection) {
          sections.push({ priority: 4, label: 'STORY SO FAR', content: historySection, tokens: historyTokens });
          tokenBudget -= historyTokens;
        }
      }
    }

    // 5. Custom context (user-provided notes for this generation)
    if (customContext) {
      const customTokens = this.estimateTokens(customContext);
      sections.push({ priority: 2.5, label: 'AUTHOR NOTES FOR THIS SCENE', content: customContext, tokens: customTokens });
      tokenBudget -= customTokens;
    }

    // 6. Scene-specific instructions
    const sceneInstructions = this.formatSceneInstructions(scene);
    if (sceneInstructions) {
      const instrTokens = this.estimateTokens(sceneInstructions);
      sections.push({ priority: 5, label: 'SCENE INSTRUCTIONS', content: sceneInstructions, tokens: instrTokens });
      tokenBudget -= instrTokens;
    }

    // Sort by priority and assemble
    sections.sort((a, b) => a.priority - b.priority);

    const assembled = sections
      .map(s => `=== ${s.label} ===\n${s.content}`)
      .join('\n\n');

    return {
      contextText: assembled,
      totalTokens: this.estimateTokens(assembled),
      remainingBudget: tokenBudget,
      sections: sections.map(s => ({ label: s.label, tokens: s.tokens }))
    };
  }

  formatVoiceProfile(profile) {
    if (typeof profile === 'string') return profile;

    let text = '';
    if (profile.profile) {
      text += profile.profile + '\n\n';
    }

    if (profile.signatureMoves?.length > 0) {
      text += 'Signature techniques: ' + profile.signatureMoves.join('; ') + '\n';
    }

    if (profile.avoidances?.length > 0) {
      text += 'Avoid: ' + profile.avoidances.join('; ') + '\n';
    }

    if (profile.dialogueStyle?.notes) {
      text += 'Dialogue style: ' + profile.dialogueStyle.notes + '\n';
    }

    if (profile.figurativeLanguage?.notes) {
      text += 'Figurative language: ' + profile.figurativeLanguage.notes + '\n';
    }

    if (profile.interiority?.notes) {
      text += 'Interiority: ' + profile.interiority.notes + '\n';
    }

    if (profile.examplePhrases?.length > 0) {
      text += 'Characteristic constructions: ' + profile.examplePhrases.join(' | ') + '\n';
    }

    return text.trim();
  }

  formatEntities(entities) {
    return entities.map(e => {
      let entry = `**${e.name}** (${e.type})`;
      if (e.description) entry += `: ${e.description}`;

      if (e.state && Object.keys(e.state).length > 0) {
        const stateEntries = Object.entries(e.state)
          .map(([k, v]) => `  - ${k}: ${v}`)
          .join('\n');
        entry += '\n' + stateEntries;
      }

      if (e.relationships && e.relationships.length > 0) {
        const rels = e.relationships
          .map(r => `  → ${r.type}: ${r.target}`)
          .join('\n');
        entry += '\n' + rels;
      }

      return entry;
    }).join('\n\n');
  }

  async getRelevantEntities(entities, scene) {
    // Score entities by relevance to this scene
    const sceneText = [
      scene.title || '',
      scene.synopsis || '',
      scene.notes || '',
      scene.content || ''
    ].join(' ').toLowerCase();

    const scored = entities.map(e => {
      let score = 0;

      // Name mentioned in scene text
      if (sceneText.includes(e.name.toLowerCase())) score += 10;

      // Aliases mentioned
      if (e.aliases) {
        for (const alias of e.aliases) {
          if (sceneText.includes(alias.toLowerCase())) score += 8;
        }
      }

      // Characters get priority
      if (e.type === 'character') score += 3;

      // Recently modified entities get a boost
      if (e.updatedAt) {
        const hoursSinceUpdate = (Date.now() - new Date(e.updatedAt).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60);
        if (hoursSinceUpdate < 24) score += 2;
      }

      return { entity: e, score };
    });

    scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);

    // Return top entities, at least those with score > 0
    const relevant = scored.filter(s => s.score > 0).map(s => s.entity);
    return relevant.length > 0 ? relevant : scored.slice(0, 10).map(s => s.entity);
  }

  formatSceneHistory(history, maxTokens) {
    if (history.length === 0) return '';

    let result = '';
    let tokens = 0;

    // Work backwards from most recent — most recent context is most important
    for (let i = history.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
      const entry = history[i];
      let section = '';

      if (entry.type === 'prose') {
        section = `[${entry.chapterTitle} > ${entry.sceneTitle}] (full text):\n${entry.content}`;
      } else {
        section = `[${entry.chapterTitle} > ${entry.sceneTitle}] (summary):\n${entry.content}`;
      }

      const sectionTokens = this.estimateTokens(section);

      if (tokens + sectionTokens > maxTokens) {
        // If this is a prose section, try to include just the end
        if (entry.type === 'prose') {
          const availableChars = (maxTokens - tokens) * this.charsPerToken;
          if (availableChars > 500) {
            const truncated = '...' + entry.content.slice(-availableChars);
            section = `[${entry.chapterTitle} > ${entry.sceneTitle}] (end of scene):\n${truncated}`;
            result = section + '\n\n' + result;
          }
        }
        break;
      }

      result = section + '\n\n' + result;
      tokens += sectionTokens;
    }

    return result.trim();
  }

  formatSceneInstructions(scene) {
    const parts = [];

    if (scene.title) parts.push(`Scene: ${scene.title}`);
    if (scene.synopsis) parts.push(`Synopsis: ${scene.synopsis}`);
    if (scene.pov) parts.push(`POV: ${scene.pov}`);
    if (scene.setting) parts.push(`Setting: ${scene.setting}`);
    if (scene.timeOfDay) parts.push(`Time: ${scene.timeOfDay}`);
    if (scene.mood) parts.push(`Mood: ${scene.mood}`);
    if (scene.notes) parts.push(`Notes: ${scene.notes}`);

    if (scene.objectives && scene.objectives.length > 0) {
      parts.push('Objectives:\n' + scene.objectives.map(o => `- ${o}`).join('\n'));
    }

    return parts.length > 0 ? parts.join('\n') : null;
  }

**Generation Engine — the core prose generation orchestrator.**

```js
// src/engine/generation-engine.js

import { ContextAssembler } from './context-assembler.js';

export class GenerationEngine {
  constructor(llm, db) {
    this.llm = llm;
    this.db = db;
    this.contextAssembler = new ContextAssembler(db);
    this.activeGenerations = new Map();
  }

  async generateScene(sceneId, options = {}) {
    const {
      mode = 'draft',           // 'draft', 'continue', 'rewrite', 'expand'
      customPrompt = null,
      temperature = 0.7,
      maxOutputTokens = 4000,
      stream = true,
      onChunk = null,
      existingContent = null,    // for continue/rewrite modes
      selectionRange = null      // for rewrite mode: { start, end }
    } = options;

    // Load scene and project data
    const scene = await this.db.scenes.get(sceneId);
    if (!scene) throw new Error(`Scene not found: ${sceneId}`);

    const chapter = await this.db.chapters.get(scene.chapterId);
    const project = await this.db.projects.get(chapter.projectId);

    // Assemble context
    const context = await this.contextAssembler.assembleContext(scene, project, {
      maxTokens: 80000,
      customContext: customPrompt
    });

    // Build the generation prompt based on mode
    const messages = this.buildMessages(mode, scene, project, context, {
      existingContent: existingContent || scene.content,
      selectionRange,
      customPrompt
    });

    // Create generation ID for tracking
    const generationId = `gen_${Date.now()}_${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;

    this.activeGenerations.set(generationId, {
      sceneId,
      mode,
      startTime: Date.now(),
      status: 'running'
    });

    try {
      let result;

      if (stream && onChunk) {
        result = await this.streamGeneration(messages, {
          temperature,
          maxOutputTokens,
          onChunk,
          generationId
        });
      } else {
        result = await this.blockGeneration(messages, {
          temperature,
          maxOutputTokens
        });
      }

      // Log the generation
      await this.db.generationLog.add({
        id: generationId,
        sceneId,
        projectId: project.id,
        mode,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        inputTokens: result.usage?.prompt_tokens || 0,
        outputTokens: result.usage?.completion_tokens || 0,
        model: this.llm.getModel('generation'),
        contextSections: context.sections,
        contentLength: result.content.length
      });

      this.activeGenerations.delete(generationId);

      return {
        generationId,
        content: result.content,
        usage: result.usage,
        contextInfo: {
          totalTokens: context.totalTokens,
          sections: context.sections
        }
      };
    } catch (error) {
      this.activeGenerations.set(generationId, {
        ...this.activeGenerations.get(generationId),
        status: 'error',
        error: error.message
      });
      throw error;
    }
  }

  buildMessages(mode, scene, project, context, options) {
    const systemPrompt = this.buildSystemPrompt(project, context);
    const userPrompt = this.buildUserPrompt(mode, scene, options);

    const messages = [
      { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
      { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }
    ];

    return messages;
  }

  buildSystemPrompt(project, context) {
    return `You are a fiction ghostwriter. Your job is to produce prose that is indistinguishable from the author's own writing.

${context.contextText}

=== CRITICAL RULES ===
- Write ONLY prose. No meta-commentary, no "here's the scene", no explanations.
- Match the voice profile EXACTLY. Every sentence should sound like this specific author.
- Maintain perfect consistency with all entity states and world rules.
- Never contradict established facts from the story so far.
- Do not summarize or tell what could be shown. Dramatize.
- Dialogue should sound natural and character-specific.
- Output the scene text and nothing else.`;
  }

  buildUserPrompt(mode, scene, options) {
    switch (mode) {
      case 'draft':
        return this.buildDraftPrompt(scene, options);
      case 'continue':
        return this.buildContinuePrompt(scene, options);
      case 'rewrite':
        return this.buildRewritePrompt(scene, options);
      case 'expand':
        return this.buildExpandPrompt(scene, options);
      default:
        return this.buildDraftPrompt(scene, options);
    }
  }

  buildDraftPrompt(scene, options) {
    let prompt = 'Write this scene from scratch.\n\n';

    if (options.customPrompt) {
      prompt += `Additional direction: ${options.customPrompt}\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Begin the scene now. Write only the prose.';
    return prompt;
  }

  buildContinuePrompt(scene, options) {
    let prompt = '';

    if (options.existingContent) {
      // Include the tail end of existing content for continuity
      const tail = options.existingContent.slice(-

---

Continuing from the generation engine's buildContinuePrompt method.

```js
      const tail = options.existingContent.slice(-3000);
      prompt += `Here is what has been written so far (ending):\n\n---\n${tail}\n---\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Continue writing from exactly where the text left off. Maintain the same voice, tense, and narrative flow. Do not repeat any of the existing text.\n\n';

    if (options.customPrompt) {
      prompt += `Direction for what comes next: ${options.customPrompt}\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Continue the prose now.';
    return prompt;
  }

  buildRewritePrompt(scene, options) {
    let prompt = '';

    if (options.existingContent && options.selectionRange) {
      const before = options.existingContent.slice(Math.max(0, options.selectionRange.start - 500), options.selectionRange.start);
      const selected = options.existingContent.slice(options.selectionRange.start, options.selectionRange.end);
      const after = options.existingContent.slice(options.selectionRange.end, options.selectionRange.end + 500);

      prompt += `Context before the passage:\n---\n${before}\n---\n\n`;
      prompt += `Passage to rewrite:\n---\n${selected}\n---\n\n`;
      if (after.trim()) {
        prompt += `Context after the passage:\n---\n${after}\n---\n\n`;
      }
    } else if (options.existingContent) {
      prompt += `Current scene text to rewrite:\n---\n${options.existingContent}\n---\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Rewrite the indicated passage. Keep the same narrative events and meaning, but improve the prose quality, voice consistency, and impact.\n\n';

    if (options.customPrompt) {
      prompt += `Specific rewrite direction: ${options.customPrompt}\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Write only the rewritten passage.';
    return prompt;
  }

  buildExpandPrompt(scene, options) {
    let prompt = '';

    if (options.existingContent && options.selectionRange) {
      const before = options.existingContent.slice(0, options.selectionRange.start);
      const selected = options.existingContent.slice(options.selectionRange.start, options.selectionRange.end);
      const after = options.existingContent.slice(options.selectionRange.end);

      prompt += `Text before expansion point:\n---\n${before.slice(-1000)}\n---\n\n`;
      prompt += `Passage to expand:\n---\n${selected}\n---\n\n`;
      if (after.trim()) {
        prompt += `Text after expansion point:\n---\n${after.slice(0, 1000)}\n---\n\n`;
      }
    } else if (options.existingContent) {
      prompt += `Current scene text:\n---\n${options.existingContent}\n---\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Expand this passage with more detail, sensory description, interiority, or dramatic development. The expanded version should fit seamlessly between the surrounding text.\n\n';

    if (options.customPrompt) {
      prompt += `Expansion direction: ${options.customPrompt}\n\n`;
    }

    prompt += 'Write only the expanded passage.';
    return prompt;
  }

  async streamGeneration(messages, options) {
    const { temperature, maxOutputTokens, onChunk, generationId } = options;

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'generation',
      temperature,
      max_tokens: maxOutputTokens,
      stream: true
    });

    let fullContent = '';
    let usage = null;

    for await (const chunk of response.stream) {
      // Check if generation was cancelled
      const genState = this.activeGenerations.get(generationId);
      if (genState?.status === 'cancelled') {
        break;
      }

      if (chunk.type === 'content') {
        fullContent += chunk.content;
        onChunk(chunk.content, fullContent);
      } else if (chunk.type === 'usage') {
        usage = chunk.usage;
      }
    }

    return { content: fullContent, usage };
  }

  async blockGeneration(messages, options) {
    const { temperature, maxOutputTokens } = options;

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'generation',
      temperature,
      max_tokens: maxOutputTokens,
      stream: false
    });

    return {
      content: response.content,
      usage: response.usage
    };
  }

  cancelGeneration(generationId) {
    const gen = this.activeGenerations.get(generationId);
    if (gen) {
      gen.status = 'cancelled';
    }
  }
}

// src/engine/entity-tracker.js

export class EntityTracker {
  constructor(llm, db) {
    this.llm = llm;
    this.db = db;
  }

  async extractEntities(text, projectId) {
    const existingEntities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    const existingNames = existingEntities.map(e => e.name);

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `You are a fiction continuity tracker. Extract all named entities from the text and their current states. Output JSON.`
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Known entities: ${existingNames.join(', ') || 'None yet'}

Text to analyze:
---
${text.slice(0, 10000)}
---

Extract entities as JSON:
{
  "entities": [
    {
      "name": "entity name",
      "type": "character|location|object|organization|concept",
      "description": "brief description based on text",
      "state": {
        "key": "value pairs describing current state"
      },
      "relationships": [
        { "type": "relationship type", "target": "other entity name" }
      ],
      "isNew": true/false (false if in known entities list)
    }
  ]
}`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'analysis',
      temperature: 0.2,
      max_tokens: 2000,
      response_format: { type: 'json_object' }
    });

    let extracted;
    try {
      extracted = JSON.parse(response.content);
    } catch (e) {
      const jsonMatch = response.content.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
      if (jsonMatch) {
        extracted = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
      } else {
        return [];
      }
    }

    const results = [];

    for (const entity of (extracted.entities || [])) {
      const existing = existingEntities.find(
        e => e.name.toLowerCase() === entity.name.toLowerCase()
      );

      if (existing) {
        // Merge state updates
        const updatedState = { ...existing.state, ...entity.state };
        const updatedRelationships = this.mergeRelationships(
          existing.relationships || [],
          entity.relationships || []
        );

        await this.db.entities.update(existing.id, {
          state: updatedState,
          relationships: updatedRelationships,
          description: entity.

        results.push({
          ...existing,
          state: updatedState,
          relationships: updatedRelationships,
          action: 'updated'
        });
      } else if (entity.isNew !== false) {
        // Create new entity
        const id = `entity_${Date.now()}_${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
        const newEntity = {
          id,
          projectId,
          name: entity.name,
          type: entity.type || 'character',
          description: entity.description || '',
          aliases: [],
          state: entity.state || {},
          relationships: entity.relationships || [],
          createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
        };

        await this.db.entities.add(newEntity);
        results.push({ ...newEntity, action: 'created' });
      }
    }

    return results;
  }

  mergeRelationships(existing, incoming) {
    const merged = [...existing];

    for (const rel of incoming) {
      const exists = merged.find(
        r => r.type === rel.type && r.target.toLowerCase() === rel.target.toLowerCase()
      );
      if (!exists) {
        merged.push(rel);
      }
    }

    return merged;
  }

  async checkConsistency(text, projectId) {
    const entities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(projectId)
      .toArray();

    if (entities.length === 0) return { consistent: true, issues: [] };

    const entityContext = entities.map(e => {
      let entry = `${e.name} (${e.type}): ${e.description || 'No description'}`;
      if (e.state && Object.keys(e.state).length > 0) {
        entry += '\n  State: ' + JSON.stringify(e.state);
      }
      if (e.relationships && e.relationships.length > 0) {
        entry += '\n  Relationships: ' + e.relationships.map(r => `${r.type} → ${r.target}`).join(', ');
      }
      return entry;
    }).join('\n\n');

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: 'You are a fiction continuity checker. Compare new text against established entity states and flag any inconsistencies. Be precise and cite specific contradictions. Output JSON.'
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Established entity states:
${entityContext}

New text to check:
---
${text.slice(0, 10000)}
---

Check for consistency issues. Output:
{
  "consistent": true/false,
  "issues": [
    {
      "severity": "error|warning|note",
      "entity": "entity name",
      "field": "what aspect is inconsistent",
      "established": "what was established",
      "found": "what the new text says",
      "quote": "relevant quote from new text",
      "suggestion": "how to fix"
    }
  ]
}`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'analysis',
      temperature: 0.2,
      max_tokens: 2000,
      response_format: { type: 'json_object' }
    });

    try {
      return JSON.parse(response.content);
    } catch (e) {
      const jsonMatch = response.content.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
      if (jsonMatch) {
        return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
      }
      return { consistent: true, issues: [] };
    }
  }

  async updateEntityFromEdit(entityId, manualUpdates) {
    const entity = await this.db.entities.get(entityId);
    if (!entity) throw new Error(`Entity not found: ${entityId}`);

    const updates = {
      ...manualUpdates,
      updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
    };

    if (manualUpdates.state) {
      updates.state = { ...entity.state, ...manualUpdates.state };
    }

    if (manualUpdates.relationships) {
      updates.relationships = this.mergeRelationships(
        entity.relationships || [],
        manualUpdates.relationships
      );
    }

    await this.db.entities.update(entityId, updates);
    return { ...entity, ...updates };
  }
}
```

export class SceneSummarizer {
  constructor(llm, db) {
    this.llm = llm;
    this.db = db;
  }

  async summarizeScene(sceneId) {
    const scene = await this.db.scenes.get(sceneId);
    if (!scene || !scene.content) {
      throw new Error('Scene has no content to summarize');
    }

    const chapter = await this.db.chapters.get(scene.chapterId);
    const project = await this.db.projects.get(chapter.projectId);

    // Get entities for this project to ensure summary references them correctly
    const entities = await this.db.entities
      .where('projectId').equals(project.id)
      .toArray();

    const entityNames = entities.map(e => e.name);

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: `You are a precise fiction summarizer. Create summaries that preserve all plot-critical information, character state changes, and revealed information. The summary will be used as context for generating future scenes, so it must capture everything a writer would need to maintain continuity.`
      },
      {
        role: 'user',

```js
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Scene: "${scene.title}" in chapter "${chapter.title}"
Known characters/entities: ${entityNames.join(', ') || 'None tracked'}

Scene text:
---
${scene.content}
---

Write a summary that captures:
1. Key events in order
2. Any character state changes (emotional, physical, relational)
3. New information revealed (to characters or reader)
4. Setting details established
5. Unresolved tensions or setups for future scenes
6. The scene's opening and closing emotional states

Be concise but miss nothing plot-critical. Use character names consistently. Write in present tense.`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'analysis',
      temperature: 0.2,
      max_tokens: 1500
    });

    const summary = response.content.trim();

    // Store the summary on the scene
    await this.db.scenes.update(sceneId, {
      summary,
      summaryGeneratedAt: new Date().toISOString()
    });

    return summary;
  }

  async summarizeChapter(chapterId) {
    const chapter = await this.db.chapters.get(chapterId);
    if (!chapter) throw new Error(`Chapter not found: ${chapterId}`);

    const scenes = await this.db.scenes
      .where('chapterId').equals(chapterId)
      .sortBy('order');

    if (scenes.length === 0) return null;

    // Ensure all scenes have summaries
    for (const scene of scenes) {
      if (scene.content && !scene.summary) {
        await this.summarizeScene(scene.id);
      }
    }

    // Reload scenes with summaries
    const updatedScenes = await this.db.scenes
      .where('chapterId').equals(chapterId)
      .sortBy('order');

    const sceneSummaries = updatedScenes
      .filter(s => s.summary)
      .map(s => `[${s.title}]: ${s.summary}`)
      .join('\n\n');

    if (!sceneSummaries) return null;

    const messages = [
      {
        role: 'system',
        content: 'You are a precise fiction summarizer. Condense multiple scene summaries into a single chapter summary that preserves all continuity-critical information.'
      },
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: `Chapter: "${chapter.title}"

Scene summaries:
${sceneSummaries}

Write a cohesive chapter summary. Preserve all character state changes, plot developments, and setups. Present tense. Be thorough but eliminate redundancy.`
      }
    ];

    const response = await this.llm.chat(messages, {
      purpose: 'analysis',
      temperature: 0.2,
      max_tokens: 2000
    });

    const summary = response.content.trim();

    await this.db.chapters.update(chapterId, {
      summary,
      summaryGeneratedAt: new Date().toISOString()
    });

    return summary;
  }

  async invalidateSummary(sceneId) {
    await this.db.scenes.update(sceneId, {
      summary: null,
      summaryGeneratedAt: null
    });

    // Also invalidate the parent chapter summary
    const scene = await this.db.scenes.get(sceneId);
    if (scene) {
      await this.db.chapters.update(scene.chapterId, {
        summary: null,
        summaryGeneratedAt: null
      });
    }
  }
}

```js
// src/engine/llm-adapter.js

export class LLMAdapter {
  constructor(config = {}) {
    this.providers = {};
    this.modelMap = {
      generation: null,
      analysis: null,
      summary: null
    };
    this.defaultProvider = null;
    this.requestLog = [];
    this.rateLimiter = new RateLimiter();

    if (config.providers) {
      for (const [name, providerConfig] of Object.entries(config.providers)) {
        this.addProvider(name, providerConfig);
      }
    }

    if (config.modelMap) {
      this.modelMap = { ...this.modelMap, ...config.modelMap };
    }
  }

  addProvider(name, config) {
    const { type, apiKey, baseUrl, models, defaultModel } = config;

    this.providers[name] = {
      type,
      apiKey,
      baseUrl: baseUrl || this.getDefaultBaseUrl(type),
      models: models || [],
      defaultModel
    };

    if (!this.defaultProvider) {
      this.defaultProvider = name;
    }
  }

  getDefaultBaseUrl(type) {
    switch (type) {
      case 'openai': return 'https://api.openai.com/v1';
      case 'anthropic': return 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1';
      case 'openrouter': return 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1';
      case 'ollama': return 'http://localhost:11434/api';
      case 'custom': return null;
      default: return null;
    }
  }

  getModel(purpose) {
    if (this.modelMap[purpose]) return this.modelMap[purpose];
    const provider = this.providers[this.defaultProvider];
    return provider?.defaultModel || 'gpt-4o';
  }

  setModelForPurpose(purpose, model) {
    this.modelMap[purpose] = model;
  }

  async chat(messages, options = {}) {
    const {
      purpose = 'generation',
      temperature = 0.7,
      max_tokens = 4000,

      max_tokens = 4000,
      stream = false,
