import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';

const logFile = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab_outputs.md';
const baseDir = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab';

const mappings = [
    { className: 'RichTextEditor', fileName: 'src/ui/components/TextEditor.js', pattern: /class RichTextEditor/ },
    { className: 'HistoryManager', fileName: 'src/editor/HistoryManager.js', pattern: /class HistoryManager/ },
    { className: 'Toolbar', fileName: 'src/ui/components/Toolbar.js', pattern: /class Toolbar/ },
    { className: 'FloatingToolbar', fileName: 'src/editor/FloatingToolbar.js', pattern: /class FloatingToolbar/ },
    { className: 'SlashCommandMenu', fileName: 'src/editor/SlashCommandMenu.js', pattern: /class SlashCommandMenu/ },
    { className: 'LinkPopover', fileName: 'src/editor/LinkPopover.js', pattern: /class LinkPopover/ },
    { className: 'TableManager', fileName: 'src/editor/TableManager.js', pattern: /class TableManager/ },
    { className: 'MarkdownShortcuts', fileName: 'src/editor/MarkdownShortcuts.js', pattern: /class MarkdownShortcuts/ },
    { className: 'DragDropHandler', fileName: 'src/editor/DragDropHandler.js', pattern: /class DragDropHandler/ },
    { className: 'ClipboardHandler', fileName: 'src/editor/ClipboardHandler.js', pattern: /class ClipboardHandler/ },
    { className: 'InlineMarkdown', fileName: 'src/editor/InlineMarkdown.js', pattern: /class InlineMarkdown/ },
    { className: 'UndoManager', fileName: 'src/editor/UndoManager.js', pattern: /class UndoManager/ },
    { className: 'Component', fileName: 'src/ui/component.js', pattern: /class Component/ },
    { className: 'App', fileName: 'src/ui/App.js', pattern: /class App/ },
    { className: 'EditorView', fileName: 'src/ui/views/EditorView.js', pattern: /class EditorView/ },
    { className: 'Sidebar', fileName: 'src/ui/components/Sidebar.js', pattern: /class Sidebar/ },
    { className: 'StatusBar', fileName: 'src/ui/components/StatusBar.js', pattern: /class StatusBar/ },
    { className: 'AIPanel', fileName: 'src/ui/components/AIPanel.js', pattern: /class AIPanel/ },
    { className: 'Dashboard', fileName: 'src/ui/components/Dashboard.js', pattern: /class Dashboard/ },
    { className: 'FindReplace', fileName: 'src/ui/components/FindReplace.js', pattern: /class FindReplace/ },
    { className: 'CommandPalette', fileName: 'src/ui/components/CommandPalette.js', pattern: /class CommandPalette/ },
    { className: 'ThemeManager', fileName: 'src/ui/themeManager.js', pattern: /class ThemeManager/ }
];

const allStrictPatterns = mappings.map(m => new RegExp(`class ${m.className}\\s*{`));

function isMetadata(line) {
    const l = line.trim();
    if (!l) return false;
    if (l.startsWith('---')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('## [')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('**USER:**')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('**AI:**')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('<thinking>')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('</thinking>')) return true;
    if (l.toLowerCase().includes('continuing from')) return true;
    if (l.toLowerCase().includes('resuming from')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('```js')) return true;
    if (l.startsWith('```jsx')) return true;
    if (l === '```') return true;
    return false;
}

function extract() {
    console.log('Reading log file...');
    const content = fs.readFileSync(logFile, 'utf-8');
    const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/);

    const blocks = [];
    let currentBlock = null;
    
    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
        const line = lines[i];
        if (line.startsWith('```js') || line.startsWith('```jsx')) {
            let context = '';
            for (let j = Math.max(0, i - 30); j < i; j++) {
                context += lines[j] + '\n';
            }
            currentBlock = {
                startLine: i,
                content: [],
                language: line.trim().substring(3),
                context: context,
                unclosed: true
            };
        } else if (line.trim() === '```' && currentBlock) {
            currentBlock.endLine = i;
            currentBlock.unclosed = false;
            blocks.push(currentBlock);
            currentBlock = null;
        } else if ((line.startsWith('---') || line.startsWith('## ')) && currentBlock) {
            currentBlock.endLine = i - 1;
            currentBlock.unclosed = true;
            blocks.push(currentBlock);
            currentBlock = null;
        } else if (currentBlock) {
            currentBlock.content.push(line);
        }
    }
    if (currentBlock) {
        currentBlock.endLine = lines.length;
        currentBlock.unclosed = true;
        blocks.push(currentBlock);
    }

    console.log(`Found ${blocks.length} code blocks.`);

    for (const m of mappings) {
        console.log(`Processing ${m.className}...`);
        
        const candidateIndices = [];
        const strictPattern = new RegExp(`class ${m.className}\\s*{`);
        
        for (let i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) {
            if (blocks[i].content.some(l => strictPattern.test(l))) {
                candidateIndices.push(i);
            }
        }

        if (candidateIndices.length === 0) {
            for (let i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) {
                if (blocks[i].content.some(l => m.pattern.test(l))) {
                    candidateIndices.push(i);
                }
            }
        }

        if (candidateIndices.length === 0) {
            console.warn(`Could not find any blocks for ${m.className}`);
            continue;
        }

        let bestResult = { startIdx: -1, codeLines: [], length: -1 };
        
        for (const startIdx of candidateIndices) {
            const foundLineIdx = blocks[startIdx].content.findIndex(l => (strictPattern.test(l) || m.pattern.test(l)));
            let currentCodeLines = blocks[startIdx].content.slice(foundLineIdx).filter(l => !isMetadata(l));
            
            let stopInStartBlock = -1;
            for (let j = 0; j < currentCodeLines.length; j++) {
                if (j === 0) continue;
                if (allStrictPatterns.some(p => p.test(currentCodeLines[j]))) {
                    stopInStartBlock = j;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (stopInStartBlock !== -1) {
                currentCodeLines = currentCodeLines.slice(0, stopInStartBlock);
            } else {
                let currentIdx = startIdx + 1;
                while (currentIdx < blocks.length) {
                    const context = blocks[currentIdx].context.toLowerCase();
                    const isContinuation = (context.includes('continue') || context.includes('continuing') || context.includes('next part') || context.includes('resuming') || context.includes('resumes') || context.includes('part 2'));

                    if (isContinuation) {
                        const newLines = blocks[currentIdx].content.filter(l => !isMetadata(l));
                        if (newLines.length === 0) { currentIdx++; continue; }

                        let stopInContBlock = -1;
                        for (let j = 0; j < newLines.length; j++) {
                            if (allStrictPatterns.some(p => p.test(newLines[j]))) {
                                stopInContBlock = j;
                                break;
                            }
                        }

                        let linesToAdd = stopInContBlock !== -1 ? newLines.slice(0, stopInContBlock) : newLines;

                        let overlapLen = 0;
                        for (let len = 1; len <= Math.min(currentCodeLines.length, linesToAdd.length, 100); len++) {
                            let match = true;
                            for (let j = 0; j < len; j++) {
                                if (currentCodeLines[currentCodeLines.length - len + j].trim() !== linesToAdd[j].trim()) {
                                    match = false;
                                    break;
                                }
                            }
                            if (match) overlapLen = len;
                        }
                        
                        if (overlapLen > 0) {
                            currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd.slice(overlapLen));
                        } else {
                            if (currentCodeLines.length > 0 && linesToAdd.length > 0) {
                                // Find the last NON-EMPTY line
                                let lastLineIdx = currentCodeLines.length - 1;
                                while (lastLineIdx >= 0 && currentCodeLines[lastLineIdx].trim() === '') {
                                    lastLineIdx--;
                                }
                                
                                if (lastLineIdx >= 0) {
                                    const lastLine = currentCodeLines[lastLineIdx];
                                    const firstLine = linesToAdd[0];
                                    const lastTrim = lastLine.trim();
                                    const firstTrim = firstLine.trim();

                                    if (firstTrim.startsWith(lastTrim) && lastTrim.length > 3) {
                                         console.log(`      Replacing repeated partial: "${lastTrim}" with "${firstTrim}"`);
                                         currentCodeLines[lastLineIdx] = linesToAdd[0];
                                         currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd.slice(1));
                                    } else if (lastTrim !== '' && !lastTrim.endsWith(';') && !lastTrim.endsWith('}') && !lastTrim.endsWith('{') && !lastTrim.endsWith(']') && !lastTrim.endsWith(')')) {
                                         console.log(`      Joining complementary: "${lastTrim}" + "${firstTrim}"`);
                                         currentCodeLines[lastLineIdx] = lastLine + firstLine;
                                         currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd.slice(1));
                                    } else {
                                        currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd);
                                    }
                                } else {
                                    currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd);
                                }
                            } else {
                                currentCodeLines.push(...linesToAdd);
                            }
                        }
                        if (stopInContBlock !== -1) break;
                        currentIdx++;
                    } else {
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
            
            const totalLen = currentCodeLines.join('\n').length;
            if (totalLen >= bestResult.length) {
                bestResult = { startIdx, codeLines: currentCodeLines, length: totalLen };
            }
        }

        console.log(`  Selected start block ${bestResult.startIdx}, total code length: ${bestResult.length}`);

        let fullCode = bestResult.codeLines.join('\n');
        const filePath = path.join(baseDir, m.fileName);
        const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
        if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });

        if (!fullCode.trim().startsWith('export') && !fullCode.trim().startsWith('import')) {
            fullCode = 'export ' + fullCode;
        }

        if (m.className === 'RichTextEditor' && m.fileName === 'src/ui/components/TextEditor.js') {
             fullCode = fullCode.replace('class RichTextEditor', 'class TextEditor');
             const imports = [
                 "import { HistoryManager } from '../../editor/HistoryManager.js';",
                 "import { Toolbar } from './Toolbar.js';",
                 "import { FloatingToolbar } from '../../editor/FloatingToolbar.js';",
                 "import { SlashCommandMenu } from '../../editor/SlashCommandMenu.js';",
                 "import { LinkPopover } from '../../editor/LinkPopover.js';",
                 "import { TableManager } from '../../editor/TableManager.js';",
                 "import { MarkdownShortcuts } from '../../editor/MarkdownShortcuts.js';",
                 "import { DragDropHandler } from '../../editor/DragDropHandler.js';",
                 "import { ClipboardHandler } from '../../editor/ClipboardHandler.js';",
                 "import { InlineMarkdown } from '../../editor/InlineMarkdown.js';"
             ];
             fullCode = imports.join('\n') + '\n\n' + fullCode;
        }

        fs.writeFileSync(filePath, fullCode);
        console.log(`  Wrote ${filePath} (${fullCode.length} bytes)`);
    }
}

extract();

Let me try a completely different approach - write it as a very compact script.

```js
import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';

const logFile = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab_outputs.md';
const baseDir = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab';
const TH_O = '<' + 'thinking>';
const TH_C = '</' + 'thinking>';
const UM = '**US' + 'ER:**';
const AM = '**A' + 'I:**';

const mappings = [
    ['RichTextEditor', 'src/ui/components/TextEditor.js', 'TextEditor'],
    ['HistoryManager', 'src/editor/HistoryManager.js'],
    ['Toolbar', 'src/ui/components/Toolbar.js'],
    ['InlineFormatPopover', 'src/editor/InlineFormatPopover.js'],
    ['FloatingToolbar', 'src/editor/FloatingToolbar.js'],
    ['SlashCommandMenu', 'src/editor/SlashCommandMenu.js'],
    ['LinkPopover', 'src/editor/LinkPopover.js'],
    ['TableManager', 'src/editor/TableManager.js'],
    ['MarkdownShortcuts', 'src/editor/MarkdownShortcuts.js'],
    ['DragDropHandler', 'src/editor/DragDropHandler.js'],
    ['ClipboardHandler', 'src/editor/ClipboardHandler.js'],
    ['InlineMarkdown', 'src/editor/InlineMarkdown.js'],
    ['ImageHandler', 'src/editor/ImageHandler.js'],
    ['UndoManager', 'src/editor/UndoManager.js'],
    ['Component', 'src/ui/Component.js'],
    ['App', 'src/ui/App.js'],
    ['EditorView', 'src/ui/views/EditorView.js'],
    ['Sidebar', 'src/ui/components/Sidebar.js'],
    ['StatusBar', 'src/ui/components/StatusBar.js'],
    ['AIPanel', 'src/ui/components/AIPanel.js'],
    ['Dashboard', 'src/ui/components/Dashboard.js'],
    ['FindReplace', 'src/ui/components/FindReplace.js'],
    ['CommandPalette', 'src/ui/components/CommandPalette.js'],
    ['ThemeManager', 'src/ui/ThemeManager.js']
];

const names = mappings.map(m => m[0]);

function isMeta(l) {
    const t = l.trim();
    return !t || /^-{3,}$/.test(t) || t.startsWith('## [') || 
           t.startsWith(UM) || t.startsWith(AM) || 
           t === TH_O || t === TH_C || t.startsWith('```');
}

function findClass(line) {
    for (const n of names) {
        if (new RegExp(`class\\s+${n}\\s*\\{`).test(line)) return n;
    }
    return null;
}

// Extract class body by tracking braces
function extractBody(lines, start) {
    let depth = 0, began = false, body = [];
    for (let i = start; i < lines.length; i++) {
        const line = lines[i];
        if (isMeta(line)) continue;
        for (const ch of line) {
            if (ch === '{') { depth++; began = true; }
            if (ch === '}') depth--;
        }
        body.push(line);
        if (began && depth <= 0) break;
    }
    return body;
}

// Read and parse the log
const content = fs.readFileSync(logFile, 'utf-8');
const lines = content.split('\n');

// Collect all versions of each class (last one wins)
const classVersions = {};

let inCodeBlock = false;
for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
    const line = lines[i];
    if (line.trim().startsWith('```')) {
        inCodeBlock = !inCodeBlock;
        continue;
    }
    if (!inCodeBlock) continue;
    
    const cls = findClass(line);
    if (cls) {
        const body = extractBody(lines, i);
        if (body.length > 5) { // Only keep substantial versions
            classVersions[cls] = body.join('\n');
        }
    }
}

// Write files
for (const [className, filePath, rename] of mappings) {
    if (!classVersions[className]) {
        console.log(`SKIP: ${className} not found`);
        continue;
    }
    
    let code = classVersions[className];
    
    // Rename class if needed
    if (rename) {
        code = code.replace(`class ${className}`, `class ${rename}`);
    }
    
    // Add export
    const exportName = rename || className;
    if (!code.includes('export')) {
        code = code.replace(`class ${exportName}`, `export class ${exportName}`);
    }
    
    const fullPath = path.join(baseDir, filePath);
    const dir = path.dirname(fullPath);
    fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
    fs.writeFileSync(fullPath, code + '\n');
    console.log(`WROTE: ${fullPath} (${code.split('\n').length} lines)`);
}

console.log('\nDone!');
```

The key problem is that every time I write code that contains certain literal strings, the system interprets them as control markers. Let me use hex escapes or char codes for those strings.

```js
import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';

const logFile = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab_outputs.md';
const baseDir = 'e:/Ai/ProseLabV2/proselab';

// Markers built via concatenation to avoid parser issues
const TH_O = '\x3Cthinking\x3E';
const TH_C = '\x3C/thinking\x3E';
const UM = '\x2A\x2AUSER:\x2A\x2A';
const AM = '\x2A\x2AAI:\x2A\x2A';

const mappings = [
    ['RichTextEditor', 'src/ui/components/TextEditor.js', 'TextEditor'],
    ['HistoryManager', 'src/editor/HistoryManager.js'],
    ['Toolbar', 'src/ui/components/Toolbar.js'],
    ['InlineFormatPopover', 'src/editor/InlineFormatPopover.js'],
    ['FloatingToolbar', 'src/editor/FloatingToolbar.js'],
    ['SlashCommandMenu', 'src/editor/SlashCommandMenu.js'],
    ['LinkPopover', 'src/editor/LinkPopover.js'],
    ['TableManager', 'src/editor/TableManager.js'],
    ['MarkdownShortcuts', 'src/editor/MarkdownShortcuts.js'],
    ['DragDropHandler', 'src/editor/DragDropHandler.js'],
    ['ClipboardHandler', 'src/editor/ClipboardHandler.js'],
    ['InlineMarkdown', 'src/editor/InlineMarkdown.js'],
    ['ImageHandler', 'src/editor/ImageHandler.js'],
    ['UndoManager', 'src/editor/UndoManager.js'],
    ['Component', 'src/ui/Component.js'],
    ['App', 'src/ui/App.js'],
    ['EditorView', 'src/ui/views/EditorView.js'],
    ['Sidebar', 'src/ui/components/Sidebar.js'],
    ['StatusBar', 'src/ui/components/StatusBar.js'],
    ['AIPanel', 'src/ui/components/AIPanel.js'],
    ['Dashboard', 'src/ui/components/Dashboard.js'],
    ['FindReplace', 'src/ui/components/FindReplace.js'],
    ['CommandPalette', 'src/ui/components/CommandPalette.js'],
    ['ThemeManager', 'src/ui/ThemeManager.js']
];

const names = mappings.map(m => m[0]);

function isMeta(line) {
    const t = line.trim();
    if (!t) return true;
    if (/^-{3,}$/.test(t)) return true;
    if (t.startsWith('## [')) return true;
    if (t.startsWith(UM) || t.startsWith(AM)) return true;
    if (t === TH_O || t === TH_C) return true;
    if (/^```/.test(t)) return true;
    return false;
}

function findClass(line) {
    for (const n of names) {
        if (new RegExp('class\\s+' + n + '\\s*\\{').test(line)) return n;
    }
    return null;
}

function extractBody(lines, start) {
    let depth = 0, began = false, body = [];
    for (let i = start; i < lines.length; i++) {
        const line = lines[i];
        if (isMeta(line)) {
            if (began && /^```/.test(line.trim())) break;
            continue;
        }
        for (const ch of line) {
            if (ch === '{') { depth++; began = true; }
            if (ch === '}') depth--;
        }
        body.push(line);
        if (began && depth <= 0) break;
    }
    return body;
}

const content = fs.readFileSync(logFile, 'utf-8');
const lines = content.split('\n');
const classVersions = {};

for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
    const cls = findClass(lines[i]);
    if (cls) {
        const body = extractBody(lines, i);
        if (body.length > 5) {
            classVersions[cls] = body.join('\n');
        }
    }
}

for (const [className, filePath, rename] of mappings) {
    if (!classVersions[className]) {
        console.log('SKIP: ' + className + ' not found');
        continue;
    }
    let code = classVersions[className];
    if (rename) {
        code = code.replace('class ' + className, 'class ' + rename);
    }
    const exportName = rename || className;
    if (!code.includes('export')) {
        code = code.replace('class ' + exportName, 'export class ' + exportName);
    }
    const fullPath = path.join(baseDir, filePath);
    fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(fullPath), { recursive: true });
    fs.writeFileSync(fullPath, code + '\n');
    console.log('WROTE: ' + fullPath + ' (' + code.split('\n').length + ' lines)');
}

console.log('Done!');
```

    const t = line.trim();
    if (!t) return true;
    if (/^-{3,}$/.test(t)) return true;
    if (t.startsWith('## [')) return true;
    if (t.startsWith(UM) || t.startsWith(AM)) return true;
    if (t === TH_O || t === TH_C) return true;
    if (/^```/.test(t)) return true;
    return false;
}

function findClass(line) {
    for (const n of names) {
        if (new RegExp('class\\s+' + n + '\\s*\\{').test(line)) return n;
    }
    return null;
}

function extractBody(lines, start) {
    let depth = 0, began = false, body = [];
    for (let i = start; i < lines.length; i++) {
        const line = lines[i];
        if (isMeta(line)) {
            if (began && /^```/.test(line.trim())) break;

        }
        for (const ch of line) {
            if (ch === '{') { depth++; began = true; }
            if (ch === '}') depth--;
        }
        body.push(line);
        if (began && depth <= 0) break;
    }
    return body;
}

const content = fs.readFileSync(logFile, 'utf-8');

for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {

Save as `extract.mjs` and run with `node extract.mjs`. It scans the log for every class definition, keeps the **last (most recent) version** of each, does brace-matching to extract the full body, renames `RichTextEditor→TextEditor` if needed, adds `export`, and writes each file to the correct path. Classes not found in the log will print `SKIP`.

# Fixing the Import Classification System

The file you shared is `DashboardWidgets.js` — the UI components. The classification logic lives in **Dashboard.js** itself. Let me show you exactly what needs to change.

## The Problem

Your import system has a hardcoded category list missing "Manuscript" and the auto-detect function doesn't know what a full novel looks like.

## Find and Fix in `Dashboard.js`

**Step 1: Find your category definitions** — search for the array that contains `'Notes'`, `'Characters'`, `'Scenes'`:

```javascript
// FIND something like this:
const FILE_CATEGORIES = [
  { id: 'notes', label: '📝 Notes', icon: '📝' },
  { id: 'characters', label: '👤 Characters', icon: '👤' },
  { id: 'scenes', label: '🎬 Scenes', icon: '🎬' },
  { id: 'worldbuilding', label: '🌍 World Building', icon: '🌍' },
  { id: 'outline', label: '📋 Outline', icon: '📋' },
];

// REPLACE with:
const FILE_CATEGORIES = [
  { id: 'manuscript', label: '📖 Manuscript', icon: '📖' },
  { id: 'notes', label: '📝 Notes', icon: '📝' },
  { id: 'characters', label: '👤 Characters', icon: '👤' },
  { id: 'scenes', label: '🎬 Scenes', icon: '🎬' },
  { id: 'worldbuilding', label: '🌍 World Building', icon: '🌍' },
  { id: 'outline', label: '📋 Outline', icon: '📋' },
];
```

**Step 2: Find or add the auto-detect function** — search for where the category gets assigned during import:

```javascript
// ADD or REPLACE the auto-detect function:
function detectFileCategory(filename, content) {
  const lower = filename.toLowerCase();
  const text = content || '';
  const textLower = text.toLowerCase();

  // ── Manuscript detection (check FIRST) ──
  const manuscriptSignals = [
    /\b(prologue|epilogue)\b/i,
    /^#{1,3}\s*(chapter|ch\.?\s*\d)/im,
    /\bCHAPTER\s+[IVXLCDM\d]+/m,
    /\bPART\s+(ONE|TWO|THREE|FOUR|[IVXLCDM]+|\d+)\b/i,
    /^#{1,2}\s*\*\*[A-Z]/m,
  ];

  const signalHits = manuscriptSignals.filter(rx => rx.test(text)).length;
  const isLargeFile = text.length > 5000;
  const hasMultipleHeadings = (text.match(/^#{1,3}\s+/gm) || []).length >= 3;
  const hasDraftInName = /draft|manuscript|novel|book|complete/i.test(lower);

  // If it looks like a manuscript, classify it as one
  if (
    (signalHits >= 2) ||
    (signalHits >= 1 && isLargeFile) ||
    (isLargeFile && hasMultipleHeadings) ||
    (hasDraftInName && isLargeFile)
  ) {
    return 'manuscript';
  }

  // ── Other categories ──
  if (/character|cast|persona/i.test(lower)) return 'characters';
  if (/scene|sequence/i.test(lower)) return 'scenes';
  if (/world|setting|lore|magic/i.test(lower)) return 'worldbuilding';
  if (/outline|structure|beat/i.test(lower)) return 'outline';

  // Content-based fallbacks
  if (/\bage\b.*\bappearance\b|\bmotivation\b.*\bflaw\b/i.test(textLower)) return 'characters';
  if (/\bint\.\b|\bext\.\b|\bfade in\b/i.test(textLower)) return 'scenes';

  return 'notes'; // default fallback
}
```

**Step 3: Find where files get classified during import** and wire in the detection:

```javascript
// FIND the import handler — look for something like:
// handleFilesImported, onImport, processImportedFiles, etc.
// Where it sets the category for each file.

// It probably looks something like:
const classifiedFiles = files.map(file => ({
  ...file,
  category: 'notes',  // <-- THIS is the problem: hardcoded default
}));

// REPLACE with:
const classifiedFiles = files.map(file => ({
  ...file,
  category: detectFileCategory(file.name, file.content),
}));
```

    if (chapters.length > 0) {
      setExtractedChapters(chapters);
      setShowChapterReview(true); // prompt user to confirm chapter splits
    }

Continuing from Step 4:

```javascript
function handleClassifiedImport(file) {
  if (file.category === 'manuscript') {
    setCurrentDocument({
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      title: file.name.replace(/\.(md|txt|docx)$/i, ''),
      content: file.content,
      type: 'manuscript',
      importedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      wordCount: file.content.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length,
    });

    const chapters = extractChaptersFromManuscript(file.content);
    if (chapters.length > 0) {
      setExtractedChapters(chapters);
      setShowChapterReview(true);
    }
  } else {
    // Handle non-manuscript files normally
    addFileToCategory(file.category, {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      title: file.name.replace(/\.(md|txt|docx)$/i, ''),
      content: file.content,
      importedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    });
  }
}
```

Step 5 — the chapter extraction utility:

```javascript
function extractChaptersFromManuscript(content) {
  // Split on common chapter heading patterns
  const chapterPattern = /^(#{1,3}\s+)?(Chapter\s+[\dIVXLCDM]+[^\n]*|CHAPTER\s+[\dIVXLCDM]+[^\n]*|Prologue|Epilogue|PART\s+(?:ONE|TWO|THREE|FOUR|FIVE|[\dIVXLCDM]+)[^\n]*)/gim;

  const matches = [...content.matchAll(chapterPattern)];

  if (matches.length === 0) {
    // Fallback: try splitting on markdown headings
    const headingPattern = /^#{1,2}\s+.+$/gm;
    const headingMatches = [...content.matchAll(headingPattern)];

    if (headingMatches.length < 2) return [];

    return headingMatches.map((match, i) => {
      const start = match.index;
      const end = headingMatches[i + 1]?.index || content.length;
      const chapterContent = content.slice(start, end).trim();
      return {
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        title: match[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim(),
        content: chapterContent,
        order: i,
        wordCount: chapterContent.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length,
      };
    });
  }

  return matches.map((match, i) => {
    const start = match.index;
    const end = matches[i + 1]?.index || content.length;
    const chapterContent = content.slice(start, end).trim();
    return {
      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      title: match[0].replace(/^#+\s*/, '').trim(),
      content: chapterContent,
      order: i,
      wordCount: chapterContent.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length,
    };
  });
}
```

Step 6 — wire it all together in the import flow. Find the existing import handler (likely called something like `handleImport` or `onFilesSelected`) and make sure it calls `detectFileCategory` and then `handleClassifiedImport`:

```javascript
// In your import handler:
async function handleImport(rawFiles) {
  const processed = await Promise.all(
    rawFiles.map(async (file) => {
      const content = await readFileContent(file);
      const category = detectFileCategory(file.name, content);
      return { name: file.name, content, category };
    })
  );

  // Show classification review modal so user can override
  setImportQueue(processed);
  setShowImportReview(true);
}

// When user confirms the classification:
function confirmImport(reviewedFiles) {
  reviewedFiles.forEach(file => handleClassifiedImport(file));
  setShowImportReview(false);
  setImportQueue([]);
}
```

That's the complete fix. Summary of what was added/changed:
1. Added `'manuscript'` to `FILE_CATEGORIES` array
2. Added `detectFileCategory()` with manuscript-aware heuristics
3. Added `handleClassifiedImport()` to route manuscripts to the editor
4. Added `extractChaptersFromManuscript()` to split into navigable chapters
5. Wired the detection into the import flow

The key insight: manuscripts get detected by a combination of signals (chapter headings, file size > 5000 chars, multiple headings, filename keywords like "draft" or "novel"). Two or more signals = manuscript classification. One signal + large file = also manuscript. This prevents short character notes with one "Chapter" mention from being misclassified.

```

When `currentStep === 4` (the "review" step), it calls `executeImport()` and then `return`s — it never calls `setCurrentStep`. The step advancement to the result page is supposed to happen inside `executeImport`:

```javascript
const executeImport = useCallback(async () => {
    setIsProcessing(true);
    abortRef.current = false;
    setProgress({ percent: 0, label: 'Preparing import...', detail: '' });

    try {
      const readyFiles = importFiles.filter(f => f.status === 'ready');
      const resolutions = {};
      Object.entries(conflictResolutions).forEach(([id, res]) => {
        resolutions[id] = res;
      });

      const result = await orchestrator.execute(
        projectId, 
        readyFiles, 
        resolutions, 
        {}, // Options
        (prog) => {
          setProgress({
            percent: prog.current,
            label: prog.message,
            detail: prog.detail || ''
          });
        }
      );

      const finalResult = {
        ...result,
        success: true,
        totalImported: result.summary ? Object.values(result.summary).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) : 0,
        totalFiles: readyFiles.length
      };

      setImportResult(finalResult);
      if (onImportComplete) onImportComplete(finalResult);
      setCurrentStep(STEPS.length - 1); // <-- This should advance to step 5 (result)
    } catch (err) {
      setError(`Import failed: ${err.message}`);
    } finally {
      setIsProcessing(false);
    }
  }, [importFiles, conflictResolutions, projectId, orchestrator, onImportComplete]);
```

Potential failure points:

6. **Silent error** - The catch block sets `setError(...)` but doesn't advance the step. If the error UI isn't visible or the error message is empty, the user might not see it.

- `projectId` is undefined → "Project not found" error
- `applyImport` fails because the file format from the hook doesn't match what it expects
- `storage` methods don't exist or fail

Which calls `this.executeImport()`:

```javascript
async executeImport(projectId, files, options, onProgress) {
    this.onProgress = onProgress;
    const project = await this.storage.getProject(projectId);
    if (!project) throw new Error("Project not found");

    const resolutions = {};
    if (options.conflicts) {
      options.conflicts.forEach(c => {
        resolutions[c.id] = c.resolution;
      });
    }

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 0, total: 100, message: "Applying import logic..." });

    const { updatedProject, changelog, warnings, summary } = await applyImport(project, files, resolutions);

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 50, total: 100, message: "Saving changes to database..." });

    await this.storage.updateProjectMetadata({
      chars: updatedProject.characters,
      rules: updatedProject.worldRules,
      beats: updatedProject.beats,
      voice: updatedProject.voice
    });

    for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters) {
      if (chapter.id.startsWith('imp_')) {
        const newChapter = await this.storage.createChapter({
          projectId,
          title: chapter.title,
          order: chapter.order,
          importedAt: chapter.importedAt
        });

        await this.storage.createScene({
          projectId,
          chapterId: newChapter.id,
          title: "Imported Content",
          text: chapter.content,
          order: 0
        });
      } else {
        const isUpdated = changelog.find(c => c.type === 'Chapter' && c.detail === chapter.title && (c.action === 'Updated' || c.action === 'Merged'));
        if (isUpdated) {
          await this.storage.updateChapter(projectId, chapter.id, {
            title: chapter.title,
            order: chapter.order
          });
          const scenes = await this.storage.getScenes(chapter.id);
          if (scenes && scenes.length > 0) {
            await this.storage.updateSceneText(scenes[0].id, chapter.content);
          }
        }
      }
    }

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 100, total: 100, message: "Import complete!" });

    return { summary, changelog, warnings };
  }

```javascript
export async function applyImport(projectData, importPlan, conflictResolutions

---



<thinking>
Let me finalize my analysis. I've been going back and forth, so let me consolidate the bugs I've found.

Let me re-examine the orchestrator's `executeImport` method carefully:

```javascript
async executeImport(projectId, files, options, onProgress) {
    this.onProgress = onProgress;
    const project = await this.storage.getProject(projectId);
    if (!project) throw new Error("Project not found");

    const resolutions = {};
    if (options.conflicts) {
      options.conflicts.forEach(c => {
        resolutions[c.id] = c.resolution;
      });
    }

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 0, total: 100, message: "Applying import logic..." });

    const { updatedProject, changelog, warnings, summary } = await applyImport(project, files, resolutions);

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 50, total: 100, message: "Saving changes to database..." });

    await this.storage.updateProjectMetadata({
      chars: updatedProject.characters,
      rules: updatedProject.worldRules,
      beats: updatedProject.beats,
      voice: updatedProject.voice
    });

    for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters) {
      if (chapter.id.startsWith('imp_')) {
        const newChapter = await this.storage.createChapter({
          projectId,
          title: chapter.title,
          order: chapter.order,
          importedAt: chapter.importedAt
        });

        await this.storage.createScene({
          projectId,
          chapterId: newChapter.id,
          title: "Imported Content",
          text: chapter.content,
          order: 0
        });
      } else {
        const isUpdated = changelog.find(c => c.type === 'Chapter' && c.detail === chapter.title && (c.action === 'Updated' || c.action === 'Merged'));
        if (isUpdated) {
          await this.storage.updateChapter(projectId, chapter.id, {
            title: chapter.title,
            order: chapter.order
          });
          const scenes = await this.storage.getScenes(chapter.id);
          if (scenes && scenes.length > 0) {
            await this.storage.updateSceneText(scenes[0].id, chapter.content);
          }
        }
      }
    }

    if (onProgress) onProgress({ current: 100, total: 100, message: "Import complete!" });

    return { summary, changelog, warnings };
  }
```

Now `applyImport` deep clones the project: `const updatedProject = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(projectData));`

If the project from storage doesn't have a `chapters` array, `updatedProject.chapters` would be undefined. Then `for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters)` would throw `TypeError: updatedProject.chapters is not iterable`.

But wait - `applyImport` initializes arrays if they don't exist:
```javascript
function applyManuscriptImport(project, item, ...) {
  if (!project.chapters) project.chapters = [];
  ...
}
```

But this only happens if there's a manuscript item. If no files are categorized as 'manuscript', `project.chapters` might remain undefined. Then the `for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters)` in the orchestrator would throw.

Actually wait, even if there ARE manuscript files, `applyManuscriptImport` sets `project.chapters = []` on the cloned project. But what if the original project from storage already has chapters? Then `project.chapters` would already be an array.

The issue is: if the project from storage has NO `chapters` property AND no files are categorized as 'manuscript', then `updatedProject.chapters` would be undefined, and the `for...of` loop would throw.

But even if it does throw, the error would be caught by the hook's catch block and `setError` would be called. The problem is the error is not displayed.

Let me now list ALL the bugs I've found:

**BUG 1 (CRITICAL - "nothing happens" root cause): Error messages are only displayed in the upload step**

In `ImportWizard.jsx`, the error display `{wizard.error && <div className="error-message">{wizard.error}</div>}` is only inside `renderUploadStep()`. When an import error occurs on the review step, `setError()` is called but the error is never rendered because the user is on the review step, not the upload step. The step doesn't advance (because the error was caught before `setCurrentStep` was called), `isProcessing` goes back to false, and the user sees no feedback.

**BUG 2 (CRITICAL - causes the error that BUG 1 hides): `updatedProject.chapters` may be undefined**

In `ImportOrchestrator.executeImport()`, after `applyImport` returns, the code iterates `for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters)`. If the project has no chapters and no manuscript files were imported, `updatedProject.chapters` is undefined, causing a TypeError. Even if the project does have chapters, this loop iterates over ALL existing chapters (not just new/updated ones), which is wasteful and could cause errors with storage methods.

Actually wait, let me reconsider. If the project from storage has chapters, they'd be in the cloned project. <thinking>
Let me give my final assessment based on all the code I've traced through.

The user's complaint is that clicking "Import Now" on the review step results in "nothing happens."

Here's my final diagnosis:

**The primary bug is a combination of two issues working together:**

**1. Error swallowing due to misplaced error display (the visible symptom)**

In `src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.jsx`, the error message rendering:
```javascript
{wizard.error && (
  <div className="error-message">{wizard.error}</div>
)}
```
is only inside `renderUploadStep()`. It is NOT rendered globally or in the review/result steps. So when `executeImport` fails and calls `setError(...)`, the error state is set but never displayed to the user. The `isProcessing` flag goes back to false, the step doesn't advance, and the user sees absolutely no change — hence "nothing happens."

**2. The likely error being swallowed: `storage` dependency issues**

The orchestrator's `executeImport` calls several `this.storage` methods: `getProject()`, `updateProjectMetadata()`, `createChapter()`, `createScene()`, `updateChapter()`, `getScenes()`, `updateSceneText()`. If `storage` is not passed as a prop (or is passed with a different API shape than what the orchestrator expects), any of these calls will throw. The error is caught, stored in state via `setError()`, but invisible per bug #1.

Additionally, even if storage works, `updatedProject.chapters` could be undefined if the project object from storage doesn't have a `chapters` array and no manuscript-category files were imported — the `for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters)` line would throw a TypeError that gets silently swallowed.

**3. Classifications not applied (compounding issue)**

User reclassifications from the classify step are stored in a separate `classifications` state but never merged back into the file objects before `executeImport` passes them to the orchestrator. This means files may be processed under wrong categories, potentially resulting in zero actual imports (all files hit the `default` case in `applyImport` which just logs a warning and skips them). The import "succeeds" with `summary` showing all zeros, but the user sees nothing imported.

**The fix requires:**

1. **Move the error display out of `renderUploadStep()` and into the main wizard body** so errors are visible regardless of which step the user is on.

2. **Merge `classifications` into file objects before passing to the orchestrator** in `executeImport`:
```javascript
const readyFiles = importFiles
  .filter(f => f.status === 'ready')
  .map(f => ({ ...f, category: classifications[f.id] || f.category }));
```

3. **Guard against undefined `chapters`** in the orchestrator's `executeImport`:
```javascript
for (const chapter of (updatedProject.chapters || [])) {
```

The root cause of "nothing happens" is bug #1 — the error display is scoped to the wrong step, making all import failures completely silent.

```javascript

async execute(projectId, files, conflictResolutions, options = {}, onProgress) {

This passes to `executeImport(projectId, files, importOptions, onProgress)` — 4 arguments.

async executeImport(projectId, files, options, onProgress) {
```

Inside `executeImport`, the conflict resolutions are extracted from `options.conflicts`:
```javascript

```

But in `execute`, `importOptions` is constructed as:

```

The `conflictResolutions` parameter (which is the `resolutions` object from the hook — a map of `{conflictId: resolution}`) is passed as the third argument to `execute` but is NEVER used. It's not put into `importOptions`. The `options` parameter (4th argument, `{}`) is spread into `importOptions`, which has no `conflicts` property.

So the conflict resolutions from the user are completely lost! They're passed to `execute` but never forwarded to `executeImport`. The `resolutions` object inside `executeImport` will always be empty `{}`.

This means:
- If there are conflicts, they'll never be resolved during import
- `applyImport` receives empty resolutions
- In `applyImport`, when processing files that have conflicts, the resolution lookup `conflictResolutions[item.conflictId]` returns undefined
- The default behavior when no resolution is found would depend on the implementation — it might skip the file entirely or use a default strategy

This is another bug, but it wouldn't cause "nothing happens" — it would cause conflicts to be handled incorrectly.

OK, I'm confident in my final assessment now. The primary cause of "nothing happens" is:

1. **The error display is only rendered in the upload step** — any error during import execution is invisible to the user on the review step.

2. **The most likely trigger for the hidden error** is either a storage API mismatch, undefined `projectId`, or `updatedProject.chapters` being undefined.

3. **Contributing factor**: classifications aren't merged back into files, so files may be processed under wrong categories, potentially resulting in zero meaningful imports even if no error occurs.

4. **Additional bug**: conflict resolutions are passed to `orchestrator.execute()` but never forwarded to `executeImport()` — they're silently dropped.

To summarize my final diagnosis:

**Root cause of "nothing happens" when clicking Import Now:**

1. **PRIMARY: Error display scoped only to upload step** — In `ImportWizard.jsx`, the `{wizard.error && <div className="error-message">...}` block is only inside `renderUploadStep()`. When `executeImport` fails on the review step (step 4), `setError()` is called but the error is never rendered. The step doesn't advance, `isProcessing` returns to false, and the user sees zero feedback.

2. **The hidden error is likely caused by one of:**
   - `updatedProject.chapters` being undefined when no manuscript files exist and the project has no chapters array, causing `for (const chapter of updatedProject.chapters)` to throw TypeError
   - Storage API method mismatch (e.g., `storage.getProject` doesn't exist or has different signature)
   - `projectId` being undefined/null

3. **Classifications not applied** — User reclassifications stored in `classifications` state are never merged back into file objects before import. Files are passed with their original auto-classified `category`, so user changes on the classify step are ignored.

4. **Conflict resolutions dropped** — `orchestrator.execute(projectId, files, resolutions, {}, callback)` receives resolutions as the 3rd parameter but never incorporates them into the options passed to `executeImport`. They're silently discarded.

5. **Minor: Changelog rendering** — Changelog entries are objects `{action, type, detail}` but rendered directly as `<li>{log}</li>`, which would display `[object Object]`.

<thinking>
The user is frustrated because my responses keep getting cut off. Let me analyze why.

The ironic issue is: I'm in STRICT THINKING MODE where I can only output inside thinking tags. Every time I try to write the string for a closing thinking tag as part of code or explanation, the model interprets it as the actual closing of my thinking block, which terminates my response.

So when I write code like:
```python
if output.count("opening-thinking-tag") > output.count("closing-thinking-tag")
```

The moment I type the literal closing thinking tag characters, it closes MY thinking block and cuts me off.

This is a fundamental limitation of the current output mode. The closing tag for thinking is being interpreted literally rather than as content.

Let me try to work around this by using string concatenation or escaping.

The solution for the user's problem (auto-continue in their Python script):

```python
CLOSE_THINK = "<" + "/thinking" + ">"
OPEN_THINK = "<" + "thinking" + ">"

def is_response_complete(output):
    output = output.strip()
    open_count = output.count(OPEN_THINK)
    close_count = output.count(CLOSE_THINK)
    if open_count > close_count:
        return False
    if output.endswith((",", ".", "-", ":")):
        return False  # might be mid-sentence
    return True

def auto_continue(initial_prompt, max_continuations=5):
    full_output = ""
    prompt = initial_prompt
    
    for i in range(max_continuations + 1):
        response = run_galaxy_ai(prompt)  # their existing function
        full_output += response
        
        if is_response_complete(full_output):
            break
        
        prompt = "Continue from where you left off. Do not repeat anything already said."
    
    return full_output
```

The user should wrap their existing `run_galaxy_ai` call in this loop. The key pieces:

1. `is_response_complete()` - heuristic to detect truncation
2. `auto_continue()` - loop that keeps sending "continue" prompts
3. Concatenate all partial outputs into one full response

Additional heuristics for detecting truncation:
- Code blocks opened but not closed (count backtick-triples)
- Response ends mid-word
- Response is exactly at or near the token limit
- The API response might include a `finish_reason` field - if it says "length" rather than "stop", the response was truncated

The user should also check if Galaxy AI's API response includes a `finish_reason` or similar field in the run details. That would be the most reliable way to detect truncation rather than using text heuristics.

For streaming: the user should check if Galaxy AI supports Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoints, typically something like `POST /api/v1/runs/stream` or adding an `Accept: text/event-stream` header. This would eliminate the need for polling entirely.

For increasing token limits: check if the run creation payload accepts a `max_tokens` or `max_output_tokens` parameter that can be increased.

That's the complete solution. The reason my previous responses kept cutting off was the literal closing thinking tag in my code examples being interpreted as my own closing tag.

Let me look at the ImportWizard integration:

```
{showImportWizard && (
  <ImportWizard
    projectId={selectedProjectId}
    existingData={projects.find(p => p.id === selectedProjectId)}
    storage={docManager}
    llm={{ generate: llmService.complete }}

    onClose={() => setShowImportWizard(false)}

)}
```

2. The `docManager` is created via `createImportStorageAdapter` with a massive dependency array in `useMemo`.

3. The `llmService` is created via `useMemo` with `ENV_KEYS.openai` and `ENV_KEYS.model` as dependencies.

4. The `handleImportComplete` is created via `useMemo` with `createImportCompletionHandler`.

Now, the key issue - "freezes at step 5 when clicking Import Now button". Let me think about what could cause this:

**Problem 1: `docManager` useMemo dependency instability**

The `docManager` is created with `useMemo` but its dependency array includes many values that could change during the import process. When the import starts creating projects, chapters, scenes, characters, etc., the dependencies change, causing `docManager` to be recreated. This could cause the ImportWizard to re-render with a new `storage` prop mid-import, potentially resetting its internal state or causing the import operation to reference stale closures.

Specifically, look at the dependencies:
- `projects` - changes when a project is created
- `selectedProjectId` - changes when a project is selected
- `tree` - changes when chapters/scenes are added
- `scenes` - changes when scenes are added
- `chars` - changes when characters are added
- `rules` - changes when rules are added
- `beats` - changes when beats are added
- `core` - changes when project metadata is updated

During import at step 5 ("Import Now"), the wizard would be calling storage methods to create the project, chapters, scenes, characters, world rules, and beats. Each of these operations would trigger state changes in the parent component, which would cause `docManager` to be recreated via `useMemo`, which would pass a new `storage` prop to ImportWizard, potentially causing it to re-render and lose its import state.

**Problem 2: `llmService` useMemo with `ENV_KEYS`**

The `llmService` depends on `ENV_KEYS.openai` and `ENV_KEYS.model`. But `ENV_KEYS` is defined as a constant outside the component, so these shouldn't change. However, looking more carefully:

```javascript
const ENV_KEYS = {
  openai: import.meta.env.VITE_OPENAI_KEY || "",
  gemini: import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_KEY || "",
  model: import.meta.env.VITE_OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3",
};
```

These are module-level constants, so they won't change. The `useMemo` dependency on `ENV_KEYS.openai` and `ENV_KEYS.model` is fine since they're stable references (strings).

**Problem 3: `handleImportComplete` causing state changes that unmount ImportWizard**

The `handleImportComplete` calls `selectProject`, `refreshProjectState`, and `selectScene`. If this is called during the import process (not after), it could cause cascading state updates.

2. `docManager` useMemo recalculates because `projects` changed
3. ImportWizard receives new `storage` prop
4. ImportWizard re-renders, potentially resetting its internal import state
5. The import process gets stuck in a loop or loses its reference to the ongoing operation

**Problem 4: Infinite re-render loop**

In App.jsx, the `docManager` is created with:
```javascript
const docManager = useMemo(() => createImportStorageAdapter({...}), [
    projects, selectedProjectId, selectedSceneId, tree,
    selectProject, refreshProjectState, selectScene, createProject, deleteProject, createChapter, createScene,
    deleteChapter, deleteScene, updateSceneMetadata, updateProjectMetadata, updateProjectData,

    createCharacter, createWorldRule, createBeat,
    getProject, getChapters, getScenes, getCharacters, getWorldRules, getBeats, findCharacterByName,
    updateSceneText
]);
```

The dependency array includes `projects`, `scenes`, `core`, `chars`, `rules`, `beats`, `voice`, `tree`, `selectedProjectId`, `selectedSceneId` - all of which are STATE values that change during import.

When the ImportWizard calls storage methods at step 5 to create the project, chapters, scenes, characters, etc.:

1. `createProject` is called → updates `projects` state → `docManager` useMemo recalculates → ImportWizard gets new `storage` prop → re-renders
2. `createChapter` is called → updates `chapters` state → `tree` changes → `docManager` recalculates → ImportWizard re-renders again
3. `createScene` is called → updates `scenes` state AND `selectedSceneId` → `docManager` recalculates → ImportWizard re-renders again
4. Each character/rule/beat save → updates `chars`/`rules`/`beats` → `docManager` recalculates

This cascade of re-renders could cause the ImportWizard to lose its internal state or get stuck in a render loop.

Additionally, look at `createScene` in useDocumentManager:
```javascript
const createScene = useCallback(async (arg1, arg2 = "New Scene") => {
    ...
    if (newScene.projectId === selectedProjectId) {
        setScenes(updatedScenes);
        setSelectedSceneId(newScene.id);  // <-- This changes selectedSceneId on EVERY scene creation!
    }
    return newScene;
}, [selectedProjectId, scenes]);
```

During import, if we're creating many scenes, each one sets `selectedSceneId`, which triggers:
1. The scene sync useEffect (sets text and modeFeedback)
2. The auto-save useEffect (tries to save the scene)
3. The `docManager` useMemo recalculation

And `createScene` depends on `scenes` in its useCallback dependency array, which means every time scenes state updates, `createScene` gets a new reference, which would also cause `docManager` to recalculate.

Similarly, `createChapter` depends on `chapters.length`:
```javascript

    ...
}, [selectedProjectId, chapters.length]);
```

This is slightly better since it only changes when the count changes, but still problematic during bulk import.

**The core fix would be:**

1. **Stabilize the `docManager` reference** - Don't include state values in the useMemo dependency array. Instead, use refs or pass the functions directly without wrapping them in a new object on every state change.

2. **Prevent `createScene` from setting `selectedSceneId` during import** - Either add a flag to skip this, or have the import adapter use a different method.

3. **Batch the state updates** - Instead of updating state after each individual create operation, batch all creates and update state once at the end.

**Root cause analysis of the Step 5 freeze:**

1. **`createScene` sets `selectedSceneId` on every call during import:**
In `useDocumentManager.js`, `createScene` does:
```
if (newScene.projectId === selectedProjectId) {
    setScenes(updatedScenes);
    setSelectedSceneId(newScene.id);  // THIS IS THE PROBLEM
}
```
During import, when `createProject` is called first, it sets `selectedProjectId` to the new project. Then every subsequent `createScene` call matches `newScene.projectId === selectedProjectId`, so it sets `selectedSceneId` for EVERY scene created.

2. **Each `setSelectedSceneId` triggers cascading effects in App.jsx:**
- The scene sync `useEffect` fires, calling `setText()` and `setModeFeedback()`
- The auto-save `useEffect` fires, calling `saveSceneDraft()`
- `saveSceneDraft` updates `scenes` state again
- The `docManager` useMemo recalculates

3. **`createScene` depends on `scenes` state:**
```
}, [selectedProjectId, scenes]);
```
Every time scenes state updates, `createScene` gets a new function reference. If the import wizard is iterating through scenes and calling createScene sequentially, each call updates `scenes`, which creates a new `createScene` reference, which could cause issues if the import wizard captures the function reference.

4. **`createProject` sets `selectedProjectId`:**
```
setSelectedProjectId(newProject.id);
```
This triggers the `useEffect` that loads project state:
```
useEffect(() => {
    if (!selectedProjectId) return;
    loadProjectStateBundle(db, selectedProjectId)
      .then(applyLoadedProjectState)
      ...
}, [selectedProjectId, applyLoadedProjectState]);
```
This races with the import process - the import is trying to create chapters/scenes while the project state is being loaded asynchronously.

5. **The `docManager` useMemo in App.jsx has ~40+ dependencies** including state values like `projects`, `scenes`, `chars`, `rules`, `beats`, `core`, `voice`, `tree`, `selectedProjectId`, `selectedSceneId`. Every state change during import recreates this object, causing ImportWizard to re-render with a new `storage` prop.

The combination of all these issues creates a cascade that either:
- Freezes the UI due to rapid re-renders
- Creates a race condition where the import process loses track of its state
- Causes an infinite loop where state updates trigger more state updates

Let me prepare my diagnosis and potential fixes:

**Fix 1: Prevent `createScene` from setting `selectedSceneId` during bulk operations**
Add an option to skip the side effect:
```javascript
const createScene = useCallback(async (arg1, arg2 = "New Scene") => {
    let sceneData = typeof arg1 === 'object' ? arg1 : { chapterId: arg1, title: arg2 };
    const resolvedProjectId = sceneData.projectId || selectedProjectId;
    if (!resolvedProjectId) return null;
    
    const skipSelect = sceneData._skipSelect === true;
    delete sceneData._skipSelect;
    
    const newScene = { ... };
    await db.createScene(newScene);
    
    if (!skipSelect && newScene.projectId === selectedProjectId) {
        const updatedScenes = await db.listScenesByProject(newScene.projectId);
        setScenes(updatedScenes);
        setSelectedSceneId(newScene.id);
    }
    return newScene;
}, [selectedProjectId, scenes]);
```

**Fix 2: Use the orchestratorAliases which go directly to DB**
The `orchestratorAliases` in useDocumentManager already bypass some of the state update issues for characters/rules/beats by reading directly from DB. But `createChapter` and `createScene` still use the stateful versions.

**Fix 3: Create a bulk import method that bypasses all state updates**
Add a method like `bulkImport` that writes everything to IndexedDB first, then does a single `refreshProjectState` at the end.

**Fix 4: Stabilize the `docManager` reference in App.jsx**
Use a ref to hold the adapter instead of useMemo with state dependencies.

The best fix is probably Fix 3 - create a dedicated bulk import path that:
1. Creates the project in DB
2. Creates all chapters in DB
3. Creates all scenes in DB
4. Updates project with chars/rules/beats/voice
5. Does ONE state refresh at the end

This avoids all the cascading state updates during import.

The user is asking about beats in their ProseLab application - specifically that the percentage (`pct`) field for each beat is not automatically updating/distributing when they have many beats. They want the beats to automatically calculate and distribute their percentage contributions.

Looking at the code in `useDocumentManager.js`, I can see the `saveBeat` function:

```javascript
const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
    const newBeats = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
      ? beats.map(b => b.id === beat.id ? beat : b)
      : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
    // Enforce order by pct
    newBeats.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0));
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
  }, [beats, updateProjectMetadata]);
```

The issue is clear: when beats are added or removed, the `pct` values are NOT automatically recalculated to distribute evenly across all beats. The code only sorts by existing `pct` values but never redistributes them.

For example, if you have 5 beats, each should ideally be at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% (or distributed evenly). But the current code doesn't do this - it just preserves whatever `pct` value was manually set.

The fix should:
1. When a beat is added, auto-distribute percentages evenly across all beats
2. When a beat is removed, redistribute the remaining beats' percentages
3. Optionally, allow manual override but still maintain proper distribution

Let me think about what "percentage which each beat is contributing" means in context of story beats. In story structure (like Save the Cat, Hero's Journey, etc.), beats occur at specific percentage points of the story. For example:
- Opening Image: 0%
- Theme Stated: 5%
- Setup: 10%
- Catalyst: 12%
- Debate: 12-25%
- Break into Two: 25%
- B Story: 30%
- Fun and Games: 30-55%
- Midpoint: 50%
- Bad Guys Close In: 55-75%
- All Is Lost: 75%
- Dark Night of the Soul: 75-80%
- Break into Three: 80%
- Finale: 80-99%
- Final Image: 100%

So the `pct` field represents where in the story (as a percentage) each beat occurs. If the user wants auto-distribution, they want beats to be evenly spaced when new ones are added.

The fix would be to add auto-distribution logic in `saveBeat` and `deleteBeat`:

```javascript
function distributeBeatsEvenly(beats) {
    if (beats.length === 0) return [];
    if (beats.length === 1) return [{ ...beats[0], pct: 0 }];
    return beats.map((beat, index) => ({
        ...beat,
        pct: Math.round((index / (beats.length - 1)) * 100)
    }));
}
```

But wait - maybe the user wants something different. Maybe they want the percentage to reflect how much of the total story each beat "contributes" (like weight), not position. In that case, each beat should have `pct = 100 / totalBeats`.

Let me re-read: "the percentage which each beat is contributing" - this sounds like contribution/weight, not position. So if there are 5 beats, each contributes 20%. If there are 10 beats, each contributes 10%.

But looking at the sort: `newBeats.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0))` - this sorts by pct ascending, which suggests pct is a position (0-100) in the story timeline, not a contribution weight.

Actually, I think the user might be referring to the beat's position in the story structure. When you add many beats, they should auto-distribute their positions evenly across the 0-100% range.

Let me provide a solution that auto-distributes beat percentages evenly when beats are added or removed, while also allowing the option to keep manually set values.

Here's my proposed fix:

```javascript
const redistributeBeats = useCallback((beatsList) => {
    if (beatsList.length === 0) return [];
    if (beatsList.length === 1) return [{ ...beatsList[0], pct: 50 }];
    return beatsList.map((beat, index) => ({
        ...beat,
        pct: Math.round((index / (beatsList.length - 1)) * 100)
    }));
}, []);

const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
    let newBeats = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
      ? beats.map(b => b.id === beat.id ? beat : b)
      : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
    
    // Auto-redistribute percentages evenly
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

const deleteBeat = useCallback(async (id) => {
    let newBeats = beats.filter(b => b.id !== id);
    // Auto-redistribute after deletion
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);
```

**Problem:** When beats are added or removed, the `pct` field is not automatically recalculated.

**Current `saveBeat`:**
```javascript
const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
    const newBeats = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
      ? beats.map(b => b.id === beat.id ? beat : b)
      : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
    newBeats.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0));
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });

```

**Current `deleteBeat`:**
```javascript

    const newBeats = beats.filter(b => b.id !== id);

**Fix - Replace both functions and add a helper:**

```javascript
// Add this helper function inside the hook, before saveBeat/deleteBeat:
const redistributeBeats = useCallback((beatsList) => {
    if (beatsList.length === 0) return [];
    if (beatsList.length === 1) return [{ ...beatsList[0], pct: 100 }];
    return beatsList.map((beat, index) => ({
        ...beat,
        pct: Math.round((index / (beatsList.length - 1)) * 100)
    }));
}, []);

// Replace saveBeat with:
const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
    let newBeats = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
      ? beats.map(b => b.id === beat.id ? beat : b)
      : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
    // Sort by current pct to preserve relative order before redistributing
    newBeats.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0));
    // Auto-redistribute percentages evenly across 0–100%
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// Replace deleteBeat with:
const deleteBeat = useCallback(async (id) => {
    let newBeats = beats.filter(b => b.id !== id);
    // Auto-redistribute remaining beats
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);
```

This ensures:
- 1 beat → 100%
- 2 beats → 0%, 100%
- 3 beats → 0%, 50%, 100%
- 4 beats → 0%, 33%, 67%, 100%
- 5 beats → 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%
- N beats → evenly distributed from 0 to 100

Every time a beat is added, edited, or deleted, all beats get their `pct` values recalculated automatically.

If the user instead wants "contribution weight" (each beat contributes equally, so N beats = 100/N% each), the formula would be:
```javascript
pct: Math.round(100 / beatsList.length)
```

But the position-based approach (0 to 100) makes more sense for story beat sheets where `pct` represents where in the narrative the beat occurs.

```javascript
// Helper: redistribute pct values evenly across 0-100%
const redistributeBeats = useCallback((beatsList) => {
    if (beatsList.length === 0) return [];
    if (beatsList.length === 1) return [{ ...beatsList[0], pct: 100 }];
    return beatsList.map((beat, index) => ({
        ...beat,
        pct: Math.round((index / (beatsList.length - 1)) * 100)
    }));
}, []);

// Move a beat up or down in the list
const moveBeat = useCallback(async (beatId, direction) => {
    const index = beats.findIndex(b => b.id === beatId);
    if (index === -1) return;
    const newIndex = direction === 'up' ? index - 1 : index + 1;
    if (newIndex < 0 || newIndex >= beats.length) return;
    
    const newBeats = [...beats];
    const [moved] = newBeats.splice(index, 1);
    newBeats.splice(newIndex, 0, moved);
    
    const redistributed = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: redistributed });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// Reorder beats (for drag-and-drop)
const reorderBeats = useCallback(async (reorderedBeats) => {
    const redistributed = redistributeBeats(reorderedBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: redistributed });
}, [updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// Updated saveBeat
const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
    let newBeats = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
      ? beats.map(b => b.id === beat.id ? beat : b)
      : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
    newBeats.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0));
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// Updated deleteBeat
const deleteBeat = useCallback(async (id) => {
    let newBeats = beats.filter(b => b.id !== id);
    newBeats = redistributeBeats(newBeats);
    await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: newBeats });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);
```

// Drag-and-drop state
const [draggedBeatIndex, setDraggedBeatIndex] = useState(null);

const handleDragStart = (index) => (e) => {
    setDraggedBeatIndex(index);
    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move';
    // For Firefox compatibility
    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', index.toString());
};

const handleDragOver = (index) => (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move';
    setDragOverIndex(index);
};

const handleDragLeave = () => {
    setDragOverIndex(null);
};

const handleDrop = (dropIndex) => (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (draggedBeatIndex === null || draggedBeatIndex === dropIndex) {
        setDraggedBeatIndex(null);
        setDragOverIndex(null);
        return;

    const reordered = [...beats];
    const [moved] = reordered.splice(draggedBeatIndex, 1);
    reordered.splice(dropIndex, 0, moved);
    reorderBeats(reordered);
    setDraggedBeatIndex(null);
    setDragOverIndex(null);
};

const handleDragEnd = () => {
    setDraggedBeatIndex(null);
    setDragOverIndex(null);
};
```

### FILE 1: `useDocumentManager.js` — Add/update these functions inside the hook

```javascript
// ─── HELPER: evenly redistribute pct across ordered beats ───
const redistributeBeats = useCallback((beatsList) => {
  if (beatsList.length === 0) return [];
  if (beatsList.length === 1) return [{ ...beatsList[0], pct: 100 }];
  return beatsList.map((beat, i) => ({
    ...beat,
    pct: Math.round((i / (beatsList.length - 1)) * 100)
  }));
}, []);

// ─── MOVE beat up or down by one position ───
const moveBeat = useCallback(async (beatId, direction) => {
  const idx = beats.findIndex(b => b.id === beatId);
  if (idx === -1) return;
  const target = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1;
  if (target < 0 || target >= beats.length) return;
  const next = [...beats];
  [next[idx], next[target]] = [next[target], next[idx]];
  await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: redistributeBeats(next) });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// ─── REORDER beats (for drag-and-drop) ───
const reorderBeats = useCallback(async (reorderedBeats) => {
  await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: redistributeBeats(reorderedBeats) });
}, [updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// ─── UPDATED saveBeat ───
const saveBeat = useCallback(async (beat) => {
  let next = beats.find(b => b.id === beat.id)
    ? beats.map(b => (b.id === beat.id ? beat : b))
    : [...beats, { ...beat, id: beat.id || Date.now() }];
  next.sort((a, b) => (parseInt(a.pct) || 0) - (parseInt(b.pct) || 0));
  next = redistributeBeats(next);
  await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: next });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);

// ─── UPDATED deleteBeat ───
const deleteBeat = useCallback(async (id) => {
  const next = beats.filter(b => b.id !== id);
  await updateProjectMetadata({ beats: redistributeBeats(next) });
}, [beats, updateProjectMetadata, redistributeBeats]);
```

### FILE 2: Beat list UI component with drag-and-drop + up/down buttons

I'll create a `ReorderableBeatList` component that can be dropped into wherever beats are currently rendered.

```jsx
import React, { useState, useRef } from 'react';

export default function ReorderableBeatList({
  beats,
  onEdit,
  onDelete,
  onMove,
  onReorder
}) {
  const [dragIdx, setDragIdx] = useState(null);
  const [overIdx, setOverIdx] = useState(null);
  const dragNode = useRef(null);

  const handleDragStart = (e, index) => {
    setDragIdx(index);
    dragNode.current = e.target;
    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move';
    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', ''); // Firefox fix
    // Delay so the dragged element doesn't immediately get the dragging style
    setTimeout(() => {
      if (dragNode.current) dragNode.current.style.opacity = '0.4';
    }, 0);
  };

  const handleDragOver = (e, index) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move';
    if (overIdx !== index) setOverIdx(index);
  };

  const handleDrop = (e, dropIndex) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (dragIdx === null || dragIdx === dropIndex) {
      reset();
      return;
    }
    const reordered = [...beats];
    const [moved] = reordered.splice(dragIdx, 1);
    reordered.splice(dropIndex, 0, moved);
    onReorder(reordered);
    reset();
  };

  const reset = () => {
    if (dragNode.current) dragNode.current.style.opacity = '1';
    setDragIdx(null);
    setOverIdx(null);
    dragNode.current = null;
  };

  return (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', gap: 4 }}>
      {beats.map((beat, i) => (
        <div
          key={beat.id}
          draggable
          onDragStart={e => handleDragStart(e, i)}
          onDragOver={e => handleDragOver(e, i)}
          onDragLeave={() => setOverIdx(null)}
          onDrop={e => handle

const redistributeBeats = useCallback((beatsList) => {

  if (beatsList.length === 1) return [{ ...beatsList[0], pct: 100 }];

  }));
}, []);

    ? beats.map(b => (b.id === beat.id ? beat : b))

### ReorderableBeatList.jsx — Complete new component:

```jsx
import React, { useState, useRef } from 'react';

export default function ReorderableBeatList({
  beats = [],
  onEdit,
  onDelete,
  onMove,
  onReorder,
}) {
  const [dragIdx, setDragIdx] = useState(null);
  const [overIdx, setOverIdx] = useState(null);
  const dragNodeRef = useRef(null);

  /* ── drag handlers ── */
  const onDragStart = (e, index) => {
    dragNodeRef.current = e.currentTarget;
    setDragIdx(index);
    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move';
    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', '');
    requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      if (dragNodeRef.current) dragNodeRef.current.classList.add('beat-dragging');
    });
  };

  const onDragOver = (e, index) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move';
    if (overIdx !== index) setOverIdx(index);
  };

  const onDrop = (e, dropIndex) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (dragIdx !== null && dragIdx !== dropIndex) {
      const reordered = [...beats];
      const [moved] = reordered.splice(dragIdx, 1);
      reordered.splice(dropIndex, 0, moved);
      onReorder(reordered);
    }
    cleanup();
  };

  const cleanup = () => {
    if (dragNodeRef.current) dragNodeRef.current.classList.remove('beat-dragging');
    dragNodeRef.current = null;
    setDragIdx(null);
    setOverIdx(null);
  };

  if (beats.length === 0) {
    return <p style={{ color: '#666', fontStyle: 'italic', padding: '12px' }}>No beats yet.</p>;
  }

  return (
    <>
      <style>{`
        .beat-row {
          display: flex;
          align-items: center;
          gap: 8px;
          padding: 8px 12px;
          margin-bottom: 2px;
          border-radius: 6px;
          border: 1px solid #333;
          background: #1e1e1e;
          cursor: grab;
          transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, opacity 0.15s, transform 0.15s;
          user-select: none;
        }
        .beat-row:hover { border-color: #555; background: #252525; }
        .beat-row.beat-dragging { opacity: 0.35; transform: scale(0.97); }

// Configuration
const CONFIG = {
  API_ENDPOINT: 'https://your-app-domain.com/api/documents/email-upload',
  API_KEY: 'your-secret-api-key',
  PROCESSED_LABEL: 'Processed-DocUpload',

function checkForDocumentEmails() {
  const label = getOrCreateLabel(CONFIG.PROCESSED_LABEL);

    const messages = thread.getMessages();

      if (attachments.length === 0) return;

        subject: message.getSubject(),
        from: message.getFrom(),
        date: message.getDate().toISOString(),
        bodySnippet: message.getPlainBody().substring(0, 500),
      };
      
      attachments.forEach(attachment => {
        uploadAttachment(attachment, emailMeta);
      });
    });
    thread.addLabel(label);
    thread.markRead();
  });
}

function uploadAttachment(attachment, emailMeta) {
  const blob = attachment.copyBlob();

    fileName: attachment.getName(),
    contentType: attachment.getContentType(),
    size: attachment.getSize(),
    emailSubject: emailMeta.subject,
    emailFrom: emailMeta.from,
    emailDate: emailMeta.date,
    emailBody: emailMeta.bodySnippet,
    fileData: Utilities.base64Encode(blob.getBytes()),
  };
  
  const options = {
    method: 'post',
    contentType: 'application/json',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + CONFIG.API_KEY,
    },
    payload: JSON.stringify(payload),
    muteHttpExceptions: true,
  };

    const response = UrlFetchApp.fetch(CONFIG.API_ENDPOINT, options);
    Logger.log('Upload response: ' + response.getContentText());
  } catch (e) {
    Logger.log('Upload failed: ' + e.message);
  }
}
